en.title: PSG wins Champions League, Paris streets turn violent
en.body: Paris Saint-Germain won the Champions League final against Arsenal on Saturday night. Hundreds of people were arrested after fans took to the streets to celebrate. Police say 416 people were detained across France, including 283 in Paris. Seven police officers were hurt. Shops were damaged and cars were set on fire.
en.body_more:
The French government sent 22,000 police officers across the country before the match. In Paris alone, 8,000 officers were ready. But the celebrations became chaotic. Nearly 20,000 supporters gathered on the Champs-Élysées near the Arc de Triomphe. Fans set off flares, lit fireworks, and blared car horns. Some groups broke shop windows and set cars on fire. In the 8th Arrondissement, supporters tried to storm a police station. Police stopped them.

Interior Minister Laurent Nunez said the trouble had been expected because of the big match and the feelings of the fans. He called the violence "absolutely unacceptable". Near PSG's Parc des Princes stadium in the 16th Arrondissement, police contained about 1,000 people. A bakery and a restaurant were damaged. Violence also broke out near the stadium where fans were watching the match on big screens.

This was not the first time. In May 2025, PSG's first Champions League win led to the same kind of trouble. That time, 201 people were hurt and more than 500 were arrested. Two people died, including a 17-year-old boy. Political leader Marine Le Pen said it was wrong that a football win could cause riots and make people fear for their safety.

PSG planned to hold an official celebration on Sunday at the Champ de Mars near the Eiffel Tower. About 85,000 to 90,000 people were expected to come. French President Emmanuel Macron was to meet the players at the Élysée Palace. Later that evening at Parc des Princes, fans and players were to gather from 7.30pm local time to mark a second straight European title.

ny.title: PSG yapambana Champions League, misewu ya Paris yakhala yachiwawa
ny.body: Paris Saint-Germain idapambana mpikisano wa Champions League motsutsana ndi Arsenal Loweruka usiku. Anthu mazana ambiri adamangidwa pambuyo poti mafani adapita m'misewu kukasangalala. Apolisi ati anthu 416 adamangidwa ku France konse, kuphatikiza 283 ku Paris. Apolisi asanu ndi awiri adavulala. Masitolo adawonongeka ndipo magalimoto adawotchedwa.
ny.body_more:
Boma la France linatumiza apolisi 22,000 m'dziko lonselo masewerawa asanachitike. Ku Paris kokha, apolisi 8,000 anali okonzeka. Koma chikondwererocho chinasanduka chisokonezo. Otsatira pafupifupi 20,000 anasonkhana pa Champs-Élysées pafupi ndi Arc de Triomphe. Otsatira anayatsa moto, anayatsa zozimitsa moto, ndipo anafuwula malipenga a magalimoto. Magulu ena anaswa mawindo a masitolo ndi kuyatsa magalimoto. Mu Arrondissement ya 8, otsatira anayesa kulowa m'malo mwa apolisi. Apolisi anawaletsa.

Nduna ya Zamkati Laurent Nunez anati vutoli linkayembekezeredwa chifukwa cha masewerawa akuluakulu komanso momwe mafaniwo amamvera. Iye anati chiwawacho "sichovomerezeka konse". Pafupi ndi bwalo lamasewera la Parc des Princes la PSG ku Arrondissement ya 16, apolisi anali ndi anthu pafupifupi 1,000. Buledi ndi lesitilanti zinawonongeka. Chiwawa chinayambanso pafupi ndi bwalo lamasewera pomwe mafani anali kuonera masewerawa pa zikwangwani zazikulu.

Iyi sinali nthawi yoyamba. Mu Meyi 2025, kupambana koyamba kwa PSG mu Champions League kunabweretsa mavuto omwewo. Nthawi imeneyo, anthu 201 anavulala ndipo oposa 500 anamangidwa. Anthu awiri anamwalira, kuphatikizapo mnyamata wazaka 17. Mtsogoleri wa ndale Marine Le Pen anati n’kulakwa kuti kupambana mpira kungayambitse zipolowe ndikupangitsa anthu kuopa chitetezo chawo.

PSG inakonza zoti ichite chikondwerero chovomerezeka Lamlungu ku Champ de Mars pafupi ndi Eiffel Tower. Anthu pafupifupi 85,000 mpaka 90,000 ankayembekezeredwa kubwera. Purezidenti wa ku France Emmanuel Macron anali kukakumana ndi osewerawo ku Élysée Palace. Madzulo amenewo ku Parc des Princes, mafani ndi osewera anali kusonkhana kuyambira 7:30pm nthawi yakomweko kuti akumbukire chikho chachiwiri motsatizana cha ku Europe.

en.title: Tobacco Farmers Blamed for Poor Handling and Low Prices
en.body: The Tobacco Commission says many farmers are damaging Malawi's tobacco by mixing grades, adding water to bales, and not caring for quality. The commission warned on Wednesday in Lilongwe that this breaks the law and makes buyers reject tobacco. When tobacco is rejected, farmers get less money. An expert said farmers must stop complaining and focus on quality instead.
en.body_more:
The Tobacco Commission Public Relations Officer Telephorus Chigwenembe said farmers are mixing tobacco grades and tampering with moisture levels in their bales. This violates the Tobacco Industry Act of 2019. He said that if farmers want good prices, they must improve their grading and curing methods, or Malawi will lose business on the world market.

Governance expert Dr. Ben Dzolowere agreed strongly. He said some farmers hide mold in tobacco and mix poor leaf with good leaf to trick buyers. He said farmers control the grade they bring to market, even if they cannot control the world price. When tobacco is clean and well cured, buyers compete for it and pay more money. When tobacco is wet, mixed, or dirty, buyers pay less. "Factories do not compete for trash — they compete for quality," Dzolowere said.

One observation at the Lilongwe Auction Floor showed the problem clearly. On May 19, 2026, only 142 bales of tobacco from independent farmers were for sale. Of these, 126 were rejected — a rate of 88.7 percent. Most were rejected because moisture inside the bales was too high. Temperature tests showed these bales had mold. Contracted farmers, who have company support, had far fewer rejections. Many independent farmers lack proper barns and storage, so their tobacco gets wet and fails the test.

The Tobacco Commission is now running campaigns to teach farmers how to dry, grade, and pack tobacco correctly. Officials say better quality is the only way to get better prices and bring confidence back to Malawi's tobacco business.

ny.title: Abwino wa Tobacco Akambirana Anzawo Kuti Sankhire Mbadwo Mwabwino
ny.body: Tobacco Commission inati amalimi amachita zabwino pang'ono pa tobacco yao. Amakunyanya kugwedeza mitengo, kuwonjezera madzi, ndi kusokoneza kalasi. Komiti inayimbirira ndi kuwunika kuti izi sizikamveka ndi mberemberi. Mwaliro umakhalanso wochita bwino pa ts'ambuzo la phindu.
ny.body_more:
Telephorus Chigwenembe wokambirana ndi media kwa Tobacco Commission anati amalimi amina acheza ndi mitengo ya mbadwo. Amakuwengera madzi m'mabale a mbadwo ndi kusiya kusungitsa mavuto. Izi zimavulala mfundo ya zodzera mbiriyakudzi. Chigwenembe anati mberemberi amafuna phindu labwino, ayenera kusankha mbadwo yabwino ndi kumvetsa momwe mbadwo imasungitsidwa. Ngati izi sizizichitikapo, Malawi idzasiya malo ake pa mtengo wapadziko lapansi.

Mwananyanja Dr. Ben Dzolowere anagwirizanira ndi izi. Anati amalimi amina akuoneka kuti amavumbutsitsa mbadwo yabwino ndi yoyipa. Amakumangu mitengo, m'malo moti akhale ogwira ntchito mabwino. Anati: amalimi amakontola kalasi ya mbadwo yotulutsidwa. Ngati mbadwo ndi yabwino ndi yasungitsidwa mwabwino, mberemberi amakita kutengana ndi kupatula ndalama zambiri. Koma ngati mbadwo ili ndiyoweta, yosasunthidwa, kapena yoyipa, mberemberi amapeza mwayi omupatula ndalama zochepa. "Amuna angiri samayezekanso ndi zinthu zoyipa," anati Dzolowere.

Kupeza kwa anthu pa maliti a Lilongwe pa May 19, 2026 kunawonetsa vuto lalinso. Mabale 142 a amalimi osapakana ndi kampani anayamba kusungidwa. Kwa izo, 126 zinakana. Kuti zinakana, 88.7 pa mwezi. Zingakhale mafuta m'mkati mwa mabale, motero amalawa akana zipunzire. Amalimi opakana ndi kampani anapeza mavuto ochepa kwambiri, chifukwa kampani zimakawala kale.

Tobacco Commission ikutsatsa njira zophunzitsa amalimi momwe ayenera kusunga mbadwo. Aliyense akuti phindu labwino ndi njira yokweza kalasi ndi kusunga mbadwo mwabwino.

en.title: Bullets part with coach Chirwa, name Munthali interim leader
en.body: FC Nyasa Big Bullets have ended Gilbert Chirwa's time as head coach. The club and Chirwa agreed to part ways on Friday. Heston Munthali, a club legend, will lead the team on an interim basis. Munthali was Chirwa's assistant. He will lead Bullets in Sunday's match against Moyale Barracks. The club will search for a new head coach after the international window.
en.body_more:
Chirwa took charge in December on a temporary basis after Peter Mponda left. He stayed on after winning the Castel Challenge Cup and finishing second in last season's league. This season, however, Bullets have not done well. After five matches, they sit sixth with eight or nine points. They have two wins and three draws. The club said it thanks Chirwa for his work and wishes him well in the future.

Munthali is a well-known figure at Bullets. He was a striker for the club and has worked in coaching roles before. Club officials said Munthali will pick the team and make decisions during matches. He will have help from the other coaches. The club believes he will keep the team ready while they find a new coach. The club said the new coach will come from abroad.

Bullets have had a mixed start to the 2026 season. They have not lost a match in five games, but they have drawn too many times. This has cost them points. They are now only five points above the teams that could go down from the league. A football expert said Chirwa did not do a bad job because he took over quickly and the team is still unbeaten. The club is looking for a coach with at least a CAF A Licence and proven experience. The new head coach will be named in the second week of June.

ny.title: Bullets akusiya Chirwa, akonza Munthali ngati mloza kwanthawi
ny.body: FC Nyasa Big Bullets asiya Gilbert Chirwa ngati mlengi wa timu. Mvuto ndi Chirwa ankagwirizana kuti atule pa Lachisanu. Heston Munthali, munthu wogwira mtengo wa Bullets, adzakhala mlengi mwanthawi. Munthali anali osmala Chirwa. Adzasingiza Bullets pa Mbalakhosi tsopano pakatipita pa ndege ku Moyale Barracks. Mvuto idzafunafuna mlengi mwachakufa pambuyo pa nthawi ya masewera a makunthu.
ny.body_more:
Chirwa analowa kugwira ntchitoyi mu Disembala mwanthawi yochepa. Mponda anasiya kale. Chirwa anakala pambuyo pothotsa Castel Challenge Cup ndi kukhala wachiwiri mu masewera akale. Koma ntachasika masewera akuno. Pambuyo pa masewera asanu, Bullets ali m'malo achisanu ndi chimodzi ndi mfundo zisanu ndi zinai kapena zisanu ndi zisanu. Anagwina awiri ndi kuonjezera kutatu. Mvuto inati zikomo kwa Chirwa kwa kugwira ntchito yake bwino.

Munthali ndi munthu wodziwika kwa Bullets. Anali mlayi wa mbalane kwa Bullets kale. Anagwira ntchito ya maphunziro a magwira ntchito nthawi zina. Aling'ono a mvuto anati Munthali adzasankha amayi oti asinze ndi kutenga zisankho mwa masewera. Adzakumbatirana ndi osmala ena. Bullets ikhulupilira kuti Munthali adzakhala ndithu kumene kokumbatira timu kunyumba, mpaka mlengi yotsimikizidwa abwere.

Masewera akuno masikuti sitabwino. Pambuyo pa masewera asanu, Bullets sanagule nambala imodzi. Koma amakoroshi ambiri ndizina. Izi zapangitsa kuti alime mfundo zingapo. Anthu angapo akuwona ziphuphu zotero. Mkulamanja mmodzi anati Chirwa sanachita chinthu choipa kwambiri chifukwa analowa kufulumira ndipo timu siifale. Mvuto ikufuna mlengi amene ali ndi CAF A Licence ndi kalyasi kwenikweni. Mlengi watsimikizidwa adzatchulidwa mu lingaliro lachisanu la June.

en.title: President bypasses vice presidents, uses ministers instead
en.body: President Peter Mutharika is giving key State duties to Cabinet ministers instead of his two vice presidents. First Vice President Jane Ansah and Second Vice President Enoch Chihana have been absent from major national events since the start of the year. The President's office says this saves money, because ministers cost less to pay than vice presidents. Ansah also lost two of her job titles in recent weeks.
en.body_more:
In January, Minister of Finance Joseph Mwanamvekha went to John Chilembwe Day on behalf of the President, even though the Second Vice President was in the country. In March, Minister of Transport Jappie Mhango spoke at Martyr's Day in Nkhata Bay instead of a vice president. Minister of Sports Alfred Gangata went to Kamuzu Day in May. The President did not send a vice president. When the President flew to South Africa in March, neither vice president was there to say goodbye.

The President's office says this is allowed by the Constitution. A person named Focus Maganga, who speaks for the President, said the President can choose to give jobs to whoever he wants. He said this does not mean there is bad feeling between the President and Ansah. He said the jobs are still being done, just in a different way.

But experts and human rights leaders are worried. They say the vice presidents look weak now. Lawyer Benedicto Kondowe said taking away jobs without giving new ones is wrong. He said it makes people think the Constitution does not matter. A human rights leader named Robert Mkwezalamba said people who pay taxes want to see vice presidents doing real work, not sitting at home. Another leader, Michael Kaiyatsa, said the country should ask if the vice president offices are being made useless on purpose.

ny.title: Purezidenti akugwiritsa ntchito amaninisitala m'malo mwa mavesi
ny.body: Purezidenti Peter Mutharika akupatsa ntchito zakazikazi kwa amaninisitala m'malo mwa mavesi ake awiri. Vice President Jane Ansah ndi Vice President Enoch Chihana sanachoka m'malo pa zochitika zazikuluzikulu kuchokera kumayambiriro mwa chaka. Ofesi ya Purezidenti akuti iji ndi njira yosunga ndalama, chifukwa amaninisitala amakostera pangano pang'ono. Ansah anabowunika ntchito zina za bwino.
ny.body_more:
Mu Januari, Minisitala wa Finance Joseph Mwanamvekha anakopera Purezidenti pa chipani cha John Chilembwe, ngakhale Vice President wachiwiri anali m'dziko. Mu Marichi, Minisitala wa Transport Jappie Mhango anazungulira pa Martyr's Day ku Nkhata Bay m'malo mwa mavesi. Minisitala wa Sports Alfred Gangata anakopera pa Kamuzu Day mu Meyi. Purezidenti sanaperekeze mavesi.

Pa Marichi, Purezidenti anatengeza ulendo ku South Africa koma mavesi asanu ndi sapunaphunzira. Ofesi ya Purezidenti ikuti izi ndi zolegalizidwa ndi Constitushoni. Woyendetsa ma uthenga, Focus Maganga, akuti Purezidenti ali ndi ufulu wopereka ntchito kwa aliyense yemwe anafuna. Akuti ichi sichinachitike chifukwa pali vuto pakati pa Purezidenti ndi Ansah. Akuti ntchito yili yikupitira, koma ndi njira ina.

Koma akatswiri ndi atsogoleri a zingwe za anthu akudandaula. Akuti mavesi akuwoneka osalimba. Mlobi Benedicto Kondowe akuti kuchotsa ntchito popanda kupatsa ina ndi zosowa bwino. Akuti izi zimachita kuti anthu asakhulupirire Constitushoni. Atsogoleri a zingwe za anthu Robert Mkwezalamba akuti anthu amene amabweza ndalama akufuna kuona mavesi akuchita ntchito yeniyeni, osakhala pansi. Wina wina, Michael Kaiyatsa, akuti dziko lonse liyenera kufunsa ngati ofesi ya mavesi ikuchotsedwa bwino bwino.

en.title: Malawi can earn big money from Europe's green change
en.body: Malawi has minerals that Europe needs for green energy. Europe is looking for graphite, uranium, and rare earth minerals. These are used to make electric cars and solar power systems. The World Bank says Malawi could earn $30 billion from mining by 2040. But right now, mining gives less than 1% of Malawi's money. Experts say Malawi must use this chance well.
en.body_more:
Paul Mvula is a mining expert. He says Malawi must not just dig and sell raw minerals. Instead, the country should build factories to process the minerals here. Mvula says Malawi needs better roads, power, and skills training for workers. This will help Malawi earn more money from its minerals.

Marvin Banda is an economist. He says Europe's need for green minerals makes Africa important again. But he warns that Malawi must make strong deals. The deals must protect Malawi's people and land, not just help big foreign companies. Banda says Malawi needs smart contracts that are fair.

A German group called PowerShift also spoke about this. Michael Reckordt works there. He said Europe has only 6% of the world's people, but uses 25 to 30% of all metals. This is unfair, he said. Reckordt asked Europe to use less. He also asked for fair deals that help miners and their families. He said mining must not harm the land or hurt workers' rights.

Sixteen journalists from Africa came to a workshop in Malawi to talk about these ideas. They discussed how Africa's minerals could change the world. Experts say Malawi must move fast to get ready. The country must build trust with fair and open deals. If Malawi does this well, mining could change the whole economy.

ny.title: Malawi ingapereka ndalama zambiri pa mage a ulalo wa Europe
ny.body: Europe ifuna minelazi ya graphite, uranium, ndi minelazi ina; Malawi ili ndi izi. Minelazi iyi imagwiritsidwa ntchito pampira ya magetsi a sun ndi magalimoto a chipakucha. Mayambitso a dziko lansalu akuti Malawi ingakuponye ndalama $30 billion pakuminya mpaka 2040. Koma lero, kuminya kumapereka ndichiloko cha 1% ya ndalama ya Malawi. Akatswiri akuti Malawi akuyenera kugwira zinthuzi moyenera.
ny.body_more:
Paul Mvula ndi wodziwikira zakunyumba. Akuti Malawi sakuyenera kungolinji ndi kupinika minelazi. M'malo mwake, Malawi akuyenera kumanga nyumba zopanga zinthu. Mvula akuti Malawi akufuna njira zabwino, magetsi, ndi maphunziro amtumiki. Apa apa, ndalama zikutengeka zambiri.

Marvin Banda ndi mphunzitsi wa ndalama. Akuti kufuna kwa Europe kwa minelazi kumapangitsa Africa kukhala yofunika. Koma akachenjeza kuti Malawi ayenera kupanga pangano zolimba. Pangano likuyenera kusunga anthu a Malawi ndi nthaka, osati kungokhala bwino kwa kampani zakunja. Banda akuti Malawi akufuna pangano zomveka bwino.

Gulu limodzi la ku Germany linatchula zinthu izi. Dzinali PowerShift. Michael Reckordt amagwira ntchito pa gulu ilo. Anati Europe ili ndi anthu 6% pa dziko lonse, koma amagwiritsa ntchito metala 25-30% pa dziko lonse. Izi si zomveka, anakuti. Reckordt anapempha Europe kuti igwiritse ntchito pang'ono. Anapemphani kuti pangano likhalire lochepa ndi lochepa kwa omina. Anati kuminya kusayenera kumangira nthaka kapena kugonjetsa anthu.

Jornalista 16 aja kuchokera ku Africa anatumutsa mgogomani mu Malawi kuti azungulire mwachigamu. Anangogonekeza momwe minelazi ya Africa ingasinthire chilengedwe. Akatswiri akuti Malawi ayenera kukhala wachangu. Malawi akufuna kupanga chipangano choyenera ndi chowonekera. Ngati Malawi ichita izi moyenera, kuminya kinga sinthire gazapose lonse.

en.title: Young man builds business from plastic waste after foundation help
en.body: A young entrepreneur in Lilongwe is turning plastic waste into useful products and making good money. Alinafe Velemu got K1 million and help from the Paul Katema Foundation's Young Builders Challenge. He came third in the competition. Now his business, Green Innovations Center, makes tables, baskets, and knife handles from old plastic. The work is growing fast.
en.body_more:
Alinafe is a student at Phwezi Technical College. He learns general fitting skills at Level 2. He started his business in 2024 at Nalikule, near Lilongwe. The foundation gave him money and a teacher to help him grow his business. This is what the Young Builders Challenge does—it gives young people seed money, training, and advice.

At his workshop, Alinafe and his team take plastic that people throw away. They clean it and cut it and shape it into new things. Tables and baskets sell well. Knife handles do too. More people want to buy these things now. His workshop makes more products every week. Alinafe says the money and teaching from the foundation have changed everything. He says his work helps in two ways: it stops plastic from going to the dump, and it gives him and others jobs.

Ashley Chingoma works for the Paul Katema Foundation as a business teacher. He says the Young Builders Challenge does more than give money. The programme also teaches discipline and how to run a business the right way. This helps young people build businesses that last. The foundation sees many young people in the programme growing their businesses and making more money.

Alinafe says his work is not just about making money for himself. He says it helps young people find jobs when work is hard to find. It also helps the earth by using old things again instead of throwing them away.

ny.title: Mwana wina akhala ndi ntchito yake yabwino kupyolera mu chiphazi
ny.body: Alinafe Velemu ali mwana wina wa ku Lilongwe womwe wapangira malonda a zinthu zatsopano kuchokera pa chiphazi chinalidwe. Paul Katema Foundation inamupatsa ndalama K1 million ndi mphunzitso mu Young Builders Challenge. Alinafe anachoka poyamba pa malo achitatu. Malonda ake, Green Innovations Center, wapangira meza, mafupa akadali, ndi malhenga a mapanga kuchokera pa chiphazi chinalidwe. Ntchitoyi yikula kwambiri.
ny.body_more:
Alinafe ndi mwana waphunziro pa Phwezi Technical College. Wina aphunzira ku Level 2 zoyendera makina. Anayamba ntchito yake mu 2024 ku Nalikule, pafupi ndi Lilongwe. Paul Katema Foundation inamupatsa ndalama komanso wina woyendera kuti amenyanjanire kumapanga mwambo wake bwino. Chinu chimachitika—afoundation imapatsa ainu ang'onoang'ono ndalama, maphunzitso, ndi malangizo.

Ku ntchito yake, Alinafe ndi anzake amachotsera chiphazi chomwe anthu amachulapo. Amachita chiyambi, amassanya ndi kuumba zinthu zatsopano. Meza ndi mafupa akadali amagulika bwino. Malhenga a mapanga nawo. Anthu ambiri akufuna kugula zinthu izi pano. Ntchito yake imapanga zambiri nthawi zonse. Alinafe akuti ndalama ndi maphunzitso achokera ku afoundation asintha kalekale. Akuti ntchito yake imachita zinthu ziwiri: imalepekeza chiphazi kuti sichipike, ndipo imapatsa anthu ntchito.

Ashley Chingoma ndi woyendera mabizinisi pa Paul Katema Foundation. Akuti Young Builders Challenge sichimapatsitsa ndalama okhaokha. Pulogalamu imaphunzitsanso umphawi ndi mwambo woyendera mabizinisi bwino. Izi zimakhalanso ndi thandizo kuti ainu ang'onoang'ono apange mabizinisi akhadutsa nthawi yayitali. Afoundation imawona ainu ambiri mu pulogalamu imene ikalukaluka ntchito zawo ndi kupeza ndalama zambiri.

Alinafe akuti ntchito yake sichina chuma chake chokhakha. Akuti imachita kuti ainu ang'onoang'ono apeze ntchito pa nthawi imene kuzipeza kuli kovuta. Imachotsera ndinamwali kwa ntchito ya chiphazi poumba zinthu zatsopano m'malo mochulupiliza zogulitsidwa ndi atsopano.

en.title: Church leader gives hope to man sick for 29 years
en.body: Apostle Clifford Kawinga visited Yotamu Nkokoko in Dedza District on Friday. Nkokoko is 55 years old and has been in bed for 29 years. He cannot move. Kawinga brought gifts to help the family. He gave a sewing machine, food, blankets, and a solar light. Nkokoko's mother said the gifts give the family hope.
en.body_more:
Nkokoko became ill in 1997 when he was in Form Three at William Murray Secondary School. The illness left him unable to move. For nearly 30 years, his mother Emily Chaima has cared for him at their home in Thundudzi Village. The family had no money and no light. Life was very hard.

Kawinga leads Salvation For All Ministries International. Through his Hope Field Initiative, he gives help to poor and sick people. In 2021, his group built a new house for Nkokoko's family. On Friday's visit, Kawinga brought more gifts. He gave K2 million in money, a radio, rice, soya, soap, and oil. The solar light will give the home power for the first time. The sewing machine will help the family make money.

Kawinga said every person has value and needs love. He said the church must care for people who suffer and feel alone. He told the community that helping others shows what real faith means. Chaima said the gifts bring big change. She said her son suffered alone for so long. Now she feels hope again.

Kawinga said this visit is not the end. He plans to help more poor families across Malawi. He said acts of care can give back hope to people who have lost it.

ny.title: Mkulu wa chipungwe amapatsa linso lachelidwe kwa munthu akuvutika 29 zaka
ny.body: Apostole Clifford Kawinga anagwirizana ndi Yotamu Nkokoko ku Dedza pa Lachisanu. Nkokoko ali ndi zaka 55 ndipo akuvutika pa phungu kwa zaka 29. Kawinga anatulutsa zinthu zambiri: injini yosesa, chakudya, blanketsi, ndi nyumba ya mwala. Amayi a Nkokoko anadziwa kuti zizindikiro za ufulu zija zimachoka m'Mulungu.
ny.body_more:
Nkokoko anayambika nthenda mu 1997 pomwe ankasukulu ku William Murray Secondary School. Nthenda iyo inayiwala kuti akudzengera. Kwa zaka palipose 30, amayi ake Emily Chaima akamuyang'anira m'nyumba. Banja linalonjeza ndalama ndiponso kuwala. Moyo unali wovuta kwambiri.

Kawinga ndiye mlongololo wa Salvation For All Ministries International. Ndi bungwe lake la Hope Field Initiative limene limapeza anthu akugula ndipo akuvutika. Mu 2021, gulu lake linabumba nyumba yatsopano kwa banja la Nkokoko. Pa chi Lachisanu, Kawinga anatulutsa zinthu zina. Anatulutsa ndalama K2 miliyoni, seera, mpunga, soya, sapu, ndi mafuta. Nyumba ya mwala iyo idzapeza magetsi koyambilira. Injini yosesa idzachita kuti banja lizipe ndalama.

Kawinga anati munthu aliyonse ali ndi mtengo ndipo akufuna chikondi. Anati mpingo iyenera kuchirikiza anthu omwe akuvutika. Anati kupereka thandizo ndi mawu okuwunika zachikhristiano. Chaima anati zinthu zija zimachinja moyo wawo. Anati mwana wake anakuvutika mokha kwa nthawi yayitali. Tsopano ali ndi ufulu.

Kawinga anati ntchito yino si mphela. Anati azapeza mabanja achilumbamtumbu a Malawi onse. Anati kupera chilungamthu kumwetsa ufulu kwa anthu omwe akusweka.

en.title: Patients Pay Bribes at Public Hospitals, Officials Admit
en.body: Malawi's public hospitals have a serious corruption problem. Patients are being forced to pay money for free treatment. Some pay bribes to jump queues. Others pay for drugs that should be free. The government's Health Secretary, Dan Namarika, told parliament this is true. He said the government has started to take action against the workers who take bribes.
en.body_more:
At Kamuzu Central Hospital, one patient paid 90,000 kwacha to use the Endoscopy Department. Another patient paid 18,500 kwacha before a tooth was pulled out. Hospital staff steal drugs and sell them in private shops. Two pharmacists and one hospital worker were found to have stolen medicine.

At Queen Elizabeth Central Hospital, patients pay money to skip the queue and get help faster. Some patients fear to tell anyone about the bribes. They worry the doctors will not help them next time. The hospital also said that theatre clothes, gauze, and cotton rolls were stolen.

At Mzuzu Central Hospital, staff ask for money before they help patients. Some workers give fake receipts. Sick people are afraid to report these bad workers because they fear being punished. A news team made a film called "Pay Up or Die." They found this problem at many hospitals. The film was made by seven news groups in Malawi. After the film, President Peter Mutharika told the Health Ministry and the Anti-Corruption Bureau to investigate and punish the workers who took bribes.

Parliament's Health Committee chief, Anthony Masamba, said this is very bad. He said the government must act fast. The hospitals have started to fight corruption. They have made complaint boxes, phone lines to report crimes, cameras, and teams to check honesty. They also work with the Anti-Corruption Bureau to stop this.

ny.title: Maalamulo Amatchula Zabwino m'Zachipatala Za Pulezidenti
ny.body: Zachipatala za pulezidenti za Malawi zili ndi vuto lalikulu. Anthu akudandaula kuti amaziwalitsa ndalama kuti apeze chithandizo. Anzogulitsa amapempha pesa kuti anthu aziwala m'ndandanda. Ena amaziwalitsa kuti azipeza mankhwala omwe ayenera kukhala ndi mphepete. Secretary wa Kachipatala, Dan Namarika, anati izi ndi zoona. Anati boma liri ku lemba anzogulitsa omwe amatenga pesa.
ny.body_more:
M'Kachipatala ka Kamuzu, munthu mmodzi analipira 90,000 kwacha kuti agwire ntchito ya Endoscopy. Wina analipira 18,500 kwacha asanalipire mazinyo. Ogulitsa amamba mankhwala ndipo amawauzitsa m'mashopu a anthu. Gulu lili la ogulitsa anzanu ndi wodzimiritsa wa kachipatala analotula mankhwala.

M'Kachipatala ka Queen Elizabeth, anthu amapempha pesa kuti aziwala m'ndandanda. Ena amakhuwa kunena ngati amapemphwa pesa chifukwa amantha kuti oswalira awa asawathandizapo kale. Kachipatala kamenekanso kuti zigawo za theatre ndi gausi anatengedwa. M'Kachipatala ka Mzuzu, ogulitsa amapempha pesa asanathandize anthu. Ena amapereka zilezo zoipitsa. Anthu akuopa kufotokoza anzogulitsa awa chifukwa amaopa kuti sachitisidwe zabwino.

Gulu la liwulesi limadala dzina lake "Pay Up or Die." Ligwiritsa ntchito ziwiri ndi magulu asanu. Ligwiritsa amakonda kuzindikira pangozi lili ndi kachipatala kakaike. President Peter Mutharika anatha kuwuza Ntchito ya Kachipatala kuti iyanjanire ndi kumpatsa chilango kwa anthu awa.

Kholo la Parliament lonse logulitsa kachipatala, Anthony Masamba, anati izi ndizochititsa mantha. Anati bomo liyenera kukhazikitsa mwachangu. Zachipatala zapangitsa mabokosi a kundawala, nambala za simu, ndi mitu yapakati. Iwo nawonso napitiliza inu ndi gulu lowerengera zabwino.

en.title: Drug Shipment Worth K106 Billion Stopped at Border
en.body: South African police have stopped a truck at the Beitbridge Border Post. The truck came from Malawi and had drugs inside. Police found about 713,000 grams of a drug used to make mandrax. The drugs are worth around K106 billion. Two Malawians and one Zambian were arrested. Police used scanners and took eight hours to search the truck.
en.body_more:
The truck was stopped after scanners found something wrong. Border officials looked very carefully at the vehicle and found hidden drugs inside. The South African Border Management Authority said they used advanced tools to check the truck. A hidden part of the truck held the drugs. The three people arrested are now in a police station in Musina, South Africa. They will go to court soon.

The South African Home Affairs Minister Leon Schreiber said this is one of the biggest drug catches in South Africa. He said police knew about the truck before it came. They waited for it at the border. Police worked for eight hours to take the truck apart and find the drugs.

In Malawi, the police are waiting to hear more news from Interpol. Malawi Police Service said they do not have full details yet. A security expert said the catch shows that bad people use roads in the region to move drugs. He said some of these people may work for the police or the government. The Malawi customs office has not yet said how the drugs left the country.

ny.title: Mtondo Wamtengo K106 Biliyoni Watsekemuka Pamalire
ny.body: Apolisi a South Africa adasima sitima pa Beitbridge Border Post. Sitima inapita kuchoka Malawi ndipo inali ndi mankhwala mkati. Apolisi anapeza mamgani 713,000 amtengo a mankhwala omwe amagwiritsidwa ntchito kupanga mandrax. Mankhwala awa ali ndi mtengo wa K106 biliyoni. Anamangidwa Amalawi awiri ndi munthu mmodzi wachiBemba. Apolisi angagwiritsa ntchito makina anu akufufuza ndipo anatengela maola asanu ndi atatu.
ny.body_more: Sitima idasimidwa sitima yakuti makina adazindikira kuti pachuma pali chimene sipali chabwino. Amagulilo a South Africa anamufufuza sitimayo mwachimbezi ndipo adapeza mankhwala amene anali akubisidwa mkati. Border Management Authority ya South Africa ananena kuti amagwiritsa ntchito zingwe zabwinobwino kufufuza sitima. Chipangizo chimodzi chimene chili mkati mwa sitima chinali ndi mankhwala aja. Anthu atu atatu amene anamangidwa akuli ku polisi imu Musina, South Africa. Iwo adzapita ku khothi.

en.title: Malawi's factory jobs drop as manufacturing shrinks
en.body: Malawi's factory jobs are falling. In 2010, 4.3 percent of workers had jobs in factories. In 2024, only 3.9 percent do. A new report from the African Development Bank warns this is bad news. The country makes fewer things now, and what it makes has low value. A bank report says Malawi must grow its factories fast or jobs will get worse.
en.body_more:
Two big reasons hurt Malawi's factories. Power cuts stop work. Money for new machines and ideas is hard to find. The report says Malawi is weaker than its neighbours. Mozambique has 4.8 percent factory workers. Zambia has 5.7 percent. But even these are far below the world average of 13.74 percent.

Malawi's factories make mostly cheap things. Food and drinks are 38.4 percent of what Malawi sells. Basic metals are 37.4 percent. The country does not make high-value goods. The bank says this must change. Malawi must make more from farm goods. It must join trade deals with other African countries.

The government says it is doing work to help. Patrick Botha, a government speaker, said they are making new rules. They stopped the sale of raw materials abroad so people will process them at home. But a work expert, Bright Limani, said the government must do more. He said taxes on factories are too high. He asked for help so young people can find jobs.

Every year, 270,000 young people look for work in Malawi. But only 40,000 new formal jobs open. The bank says if Malawi does not grow its factories soon, more people will stay poor and out of work.

ny.title: Mgona wa zinthu zam'malawi wachepela kuchoka 2010
ny.body: Kuchita zinthu m'Malawi kuchepela. M'chisonko cha 2010, anthu 4.3 pa mazana amene ankagwira ntchito m'magona. M'2024, anthu 3.9 pa mazana okha. Bwalo lokhalankhula la Africa lananeneza izi. Zinthu zomwe Malawi zimapanga zili zochepa. Zinthu zimene zimapangidwa zimapangidwira pang'ono. Bwalo lonse limati Malawi iyenera kupanga zinthu zambiri ngati iyendi ntchito zichuluke.
ny.body_more:
Zinthu ziwiri zimapweteka magona a Malawi. Magetsi amatha ndipo anthu sampeza ntchito. Ndalama zopeza makina atsopano ndi zovuta. Lipoti likuti Malawi ili ndi vuto kuposa mitundu ina. Mozambique ili ndi anthu 4.8 pa mazana amene angagwira ntchito m'magona. Zambia ili ndi 5.7 pa mazana. Koma ifeyo ili pang'ono kuposa ulemu wapadziko lonse wa 13.74 pa mazana.

Malawi imapanga zinthu zazing'ono zochepa. Chakudya ndi inu zimapangika 38.4 pa mazana cha zinthu zomwe Malawi isavuta. Zitsulo zosavuta zimapangika 37.4 pa mazana. Malawi samagwira ntchito yapamwamba. Bwalo lokhalalankhula limati iyenera kukhala ndi vutoli. Iyenera kumangira pa zopsya. Iyenera kugwirizana ndi maiko ena a Africa mu malonda.

Gavumenti likuti liri ntchito. Patrick Botha, mnumwake walangiza kuti amasinthira milandu. Asindikitsanso kugula zinthu zomwe sithafinso kupangidwa. Koma Bright Limani yemwe amadziwa zinthu za ntchito anasambira gavumenti kuti aichire zambiri. Anati mlandu wa ntchito ndi wamkulu. Akafunsa kuti pantsi pa anthu apang'ono.

Chaka chilichose, anthu 270,000 amakhalanso akuchainga ntchito m'Malawi. Koma ntchito atsopano zimakhala 40,000 zokha. Bwalo lokhalalankhula limati ngati Malawi siyikupanga zinthu mabwino, anthu ambiri achisiya mtima ndi akusowa ntchito.

en.title: No forex means cannabis factory still closed
en.body: A big cannabis factory in Malawi cannot open. The factory cost $10 million. It needs money from other countries to buy machine parts. The company, MMC Laboratories, has waited almost two years. Eve Mwase works there. She said the factory can start in eight months if the country gets more foreign money. The factory will make medicine from cannabis. It will sell to Europe.
en.body_more:
MMC Laboratories already has most of the machines it needs. The drying machine came to Malawi. But some parts are still missing. Without foreign money, the company cannot buy them. Mwase said this is the main problem: "We are almost two years behind. The lack of forex is our main issue."

When the factory works, it will make 700 tonnes of cannabis products each year. This will bring $100 million to Malawi every year. Europe wants to buy these products. But right now, nothing is made. The money does not come.

Economist Velli Nyirongo said the government must help. He said the country must give foreign money to factories that sell goods to other countries. "The authorities need to give forex to factories that make money for Malawi," he said. He also said the government must make business easier and faster. Banks must help factories buy machines.

The Reserve Bank of Malawi said it will help. Boston Maliketi works there. He said the bank will look for ways to help factories like MMC. But many other small businesses have the same problem. They cannot buy what they need from other countries.

ny.title: Ndalama ya nje siiyikwanira, maseŵera a gasi sagwira ntchito
ny.body: Katundu wa gasi wamankhwala ku Malawi sanaloweza. Likiti liya $10 million. Akufuna ndalama ya ku nja kuti agule zigawo za makina. Kampani ya MMC Laboratories imakhalira patali. Eve Mwase akugwira ntchito kumeneho. Anati, ngati ndalama ikubwera, makhomoni atha kudziyambitsa mu miyezi isanu ndi itatu. Makhomoni awa atha kupanga mankhwala kuchokera ku gasi.
ny.body_more:
MMC Laboratories ili ndi makina ambiri kale. Makina osiyitsa zinthu asinthu. Koma zigawo zina sizakumwa. Ngati palibe ndalama ya ku nja, kampani iyi sititha kugula. Mwase anati izi ndi chinthu chachikulu: "Tili patali pafupifupi zaka ziwiri. Ndalama ya ku nja ndi vuto lakufa."

Posankha, makhomoni atha kupanga ma tani 700 a gasi chaka chilichose. Izi zidzabweretsa $100 million ku Malawi. Anthu a Europe akufuna kugulawo. Koma pano, palibe chomwe chikupangidwa.

Wawo ndi Velli Nyirongo anati boma liyenera kuthandiza. Anati boma liyenera perekeza ndalama ya ku nja kwa maseŵera amene amagulitsa zawo ku nja. "Anthu a boma ayenera perekeza ndalama kwa makampani amene amapha ndalama kwa Malawi," anati. Ananinanso kuti bomo liyenera kupangitsa ntchito mofulumira. Mabangki ayenera kuthandiza maseŵera.

Namukati wa Reserve Bank of Malawi anati ayenera kuthandiza. Boston Maliketi akugwira ntchito kumeneho. Anati banki lidzayang'anitsa njira zothandizira makampani ngati MMC. Koma maseŵera ambiri amasankhanitsa ndi mavuto awa. Osalile kugula zinthu zimene zakumwa kuchokera ku nja.

en.title: Malawi football clubs welcome reserve league, want money help
en.body: Malawi's top football clubs have said yes to a new plan by the Football Association. From 2027, each big club will have its own reserve team in a new league. The clubs like the idea. But they say they need money to pay for it. The Football Association said this plan on Tuesday. It will help young players grow and learn the game better.
en.body_more:
Big Bullets CEO Albert Chigoga said the new league is good. But he said money is a big problem. Bullets have a main team, a women's team, and now a reserve team. All three teams must travel around the country to play matches. This costs a lot. Other club leaders said the same thing. They all want the Football Association to find sponsors to give money.

Civil Service United's leader Rashid Ntelera also said the new league is good. But he asked the Football Association to check the ages of reserve players. He said clubs need good sponsors to help pay for everything. A sports person named Parry Chinyama said the league can work. But first, the Football Association must find a big company to give money.

The Football Association leader Fleetwood Haiya said this reserve league will help find new talent for Malawi. Young players will move up from the reserve team to the main team. This is good for the national team too. Before the full league starts in 2027, the Football Association will run a reserve cup in 2026. This will give reserve teams matches to play. If a club goes down from the top league, its reserve team also goes down. The Football Association is now looking for sponsors to pay for the new league.

ny.title: Mkalabu wa Malawi akonda nkondo yama reserve, akufuna ndala
ny.body: Mipira ya Malawi ayamba kungokhala bwino ndi chikondi cha ligi yatsopano. Kuyambira 2027, ulalo uliwonse wa mainkhondo adzakhala ndi gulu la ma reserve muligilo yatsopano. Midanidale ayamba kungokhala bwino ndi nkondoyi. Koma akuti akufuna ndalama zokayipitilira. Bungwe la football linakuwunika zinthuzi Lachisanu. Ligi yatsopano idzathandizira ochinyamata atsopano kuphunzira mapolo.
ny.body_more:
Mtsogoleri wa Bullets Albert Chigoga anankha kuti ligi yatsopano ndi yabwino. Koma anati vuto lalikulu ndilo ndalama. Bullets ali ndi gulu lalikulu, gulu la ana atsikana, ndi gulu la reserve. Magulumi onse atatu ayenera kusambira kumapeto onse a dziko. Zinthuzi zimakositsitsa ndalama zambiri. Anthu ena amene amayendetsa midanidale anankha zinthu zomwezi. Onse akufuna kuti bungwe la football lipeze anthu amene angapatse ndalama.

Mtsogoleri wa Civil Service United Rashid Ntelera anakonda ligi yatsopano. Koma anamfunsa bungwe la football kuti litsimikitsire zaka za ma reserve. Anati midanidale iyenera kupeza anthu abwino amene angapatse ndalama. Munthu wina mwina Parry Chinyama anankha kuti ligi iyi ingatheke bwino. Koma mbuyu, bungwe la football liyenera kupeza kampani lalikulu likapekezere ndalama.

Mtsogoleri wa bungwe la football Fleetwood Haiya anankha kuti ligi ya ma reserve idzathandiza kupeza anthu atsopano kwa Malawi. Ochinyamata ochokera mu ma reserve adzayenda kumagulu akuluakulu. Izi zili zabwino kwa gulu la nchi yonse. M'ma 2026, bungwe la football lizayambitsa mkalabu wa reserve. Izi zizapatsa ma reserve maps amene azacheza. Ngati gulu likatsika mu ligilo lakuluakulu, ma reserve akuwo akutsiketsanso. Bungwe la football likupitirira kulooza anthu amene angapatse ndalama.

en.title: Airtel Top 8 football cup starts June 13 in Lilongwe
en.body: The Airtel Top 8 football cup will start on Saturday, June 13, 2026. Two games will play at Bingu National Stadium in Lilongwe. Blue Eagles will face Civil Service United at 14:00. After that, FCB Nyasa Big Bullets will play Ekhaya. Bullets are the holders. The Football Association of Malawi said the news on Wednesday.
en.body_more:
On Sunday, June 14, two more games will happen. Mighty Wanderers will play Creck Sporting at Zomba Stadium. Silver Strikers will play Karonga United at Silver Stadium. The top four teams have home games first.

The second games will be on July 11 and 12. Ekhaya will host Bullets. Civil will host Eagles. Creck will host Wanderers. Karonga will host Silver. The teams with the higher total score will move on to the semifinals. If the score is the same, the away goals rule will count.

The semifinals will play on August 1 and 2. The final will play on August 29. The place for the final is not yet known. The winners will get K40 million. The second place team will get K14 million. All eight teams will get K4 million each. The best player will get K2 million. The top goal scorer will get K1.35 million. Each best player of each game will get K140,000.

Big Bullets won the cup three times in a row: 2023, 2024, and 2025. Last year they beat Silver 4-3 on penalties after the game was 1-1. Big Bullets have won four cups in total. Silver have won two. Wanderers and Eagles have one each.

ny.title: Mpira wa Airtel Top 8 imayamba Juni 13 ku Lilongwe
ny.body: Mapikisano a mpira wa Airtel Top 8 iyambike sabata, Juni 13, 2026. Masewera awiri akhala ku Bingu National Stadium mu Lilongwe. Blue Eagles azasonkhana ndi Civil Service United ola 14:00. Pambuyo pake, FCB Nyasa Big Bullets azasonkhana ndi Ekhaya. Bullets ndi omenyayo mchaka chathyathya. Football Association of Malawi inena nkhani yi pa Wenesiti.
ny.body_more:
Chipadi, Juni 14, masewera ena awiri akhala. Mighty Wanderers azasonkhana ndi Creck Sporting ku Zomba Stadium. Silver Strikers azasonkhana ndi Karonga United ku Silver Stadium. Timu zinayi za kumapiri zinayang'ana pamalo pawo poyamba.

Masewera athanthawu akhala pa July 11 ndi 12. Ekhaya isonkhane ndi Bullets. Civil isonkhane ndi Eagles. Creck isonkhane ndi Wanderers. Karonga isonkhane ndi Silver. Timu yomwe imachita ma goal ambiri kuonana nazo iyenda kumasewera a zapali. Ngati kupikisana, ma goal a mulungu wa sewera iyenda kuweruza.

Masewera a zapali akhala pa August 1 ndi 2. Sewera la kumapiri lichita pa August 29. Malo a kumapiri sakudziwika kale. Timu yomenyayo ipeza K40 miliyoni. Timu yachiwiri ipeza K14 miliyoni. Timu zisanu zonse zichoke na K4 miliyoni iliyonse. Ompsyi wabwino kwambiri apeza K2 miliyoni. Ompsya wa magoli ambiri apeza K1.35 miliyoni. Ompsya wabwino mu sewera lililonse apeza K140,000.

Big Bullets amenyedwa sewera ndi nthawi zitatu mobvuto: 2023, 2024, ndi 2025. Mchaka chathyathya anali ndi Silver 4-3 pa mpira wawo pambuyo pa nthawi yonse kukhala 1-1. Big Bullets amenyedwa sewera 4 nthawi. Silver amenyedwa 2. Wanderers ndi Eagles amenyedwa chimodzimodzi.

en.title: Maize farmers lose billions as government delays purchases
en.body: Maize farmers in Malawi could lose between 40 and 48 billion kwacha in sales money. The problem: the government has not yet given money to buy their crops. Two state agencies need 120 billion kwacha to buy maize from farmers. Instead, traders are buying maize at only 500 kwacha per kilogramme. The right price should be 900 kwacha per kg. Farmers are selling cheap because they need money fast.
en.body_more:
Three weeks ago, the government said it would release the money soon. But the National Food Reserve Agency and Agricultural Development and Marketing Corporation have not yet bought any maize. Each agency got 60 billion kwacha to spend. They want to buy between 100,000 and 120,000 metric tonnes of maize at the fair price of 900 kwacha per kg.

Policy expert Tamani Nkhono-Mvula said the delay is very bad. Low prices make farmers desperate. They sell because they need money for their families right now, not because they want to. This will make farmers poor and hungry in the future. Betty Chinyamunyamu, chief of the farmers group, said traders are breaking the law. They buy maize below 900 kwacha even though that is the lowest price allowed. Many farmers cannot even get back the money they spent on seeds and work. If prices stay so low, farmers may plant less maize next year, use less good seed, or stop growing maize altogether.

The head of the Food Reserve Agency, Bruce Munthali, said his group wants to buy more maize. They want to store 150,000 metric tonnes for the country, up from 82,000 now. This will help keep food cheap and safe. The grain traders group also asked the government to move fast. This year's harvest is about 3.3 million metric tonnes, which is good. But the country needs 3.5 million metric tonnes to feed all people.

ny.title: Abwenzi a chimanga achonda phindu laka 40 mpaka 48 billion
ny.body: Abwenzi a chimanga ku Malawi akuvuta. Banja lokhala ndi mdalidali samatsintsa ndalama zawo. Ndalama zina 120 billion kwacha ziyenera kupita kwa bwenzi lachimanga. Koma ndalama sizinafike. Abwenzi amayang'ana kugula chimanga, koma amayang'ana kumangira ndalama 900 pa kilo. Amalonda osakubwera chipatala akwanira kugula pa 500 pa kilo. Abwenzi amakwetelesa chimango pamitengo yotsika chifukwa akufuna ndalama.
ny.body_more:
Miyezi isanu kale, banja linasiya kuti lingathe kupereka ndalama. Koma kumapeto kuno, ndalama sizinabwera. Bwenzi lachimanga lachuma ndi malonda achuma akuyang'ana kugula chimanga ting'onoting'ono. Aliyense wamba analowa ndalama K60 billion. Akufuna kugula chimanga ting'onoting'ono pakati pa 100,000 mpaka 120,000 toni.

Msunthako wa sukulu wa uzeledwe, dzina lake Tamani Nkhono-Mvula, ati ndilo chandamula. Mitengo yotsika imapangitsa abwenzi kuti amakwetelese chimango pansi. Iwo akumayang'ana kugula pambuyo pansi chifukwa akufuna ndalama kwa mokaphapu. Izi zidzapangitsa abwenzi akhale osauka komanso osudzula. Mayi akuyang'anira abwenzi, dzina lake Betty Chinyamunyamu, ati amalonda akumangira chimango pakusagwiridza kachlaw. Awa amalonda amagula chimango pa 500 kwacha paloko, komanso izi zikumangira kuti abwenzi sagwire ntchito yawo. Ngati mitengo yotsika ikadedzera, abwenzi angasita kumamala chimango, kapena amasiya yomalako.

Muyang'anira bwenzi lachimanga, Bruce Munthali, ati akufuna kugula chimanga kwambiri. Akufuna kusunga ting'onoting'ono 150,000 toni mu nthaka. Izi zidzathandiza kuti nthaka ikhale yotsika ndi yoteteza. Chaka ichi, chimanga chaguma ting'onoting'ono 3.3 miliyoni toni. Koma nthaka yenera kukhala ndi 3.5 miliyoni toni.

en.title: Malawi export plan is not working as hoped
en.body: Malawi's plan to sell more goods to other countries is failing. The plan started in 2021. It wanted exports to grow to 20 percent of the economy by 2026. But exports are getting smaller, not bigger. In 2021, Malawi sold $1.02 billion of goods. In 2025, this fell to $913.8 million. The government now says the 2026 goal will not happen.
en.body_more:
The plan had big goals. It wanted to raise exports from 14.6 percent of all the goods Malawi makes to 20 percent by 2026. But five years have passed and exports are going down, not up. In 2023, exports were at their best at $1.03 billion. Then they fell to $958.5 million in 2024 and $913.8 million in 2025. This is the lowest amount since the plan began.

The real problem is that Malawi sells the same things to the same countries. Most exports are farm goods and low-value products. Very few new things are sold. The plan itself said this was a big weakness. Malawi needs to sell more kinds of things and to more countries. Without this change, the country will not make the $6.4 billion target for 2026.

Economist Murry Siyasiya said the news is very bad. He said that when exports go down and imports go up, the country has less money. This makes problems like fuel shortages worse. He said the government must do more to help make better products and build factories. Minister Simon Itaye agreed the 2026 goal will not be reached. He said the country has many problems: poor roads, weak factories, and less aid from other countries. The government is now looking at a new plan.

Itaye said the private business sector must help more. He said companies must make and sell more goods if Malawi wants to grow. The country's long-term plan for 2063 puts export growth at the center of creating jobs and bringing in money from abroad. Without a real change in how things are made and sold, this goal will be very hard to reach.

ny.title: Mapulaneti a kuvomeleza katundu pa 2026 sali bwino
ny.body: Mapulaneti a Malawi okhudzana ndi kutenthetsana katundu ku dziko lina palibe chilungamo. Mapulaneti iyoyo idayambitsa mu 2021. Inafuna kuti kutenthetsana katundu kufike pa 20 peresenti mu 2026. Koma kutenthetsana katundu kumachepa, sizikuchuluka. Mu 2021, Malawi idatenthetsana katundu wa dola 1.02 biliyoni. Mu 2025, idachepetsedwa kufikira 913.8 miliyoni dola. Boma lino likunena kuti kolero wa 2026 sudzatheke.
ny.body_more:
Mapulaneti iyoyo anali ndi zilingo zambiri. Anafuna kuti kutenthetsana katundu kuchuke kuchokera pa 14.6 peresenti kufikira 20 peresenti mu 2026. Koma zaka zisanu zapitapo ndipo kutenthetsana katundu kumachepa. Mu 2023, kutenthetsana katundu kunachita bwino kwambiri, kwa dola 1.03 biliyoni. Kenaka kunachepetsa kufikira 958.5 miliyoni dola mu 2024 ndi 913.8 miliyoni dola mu 2025. Iyi ndi nambala yochepera kwambiri kuyambira pamene mapulaneti iyoyo idayambitsa.

Vuto lolakwika ndi lakuti Malawi imatenthetsana katundu wofanana ndi kudziko limodzi lofiira. Zambiri ndi zinthu zogula kuchokera kugumbi ndi katundu womwalowu. Sindisonkhani katundu wa mitundu yosiyanasiyana ndikudziko lina. Mapulaneti iyoyo inena kuti ichi ndi vuto lalikulu. Malawi iyenera kutenthetsana katundu wosiyanasiyana ndikudziko lina. Ngati sizingachitike izi, dziko lidzisowe kulanga dola 6.4 biliyoni pa 2026.

Mkazi Murry Siyasiya akuti nkhani iyi ndi yovuta kwambiri. Akuti pamene kutenthetsana katundu kuchepetsedwa ndipo mtengo wa katundu womwalowu wukuchuluka, dziko lachepetsedwa ndalama. Izi zimapangitsa vuto lachepera mafuta ndi zinthu zina. Anafuna kuti boma liphunziritsi nkhani yokulaka ntchito ndikumanga zipangizo zotenthetsana. Minista Simon Itaye anatchula kuti kolero wa 2026 sudzatheke. Anena kuti dziko lili ndi mavuto ambiri: misewu yowonongeka, zipangizo zimomwe sizingakhale bwino, ndi thandizo lochepera kuchokera kudziko lina. Bomo linu likuyang'aniranso mapulaneti atsopano.

Itaye anena kuti achina bizinesi eni ayenera kuthandiza kwambiri. Anena kuti machitiko ayanira kutenthetsana katundu wambiri ngati Malawi ikufuna kukula. Mapulaneti a dziko la nthawi zina pa 2063 amayika kutenthetsana katundu paaliyense pa nthawiyi yokulaka ntchito ndikumveka ndalama kuchokera kudziko lina. Ngati sizingachitike kusintha, cholero ichi chidzabwera ndi mathata ambiri.

en.title: Questions Grow Over Secret Plan To Free Wildlife Trafficker
en.body: People are asking hard questions about a plan to secretly free and send home Lin Yunhua, a Chinese man convicted of illegal wildlife trade. Lin was sent to prison for 14 years but left after only four years when the former president pardoned him. Now he is back in prison on new charges. People say he tried to pay judges and prison workers for help while in jail. Reports now say some government people want to free and deport him before his trial ends.
en.body_more:
Lin Yunhua was first arrested for buying and selling wild animals illegally. His early pardon caused anger from groups that protect wild animals. They said the pardon was wrong and made Malawi look bad to the world. When Lin left prison, police from the Anti-Corruption Bureau caught him again. They say he paid money to judges and prison staff while locked up. The court said he was a flight risk and refused to let him go free while waiting for trial.

Now secret sources tell journalists that people in the government want to quietly send Lin back to China before the corruption case finishes. One person with knowledge of the plan said: "People are working day and night to free him and deport him before the next court date. The Director of Public Prosecutions and the Ministry of Justice should know what is happening." If this is true, many people will be very angry. It would raise big questions about whether the courts are free to work without help from politicians.

When asked about this, Director of Public Prosecutions Fostino Maere said he knew nothing about a plan to free or deport Lin. He said people could ask the Ministry of Justice for more answers. Lin last came to court early this month. Two state workers told the court what they knew. The trial was stopped and set for a later date. Legal experts say sending a person away before a trial ends would break the law and the constitution.

ny.title: Mafunso Akulu Pamachitidwe Okhulutsani Lin Yunhua
ny.body: Anthu akufunsa mafunso okhulutsana ndi chikondi chokhala ndi chigawo chimene chili ndi umphawi wa nyama chachilendo. Mwina pali njira yachinsinsi kuti amutulutsire komanso amutume kwachalo Lin Yunhua, mwina ayenera kukhala ndi mlandu wowonjezera. Analipidwe pa zaka 14 kuti azilimbe kwa kusambira nyama, koma analiphera zaka 4 payamba ndisadini zaprezidenti lakale Lazarus Chakwera. Mwakuti analekeza, anthu oteteza nyama ankhala akufunsafunsa. Tsopano adzatengana ndindi mluwa wina gulu la anthu limene lingakhale litali mwa kugulitsira ndi kulibenso koma anthu akugwira nthata ati kuli ndi plan yamubvutsa mu malo palit.
ny.body_more:
Mwina kuli anthu a boma amene akufuna kuthawa Lin Yunhua ku China asanathe mlandu wake. Wina amene akudziwa zimene zikuchitika anati: "Anthu akugwira ntchito kalipini ndi kalipini kuti amutulutsire ndi kumutumiza kumlendo payina posachepa ndidi mlandu umalire." Konse izi ngati ndi zoona, anthu onse adzakhala okwiya kwambiri. Zimenezo zizidzafunsa mafunso ovuta okhulutsana ndi ufulu wa milanduyi.

Atambuza Director wa Public Prosecutions Fostino Maere, anati saziba chimene chimachitika ndipangano loti amutulutsire. Anati anthu iyense atha kofunsa Ministry ya Justice. Lin analowa korti masikati a mwezi uno. Anthu awiri anamene anati zina zimene zimalanphulirika. Mlandu unatha ndi kuti uyenera kudzabwerera sana. Akatswiri amene akudziwa milandu iyense anati kutumiza munthu apansi asanathe mlandu wake sikhoza kuba ndi zikhomo zakazinu.

en.title: Malawi Fuel Drivers Say Tanzania Trucks Get Better Work
en.body: Fuel drivers in Malawi say the government gives more work to trucks from Tanzania. Groups of Malawian drivers say this is not fair. They say Tanzania drivers make many trips and get paid in dollars. Malawian drivers wait weeks to load fuel. They get paid in kwacha. This costs the country money when Malawi needs foreign currency.
en.body_more:
Frank Banda speaks for the Transporters Association of Malawi. He said one Tanzania truck costs about $13,500 to bring fuel into Malawi. That is about 23.4 million kwacha. He said if Malawi used local drivers paid in kwacha, the money would stay in the country. Banda said they talked to Mera and Nocma about this. They have not had good answers yet.

The fight over fuel trucks is not new. From 2020 to 2024, Tanzanian trucks used a system called DDU. Local drivers said this was not fair. They did not have the same chance to work. In August 2024, the High Court of Malawi said no to DDU. It said this system was wrong.

Francis Mkandawire works for the Truck Drivers Union. He said they have talked to the government many times. They have been to many meetings. But things have not changed. Mera said in April 2026 it gave 85 percent of fuel work to Malawian drivers. But drivers say this is not what happens. Tanzanian trucks still get the most work.

Mera spokesperson Fitina Khonje said she will keep talking with the drivers. She said they will tell the news what they agree on. Nocma did not say anything about this. Banda said if the talks do not work, he will ask members what to do next.

ny.title: Magalimoto a Tanzania Akuti Mchakulo Wambawenji wa Malawi
ny.body: Akatswiri a magalimoto a mafili mu Malawi akuti nyumba za mafili za Tanzania zimakuwa ndi ntchito yambiri. Mayendesi a Transports Association of Malawi ndi Truck Drivers Union akuti izi sizikuchitika moyenera. Akuti akatswiri a Tanzania amapanga misanzo yambiri ndipo amakulandila ndalama za dola. Akatswiri a Malawi amasunga masabata angapo asanatumize mafili. Amakulandila ndalama za kwacha.
ny.body_more:
Frank Banda ndi mneneri wa Transporters Association of Malawi. Akuti galimoto imodzi ya Tanzania imatera ndalama zokwera $13,500 ndi mafili mu Malawi. Zikufanana ndi kwacha za miliyoni 23.4. Akuti ngati Malawi ingagwiritse magalimoto a m'dziko likulu, ndalamayo ingakhale mu Malawi. Banda akuti alankhula ndi Mera ndi Nocma pa izi. Koma akatswiri akuti sianapereka mayankho abwino.

Cisitansi ca mafili sinachakufa. Kuchokera 2020 mpaka 2024, magalimoto a Tanzania ankagwiritsa ntchito njira inayitanidwa DDU. Akatswiri a Malawi anasiya kuti njirayi sinayenera. Iwo sinanali ndi mwayi wofanana. Mu Augustu 2024, Khothi Lalikulu la Malawi linanena kuti DDU sichonde. Linasiya kuti njirayo inali yosavuta.

Francis Mkandawire ndi wongochita pa Truck Drivers Union. Akuti akatswiri alankhula ndi boma kangangache. Apite kumitindo yambiri. Koma zinthu sizasinthidwa. Mera inasya mu Aprililo 2026 kuti inaperekeza ntchito 85 pesentsi kwa akatswiri a Malawi. Koma akatswiri akuti izi sizensi. Magalimoto a Tanzania sichichangu kusiya.

Fitina Khonje akuti Mera idzalankhula kwa akatswiri. Akuti idzati kwa nyuzipepala chifukwa chimene azigwiritsa. Nocma sanakugwiritsa ntchito. Banda akuti ngati kulankhula sichita, adzafunsani mamembala chiyani.

en.title: Senegal Prime Minister Sacked, Becomes Parliament Speaker
en.body: Senegal's President Bassirou Diomaye Faye fired his Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko on May 22, 2026. Weeks later, Sonko became the new Speaker of Parliament. Sonko's PASTEF party has nearly 80% of seats in parliament. He said he will not cause problems for the government. The move shows tension between the President and his party.
en.body_more:
Sonko was one of the top leaders who brought President Faye to power in 2024. The two men wore the same suit and tie to their first cabinet meeting and looked like close allies. But months of stress built up between them. Now Faye has removed Sonko from his job as Prime Minister.

Sonko's new job as Speaker of Parliament is powerful because his party holds most seats in the government. This gives him big control over laws and checks on the President. Some say this move balances power inside the party during a hard time for Senegal.

Sonko said publicly that he will not use his new job to cause chaos or break the government. His words are meant to calm people and show that Senegal is still stable. The world is watching to see if the two men can work together or if more problems will come.

This story is like what has happened in other African countries. In Kenya, President Uhuru Kenyatta and his Deputy William Ruto were close allies but then fought badly. In Malawi, President Lazarus Chakwera and Vice President Saulos Chilima also had a big split. Political teams often start strong after winning but then break apart when leaders want more power for themselves.

ny.title: Senegal: Mtsogoleri Sonko Asokoneza, Lero Ndi Speaker
ny.body: Purezidenti wa Senegal Bassirou Diomaye Faye anachotsa Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko pa Mei 22, 2026. Kanthawi kochepa kenako, Sonko anakhala Speaker wa Parliament. Chipani cha Sonko, PASTEF, chili ndi zigawo zambiri m'parliament ya Senegal. Sonko anati sadzagwiritsa ntchito maso ake kuti gwiritsa ntchito maphokoso. Ndithere kuti pamakonda ndi purezidenti pali mavuto.
ny.body_more:
Sonko anali mmodzi mwa atsogoleri akulu amene adatengera Purezidenti Faye ku ufulu m'chaka 2024. Anto awiriwo ankavala chigown chimodzi ndi tayi iyi pansi pa msonkhano woyamba. Ankakonda kwambiri. Koma nthawi ingapo yapita, kupikisana kumayamba pakati pawo.

Lero Faye anachotsa Sonko muma Prime Minister. Koma Speaker wake m'parliament ndi ntchito yayikulu, chifukwa chipani chake chili ndi ziyankhumi zambiri za ma seat. Izi zikampatsa mwabu waukulu pa malamulo ndi kumvetsa mafunso a purezidenti. Ena akuti izi ndi nthawi yoyenera mphamvu mkati mwachipani.

Sonko anati poyamba kuti sidzagwiritsanso ntchito maso ake kuti gwiritsa ntchito maphokoso mu boma. Mawu ake amalankhula ndi anthu ndi kumvetsa kuti Senegal ili ndithere. Dziko lonse likuyang'ana kuti mawu ake ndiwona.

Ntchitoyi ndi ngati zina zomwe zayamba m'mayiko ena a Africa. Mu Kenya, Uhuru Kenyatta ndi William Ruto anakonda kwambiri koma tsopano akupikisana. Mu Malawi, Lazarus Chakwera ndi Saulos Chilima anakonda koma sinu ndithere lero. Gulu lakukonda lachipani limalowa ndithere pomwe zikantikiliza, koma pambuyo pake anthu akufuna mphamvu ndi kumenyana.

en.title: Court cuts coach's pay claim from 916 million to 53 million
en.body: A court in Malawi has cut former football coach Patrick Mabedi's money demand from MK916 million to MK53.7 million. The Industrial Relations Court said Mabedi was fired in an unfair way. The Football Association of Malawi must pay him the money in seven days. Mabedi was the coach of the Malawi national team. He was fired in October 2024 after just one year of work.
en.body_more:
Mabedi started his job as coach in November 2023. He signed a two-year deal with the football body. But in October 2024, he was fired because of poor game results. The court said the firing was wrong. It said Mabedi was never told what he did wrong. He was never called to a meeting to talk about it. He was never asked to say his side of the story before he lost his job.

The court found two big problems with how the football body acted. It said the firing was unfair. It also said the football body broke the work rules. The judge worked out what Mabedi should get. His pay was MK8.4 million each month. He also got fuel — 350 litres each month. When the court added this up, his pay was MK10.7 million a month. The court gave him three months of pay for the unfair firing. This was MK32.2 million. The court also gave him MK21.4 million for breaking the work rules.

Mabedi had asked for much more money. He wanted MK917 million. He asked for a new car, plane tickets, a bonus, and other things. The court said no to almost all of this. The judge said many of these things were not real or were already paid in other ways. The football body now has seven days to pay the money. Both sides can ask a higher court to look at this choice again. They have 30 days to do this.

ny.title: Mahosuthu wake Mabedi kuchoka MK916 miliyoni mpaka MK53 miliyoni
ny.body: Chikwati cha Industrial Relations Court chidachotsa ndalama zimene Mabedi anafuna kuchoka MK916 miliyoni mpaka MK53.7 miliyoni. Mabedi anali mphunzitsi wa timu ya Flames ya Malawi. Chikwati chinati adathetsedwa mosamalira. Football Association of Malawi iyenera kumupatsa iye ndalama muna masiku asanu.
ny.body_more:
Mabedi adayambira ntchito yake mwa November 2023. Adapulukilidwa pamtambo wa zaka ziwiri. Koma pa October 2024, adathetsedwa chifukwa zotsatira zoipa za zipangiziro. Chikwati chinati madontho a ku mufunike anali osalungama. Chinati osanapumulikire za zomwe adachita. Sanachitike zipungizo kuti aziwunika momwe adakonda. Anathetsedwa osanachikumbuza kuti azilongosolere.

Chikwati chinapeza mavuto awiri a mtundu wamkulu. Chinati kuthetsa kwake kunali kosamveka. Chinati Football Association idachitchira malamulo a ntchito. Mwini mlangizi anapeza zomwe Mabedi amayenera kupeza. Malipiro ake ali MK8.4 miliyoni pa mwezi. Apeza ndi mafuta — 350 litala pa mwezi. Chikwati chidawerengera kuti malipiro ake ndi MK10.7 miliyoni pa mwezi. Chikwati chimupatsa miyezi itatu ya malipiro chifukwa chokuthetsa kosamveka. Ichi ndi MK32.2 miliyoni. Chikwati chimupatsa nawonso MK21.4 miliyoni chifukwa cha kuchita zosalungama.

Mabedi anafunsa ndalama zochuluka. Anafunsa MK917 miliyoni. Anafunsa galimoto yatsopano, ndege, ndi zina zina. Chikwati chidamkankhira. Mwini mlangizi chinati nzambazi zina sizili ndi chimene chikukhala. Chikwati chinati nzambazi zina zapiyidwa m'njira ina. Football Association iyenera kumupatsa ndalama muma masiku asanu. Mbali zonse ziwiri zitha kufunsira kukhoti kokulera kuti zonse izi ziwonetse.

en.title: MAGLA gives K40 million to help FDH Bank Premiership awards
en.body: The Malawi Gaming and Lotteries Authority (MAGLA) has given K40 million to help pay for monthly awards in the FDH Bank Premiership. The money will reward the best goal and best save each month during the 2026-27 season. MAGLA and the Super League of Malawi (SULOM) signed an agreement on Monday in Blantyre. The plan will make the league stronger and give players more reason to play well.
en.body_more:
The prize for the goal of the month and the save of the month is K1 million each. The rest of the money will help pay for the work that runs these awards. MAGLA Director General Muchanakhwaye Mpuluka said the money helps football grow in Malawi. He also said the awards will help MAGLA tell people about the right way to use games of chance.

Mpuluka said football reaches many people in the country. This makes it a good way to share messages about safe gaming. He said the plan does two things at once: it helps find the best players and it helps teach the public.

SULOM Vice President Ronald Chiwaula was happy with the deal. He said the awards will push players to do better. Chiwaula also said thank you to FDH Bank. FDH Bank is the main sponsor of the league and agreed to let SULOM bring in MAGLA as a new partner. The FDH Bank Premiership is the top football league in Malawi and many people watch it.

ny.title: MAGLA amapereka K40 miliyoni kwa mpira wa FDH Bank Premiership
ny.body: Malawi Gaming and Lotteries Authority (MAGLA) amapereka K40 miliyoni kuti lifunse mphaso ya kupindula ndi kusindikiza pa chaka cha 2026-27 mumpira wa FDH Bank Premiership. MAGLA ndi Super League of Malawi (SULOM) anasaina pangano Lolemba la Lubumbeta pa Lolemba Monday mu Blantyre. Nkhondo iyi idzathandiza kulimbikitsa osewera kuchita bwino kwambiri.
ny.body_more:
Wina aliyense ayenera kupeza K1 miliyoni ngati anapindula goal yabwino kapena kusindikiza kwa mwezi umodzimodzi. Ndalama zina K10 miliyoni zidzagwiritsidwe ntchito kufuntsawo zodziwika za mphaso iyi. Muchanakhwaye Mpuluka, yemwe akugwira ntchito ya MAGLA Director General, anati ndalamazi zithandiza mpira kufalikira mu Malawi. Anatinanso kuti mphaso iyi ithandiza MAGLA kuwuza anthu kuti mchimwene wamaliseche umasiyidwe mozindikira.

Mpuluka anati mpira umafika kwa anthu ambiri mu dziko lonselo. Iyoyi ndi njira yabwino yotumiza uthenga kwa anthu okhudzidwa ndi dola dzalo dzomachimwene. Anati ndilo likhalidwe limodzi lokha: lithandiza kupeza osewera abwino komanso lithandiza kuwuza anthu mwachisalani.

Ronald Chiwaula, yemwe akugwira ntchito ya SULOM Vice President, anasangalala ndi pangano lino. Anati mphaso iyi idzapangitsa osewera kuti azigwiritse ntchito mphamvu zawo zonse. Chiwaula anatinanso asante kwa FDH Bank. FDH Bank ndi wosundira wamayamidwe wampira wa Premiership ndipo waloledza SULOM kudzakumbiza MAGLA monga mnzake watsopano. FDH Bank Premiership ndi mpira wamakulu mu Malawi ndipo anthu ambiri amawona.

en.title: MPICO gives money to architects' big meeting
en.body: MPICO, a big company in Malawi, gave six million five hundred thousand kwacha to help pay for a big meeting. The Malawi Institute of Architects will hold this meeting in Blantyre from May 27 to 29, 2026. MPICO said it gave the money because architects are very important for building and making the country grow. The money was given on Monday, May 25, in Lilongwe.
en.body_more:
Stella Sokosa is the boss of MPICO. She said that architects help to make buildings and cities. She said MPICO works with architects every day, so the company wanted to help them. She said the building work in Malawi is hard now because money is short and fuel costs a lot. But she said when companies like MPICO build new things, it shows people that Malawi will be okay.

Dorothy Nyong'onya is the deputy boss of the Malawi Institute of Architects. She said the money from MPICO came at a very good time. She said the meeting will talk about how to build strong buildings when the country has money problems. She said over 120 architects will come to this meeting. They will talk about how to make buildings that are good and will last a long time, even when money is hard to find.

Nyong'onya said MPICO is a good partner because the company builds things in Malawi. She said MPICO's building work will be used as an example at the meeting. She said architects will learn from what MPICO does. The big meeting will use a name: "Architecture at the Crossroads: Resilient Construction in Volatile Economic Environments." This name means that building in Malawi right now is hard but architects can find ways to make it work.

ny.title: MPICO amapatsa ndalama kwa akatswiri a zomangamanga
ny.body: MPICO, kampani yayikulu ku Malawi, idapereka ndalama za kwacha zisanu ndi masentsi miliyoni sisezeropeswezo kuposera msonkhano wa Malawi Institute of Architects. Msonkhano uyu udzachitika ku Blantyre kuyambira May 27 mpaka 29, 2026. MPICO inena kuti idapereka ndalama chifukwa akatswiri a zomangamanga ndi ofunikira kwambiri pa ntchito ya kumanga ndi kusika. Ndalama zinaperekedwa pa Lachisanu, May 25, ku Lilongwe.
ny.body_more:
Stella Sokosa ndi mfumu wa MPICO. Anena kuti akatswiri a zomangamanga amathandiza kumanga nyumba ndi mizinda. Anena kuti MPICO imagwirizana ndi akatswiri onsewo, chifukwa chake kampani idafuna kuwathandiza. Anena kuti kumanganso ku Malawi ndikosavuta lero chifukwa ndalama zazitha ndi mafiyelo akugwira nthito kwambiri. Koma anena kuti kupundula kwa kampani ngati MPICO kumawonetsa anthu kuti Malawi idzakhala bwino.

Dorothy Nyong'onya ndi womvera pamsozi wa Malawi Institute of Architects. Anena kuti ndalama za MPICO zapeza nthawi yabwino kwambili. Anena kuti msonkhano uyu udzakambirana mmene mungamangire nyumba zachisale pomwe ndalama zili zochepa. Anena kuti akatswiri monga 120 azipitilira ku msonkhano. Iwo akambirana mmene angamangire nyumba zomwe zimayenera ndipo zidzakhala ndi mphamvu kwambiri, ngakhale ndalama zikutsutsani.

Nyong'onya anena kuti MPICO ndi mnzake wabwino chifukwa kampani imamangira kwambili ku Malawi. Anena kuti ntchito ya kumanga ya MPICO izagwiritsidwa ntchito kuphunzitsa akatswiri pa msonkhano. Anena kuti akatswiri adzisankhira zinthu mu ntchito ya MPICO. Msonkhano wamakulu uziyenera dzina: "Architecture at the Crossroads: Resilient Construction in Volatile Economic Environments." Dzinalo limachitika kuti kumangira ku Malawi lero ndikosavuta koma akatswiri angapeze njira zopangira izi.

en.title: Court Orders Lawyer Kamangila to Pay Judge Manda Damages
en.body: A High Court judge has ruled that lawyer Alexious Kamangila must pay money to Judge Kenan Manda. Judge Howard Pemba said Kamangila did not follow court rules. Manda had asked the court for help in March 2025. Kamangila did not send papers that the court asked for. Now the court will decide how much money Kamangila must pay.
en.body_more:
Judge Manda sent a letter to Kamangila on 7 October 2024. In that letter, Manda asked for 250 million kwacha in damages. He also asked Kamangila to say sorry in public and to stop making bad statements about him. Kamangila did not follow the court's directions. The court said Kamangila had to send witness statements and other papers within 21 days. He did not do this.

Judge Pemba said the case could go ahead without Kamangila being there first. The court has now told a court officer, called the Registrar, to decide how much money Kamangila must give to Manda. This meeting has not yet been set.

The court also gave an order to stop Kamangila from publishing the same words or words like them. Kamangila must delete the posts within 24 hours of getting the court's order. Kamangila has said he does not agree with what the court decided. The case started because of statements Kamangila made in October 2024.

ny.title: Khothi Gawula Kamangila Mufuna Kuwala Ndalama kwa Judge Manda
ny.body: Mzungu wa amalamulo Alexious Kamangila ayenera kuwala ndalama kwa Judge Kenan Manda. Judge Howard Pemba anasya kuti Kamangila sanathandize malamulo a khothi. Judge Manda anafuna thandizo mwezi wa Marachi 2025. Kamangila sanatumize zipepalipali zomwe khothi zinayemba. Tsopano khothi lidzaganiza kuti ndalama zingati Kamangila ayenera kuwala.
ny.body_more:
Judge Manda analemuza Kamangila pa 7 Okutobala 2024. M'lemuzomo, Manda anakumbira ndalama zamiliyoni 250. Anapitirira kumbira Kamangila kuti asalule pantsi pa anthu onse ndipo asekire kukunena zinthu zoyipa zomwe akunena za Manda. Kamangila sanathandize malamulo a khothi. Khothi linayemba Kamangila kuti atumize mphunzitsi ndi zipepala zina mupingo la masiku 21. Kamangila sanachite izi.

Judge Pemba anasya kuti nkhani ingathawire popanda Kamangila kukhala pano. Khothi liku Registrar, ndi wothandizira khothi, kuti azindikire ndalama zingati Kamangila ayenera kuwala kwa Manda. Mitulikumene yoganizira iji isanasankhidwebe.

Khothi linapatsako lamulo lokhetsa Kamangila kuti asalikiritse mawu omwewo kapena mawu ofanana. Kamangila ayenera kuchotsa mawuwo mu maola 24 akutengera kulanda lamulo la khothi. Kamangila anasya kuti sagwirizana ndi zomwe khothi zinaganiza. Nkhani inayamba chifukwa cha mawu amene Kamangila anagwiritsa pa Okutobala 2024.

en.title: 10.8 Million Malawians Live in Deep Poverty, UN Report Shows
en.body: A new United Nations report says 55.9 percent of Malawians are very poor. This means about 10.8 million people do not have enough food, clean water, electricity, schools or doctors. The report is called "A Matter of Choice: People and Possibilities in the Age of AI." It measures poverty in a new way. It does not just look at money. It looks at ten things: food, water, toilets, power, houses, schools and more.
en.body_more:
The report uses a score called the Global Multidimensional Poverty Index. Malawi's score is 0.231. This score comes from surveys done in 2019 and 2020. A person is counted as very poor if they lack one-third of these ten things at the same time. For most Malawian families, this means children go to school hungry. Homes have no electricity or clean water. People cannot easily get to doctors.

The report also says that poor people with little school do not use the internet. This is a big problem now. Digital tools are used for trade, jobs and money services all over the world. Many Malawians cannot use these tools. This leaves them out of new ways to earn money. Malawi's income per person is only 1,634 US dollars. This is very low. A workforce that is sick, uneducated and without basic services cannot help the economy grow.

Malawi ranks 172nd out of 193 countries on the Human Development Index. This shows how hard life is for most people. Economist Marvin Banda said the economy cannot grow when people are hungry and uneducated. "We cannot celebrate small growth when most people live without basic needs," he said. Francis Thauzeni from the Malawi School of Government said these facts must make leaders change their plans for health, schools and services. Without big changes, Malawi will not reach its goal to become a strong, modern country by 2063.

ny.title: Anthu 10.8 Million ku Malawi Akhalire mu Mdima Wa Ndalama
ny.body: Lipoti lina lochokera ku United Nations lidati anthu 55.9 pezenti a ku Malawi ndakhalira mu mdima mwanji. Izi zikutanthauza kuti pafupifupi anthu 10.8 miliyoni alibe chakudya cha okwanira, madzi otsotsa, magetsi, kuphunzira koyenera komanso doctors. Lipoti liyi likuitwa "A Matter of Choice: People and Possibilities in the Age of AI." Iliyeso mdima m'njira yatsopano. Sanaphunzire pa ndalama yokha. Iliyeso zinthu khumi: chakudya, madzi, chimbudzi, magetsi, nyumba, sukulu ndi zina.
ny.body_more:
Lipoti likugwiritsa ntchito njira yotchuka "Global Multidimensional Poverty Index." Malawi ali ndi gawo la 0.231. Gawo ili lidachokera kufunso kwa anthu mu 2019 ndi 2020. Munthu amatchedwa mdima mwanji ngati alibe gawo limodzi mwa zinthu khumi izi. Kwa anthu ambiri a Malawi, izi zikutanthauza kuti ana akuphunzira akufa nkhala. Nyumba ilibe magetsi kapena madzi otsotsa. Anthu alibe mphanvu yosankhanitsa doctor.

Lipoti linalinso kuti anthu osakumva sukulu sizigwiritsa ntchito intaneti. Ichi ndi mavuto akulu kwambiri. Zinthu za digito zikugwiritsidwa ntchito pamilandu ya chuma, ntchito ndi ndalama padziko lonse. Anthu ambiri a Malawi sanapeza mwayi ogwiritsitsa ntchito zinthuzi. Izi ziwabwezeretsa kuchokera ku njira zatsopano za kupeza ndalama. Ndalama ya munthu m'Malawi ndi US dollars 1,634 yonkha. Izi ndi yotsika. Anthu oyenda kumagalimoto akali olapha, osakhala ndi maphunziro oyenera komanso osalibe zinthu zofunika sizingathandize kuti economy ikaziuka.

Malawi ali ndi nomolo 172 mwa mayiko 193 mu Human Development Index. Izi zikuwonetsa kuti matumba ndi amaningi. Katswiri wa economy Marvin Banda anati economy sangathandizenso kuzikala kuwombera ngati anthu ali nkhala ndi osakhala ndi maphunziro. "Sitinadyeni kuzigwirizanya kuti ndalama idakwera pang'ono munthawi yomwe anthu ambiri akhalira popanda zinthu zofunika," anati iye. Francis Thauzeni wobwera ku Malawi School of Government anati mfundo izi ziyenera kumwalira inyumba yaufulu kuti azitembenuza mapulani a doctor, sukulu ndi zinthu zina. Popanda kusiyana kokulira, Malawi sichibvomereze chiyero chake chiti kudzakhala dziko lamagetsi m'2063.

en.title: More students can get government loans starting today
en.body: The government will give student loans to 38,000 young people in the 2026–27 school year. This is 9% more than last year, when 34,863 students got loans. Applications open today, May 25, and close on June 24. For the first time, students who study online or by post (called ODeL students) can also apply for loans.
