The loans board says the money is for students who need help and are good at school.
en.body_more:
ODeL stands for Open, Distance and e-Learning. These students take classes by post, online, or a mix of both. Before now, only students in regular classrooms could get government loans. Many ODeL students had to stop school because they did not have money. Now they can apply under the same rules as other students.

Vitumbiko Mzumara is a student leader at Mzuzu University. He said many young people come from secondary school and choose ODeL study. Some do this because the government tells them to. But many stop their studies because they have no money. He said the loan will now help these students finish their degree. He also said ODeL students are just like other university students. They do the same subjects. The only difference is how they learn.

Education expert Lexon Ndalama said giving loans to ODeL students is good. It helps more people go to university. He said it will not make the school work worse if the university checks that teachers are good and students do their work.

Benedicto Kondowe works to help education in Malawi. He said the 9% rise is good, but many more students need loans. He asked old students who got loans to pay them back. This will free up money for new students. He also said the government must check that only real students get the loans.

ny.title: Anthu ochita masomo a pa nthawiyi atha kupereka ndalama
ny.body: Boma lipereka ndalama za masomo kwa 38,000 ana a mphunzitsi mu chaka cha 2026–27. Uku ndi 9% kusiyana ndi chaka chatha, pomwe 34,863 ana anapeza ndalama. Kupereka kwa ndalama kudzayamba lero, Meyi 25, ndipo kudzathira pa Juni 24. Koyamba, ana amasomo a pa nthawi (ODeL) atha kuwonanso ndalama. Bodi ya ndalama imati ndalama ndi ya ana omwe ali ndi mavuto komanso omwe amasowa pa masomo.
ny.body_more:
ODeL ndi masomo omwe anthu amapeza kwa diposto, pa intaneti, kapena mwamzeyachokana. Kale, kokha ana omwe anachita masomo mu sekeleni amatha kupeza ndalama ya boma. Ambiri a ODeL atha kukhala osadwala masomo chifukwa cha ndalama. Tsopano atha kuwonanso ndalama panthawi imoiyonse ngati ana ina.

Vitumbiko Mzumara ndi mwananyanja wa anthu a pa yunivesite ya Mzuzu. Anena kuti ana ambiri achoka kusekondale akusankha ODeL. Ena chifukwa chakuti boma likawamwalira. Koma ambiri amatha kusiya masomo chifukwa savala ndalama. Anena kuti ndalama iyi idzathandiza ana iyi kutha degree yao. Anena kuti ODeL ana ndi ngati ana ina a yunivesite. Amachita ziphunzitso zomwezo. Kusiyana kokha ndi momwe amaphunzira.

Lexon Ndalama ndi phungu la masomo mu Malawi. Anena kuti kupereka ndalama kwa ODeL ana ndi bwino. Kumuthandiza anthu ambiri kupeza masomo. Anena kuti sikuzovuta kugwila ntchito zosekedwa ngati yunivesite iyang'aniziritso kuti maphunzitsi ali bwino ndipo ana amachita ntchito zawo.

Benedicto Kondowe ndi wothandiza masomo. Anena kuti kupanda 9% ndi bwino, koma ana ambiri akufuna ndalama. Ananena kuti ana omwe adapeza ndalama kale ayenera kuzilipira. Izi zizathandiza ana atsopano. Anena kuti boma liyenera kuyang'anira kuti kokha ana enieni apeze ndalama.

en.title: Union Says New Wages Are Not Enough for Workers
en.body: Malawi's government announced new minimum wages that will start on June 1, 2026. But the Civil Servants Trade Union says the pay rises are too small. Union leader Lameck Magawa said the new wages do not match the high cost of living. Domestic workers will earn K83,720 per month, up from K72,800. Commercial workers will get K157,500, up from K126,000. Magawa said even the highest wage of K400,000 for truck drivers is not enough.
en.body_more:
Magawa was very clear about his anger. He called the commercial workers' raise of K157,500 "just an assault." He said it does not help workers who are struggling to buy food, pay rent, and pay for school. The government thinks the new wages are good, but workers say they will lose the money to high prices very fast.

Magawa said the government made a mistake. He said they should have cut taxes for employers and workers first. Then they could have given the new wages. He said "The best thing government could have done was to reduce taxes on employers and revise Pay As You Earn for employees before coming up with these new rates."

Workers in Malawi have big problems right now. Food prices are very high. Transport costs a lot of money. The money is worth less and less each month. Many workers have jobs but still cannot pay for basic things. Even when wages go up, prices go up more. This is called inflation. Many workers across the country feel they earn more money on paper but are actually poorer than before.

Labour leaders have asked the government for years to link wages to real living costs. They say thousands of workers in Malawi work full time but still cannot meet basic needs.

ny.title: Mgwirizano wa Antchito Watsutsa Malipiro Yatsopano
ny.body: Boma la Malawi linalengeza malipiro atsopano omwe adzayamba pa Juwan 1, 2026. Koma Civil Servants Trade Union (CSTU) iyamba kuti malipiro owonowonjeza osachuluka. Mlengeziwo Lameck Magawa anati malipiro atsopano sakamalingana ndi mtengo wa moyo wakulo. Antchito a ku nyumba adzapeza K83,720 pa mwezi, kuchokera pa K72,800. Antchito a malonda adzapeza K157,500, kuchokera pa K126,000. Magawa anati malipiro akulu a K400,000 pa magalimoto sakawo okwanira.
ny.body_more:
Magawa anasuta kwambiri. Anati malipiro a antchito a malonda a K157,500 ndi "kudumpha kokwanira." Anati sizithandiza antchito akulu omwe akusamba ndi kuthenga chakudya, kumalipira kunyumba, ndi kumalipira sukulu. Boma likuti malipiro atsopano ndi zabwino, koma antchito akulu akuti ndalama idzatayidwa mabilis chifukwa cha mtengo wakulu.

Magawa anati boma lidakambirana. Anati mungasintso mwisonkhano wa malipiro koyamba. Kenako mungaphe malipiro atsopano. Anati "Kanthu kabwino kamene boma lingakhale nlakhale kuntsintsa mwisonkhano wa malipiro kwa ogulitsa komanso ogula koyamba." Izi ndi mawu ake.

Antchito akulu a Malawi ali ndi zovuta zambiri sero. Mtengo wa chakudya ukuikapo kwambiri. Mtengo wa ulendo uno ukuikapo kwambiri. Ndalama yotha mabilis. Antchito ambiri ali ndi ntchito koma sanathe ndikulipira zinthu zofunika. Ngakhale malipiro akuikuka, mitengo ikunenekanso. Iji iyi imatchedwa inflation. Antchito ongachulukanso a Malawi akuona kuti amagwira ndalama yambiri pa mapepala koma mwachinsinsi aliwoso ophangu.

Atigwirizano ta antchito akufunsa boma kwakanthawi kuti ligamulitsa malipiro ndi mtengo weniweni wa moyo. Akuti tanthauziwo antchito akulu a Malawi akugwira ntchito nthawi yonse koma akusatha ndikulipira zinthu zamuzimu.

en.title: UTM Party Does Not Invite MCP to Chilima Memorial
en.body: The UTM Party will not ask the MCP to attend a memorial service for former Vice-President Saulos Chilima on June 13, 2026. UTM spokesperson Felix Njawala said all other political parties are invited, but not MCP. Chilima died in a military plane crash in June 2024. The memorial will be at Ntcheu Stadium, about 15 kilometres from where Chilima was buried.
en.body_more:
Njawala said UTM wants to avoid trouble at the event. He claimed that MCP members were booed at a similar memorial last year, so organizers do not want that to happen again. However, he did not say which MCP members were booed or give more details.

MCP publicity secretary Jessie Kabwila said this was not true. She said she went to last year's memorial and nothing bad happened. She said MCP would have wanted to be at the new memorial to honour Chilima, who was Vice-President when MCP led the government. Kabwila said if UTM does not want them there, that is UTM's choice.

Bad feelings between the two parties started after the plane crash in June 2024. Some UTM people have said they think foul play happened, but two official inquiries found the crash was an accident. In 2025, on the first anniversary, President Lazarus Chakwera held one memorial at Lunjika Turn-off near the crash site, and Chilima's family held another one in Nsipe. UTM then organized a candlelight vigil that was attended by other opposition parties like the Democratic Progressive Party and United Democratic Front, but not MCP.

Political analyst Chimwemwe Tsitsi said it is bad for a political party to organize a national memorial and leave out other political groups. He said a memorial for a former Vice-President should not exclude people based on what party they belong to.

ny.title: UTM Siyana Kuimbitsa MCP ku Chisano cha Chilima
ny.body: Chipani cha UTM sachoosa kuimbitsa Chipani cha MCP ku chisano chachikulu cha Saulos Chilima pa June 13, 2026. Njawala, wolankhula wa UTM, anati chipani cha UTM wamuimbitsa machipani ena onse, koma si MCP. Chilima anamwalira mu ndege ya sitola mwezi wa June 2024. Chisano chidzakhala ku Ntcheu Stadium, pafupi ndi malo kumene Chilima anadikidwa.
ny.body_more:
Njawala anati UTM akufuna kupewa mavuto mu chisano chicho. Anati anthu a MCP anabludza kale pa chisano chachakale chonse. Koma sanaphe kuti ndiye aliyense anabludza kapena ndipo kunena zambiri.

MCP wolankhula wa chipani, Jessie Kabwila, anati izi sizinali zowona. Iye anati anagona ku chisano chakale ndi palibe chinachake chabwino. Anati MCP ankafuna kukhala kumeneko kuti kumvera Chilima, yemwe anali Vice-President pamene MCP ndi chipani lachikulu. Kabwila anati ngati UTM sakufuna, ndi chigwani cha UTM.

Chipani chicha ndi MCP chili ndi mano abwino kale kuchokera pa ndege inafu mwezi wa June 2024. Anthu ena a UTM akuti zinthu zabwino sizikungachitike, koma ophunzira omalitchi anati ndege inagwa ndi nkhawa yosiyana. Pa 2025, pano pakati pa chaka choyamba, President Lazarus Chakwera anachita chisano chimoza ku Lunjika Turn-off pafupi ndi malo amene ndege inagwa, ndi banja la Chilima linakanira ku Nsipe. UTM ndi changu inanso kukhala ndi chisano chambiri chamwezi ndi kuya cha chulu, chikasungidwa ndi machipani ena a Democratic Progressive Party ndi United Democratic Front, koma si MCP.

Owufuna nzeru Chimwemwe Tsitsi anati sikuziwona bwino kuti chipani chimoza chichupatsa chisano chachikulu ndikuluta chipani chinanso. Anati chisano cha Vice-President wanakale chiyenera kusankhana onse, osati anthu angapo chabe.

en.title: Masters win big, Silver draw with Wanderers
en.body: Masters FC moved to the top of the FDH Bank Premiership last night with a 4-0 win over Goshen City Dedza Dynamos. The match took place at Bingu National Stadium in Lilongwe. Hastings Ndau scored three goals for Masters. At Zomba Stadium, Silver Strikers drew 0-0 with Mighty Wanderers and now sit in second place with 10 points.
en.body_more:
Ndau opened the score in the 21st minute for Masters, and Enock Kaonga doubled the lead nine minutes later. The first half ended 2-0. Ndau added a third goal in the 54th minute and completed his hat-trick in the 81st minute. Masters coach Peter Mponda said his team played very well. "I am happy with how my charges have played today. They played according to plan," he said. This was Masters' fourth win in five matches, giving them 12 points at the top of the table.

Silver Strikers had the chance to move top with a win at Zomba, but the goalless draw left them in second place on 10 points, the same as third-placed Blue Eagles. However, Silver have a better goal difference than Blue Eagles. Silver played well in the second half but could not score past Wanderers goalkeeper Dalitso Khungwa. Silver assistant coach Mapopa Msukwa said the draw was acceptable. "We dominated in the second half and we were unlucky not to get a goal. All the same, most of our fixtures against Wanderers end in stalemates and plucking a point from this match is not bad," he said.

Wanderers climbed two places to seventh with their point. Coach Bob Mpinganjira said his team struggled in the middle of the field. "It was a difficult game. We didn't play our game, especially in the midfield. We lost a lot of balls and it was difficult to play our game," he said. In other matches, Mafco FC beat Chitipa United 2-0 at Champion Stadium in Mponela, with Clifford Msowoya scoring twice. Karonga United won 1-0 against Civil Service United at Civo Stadium in Lilongwe, with Godfrey Suwale scoring the only goal.

ny.title: Masters akweza pamwambamba, Silver kulumikizana ndi Wanderers
ny.body: Masters FC anachoka pamwambamba pa FDH Bank Premiership ndakuwapologoza Goshen City Dedza Dynamos 4-0 usiku lwa dzulo. Njira imasindikira kunali pa Bingu National Stadium mu Lilongwe. Hastings Ndau anagola magoli atatu kwa Masters. Pa Zomba Stadium, Silver Strikers anagula ndi Mighty Wanderers 0-0 ndipo akali ndi mfundo 10 mu ndawo yachiwiri.
ny.body_more:
Ndau anayambitsa magoli pa mphindi ya 21, ndipo Enock Kaonga anapindula msewu wa 2-0 mphindi zisanu ndi 9 pambuyo pake. Gawo loyamba lipotero likutsirizira pa 2-0. Ndau anadza ndi goli la 3-0 pa mphindi ya 54, ndipo anakwanira magoli atatu pa mphindi ya 81. Masters anawina ndilo lachinayi mu magemu asanu, ndipo akupeza mfundo 12 kuzungulira kwa Champions League. Msulidza wa Masters, Peter Mponda, anati antchito yake inasangalatsidwa kwambiri. "Ndine amala ndi momwe anthu anga anachita lero. Anasankhira ndi momwe tidavumbula," anati.

Silver Strikers anali ndi mwayi wokweza pamwambamba ndi kuwina, koma magoli 0-0 adawaleza m'malo wachiwiri ndi mfundo 10, yomweyo mwang'ano wa Blue Eagles. Komabe, Silver ili ndi mafunde ofanana ndi Blue Eagles. Silver inasintha bwino pa gawo lachiwiri koma sinadza ndi goli pasila pa Wanderers goalkeeper Dalitso Khungwa. Msankhanya wa Silver, Mapopa Msukwa, anati magoli 0-0 anali bwino. "Tinachita bwino pa gawo lachiwiri ndipo tinali osaonedwa kuwina. Koma magemu angawo ndi Wanderers amakwanira magoli 0-0, ndipo kupeza mfundo inu si chinthu chaipa," anati.

Wanderers anapunduka mfundo 7 ndi mfundo ina. Msulidza wa Wanderers, Bob Mpinganjira, anati magemu anali oyimba. "Anali magemu oyimba kwambiri. Sitinadza nthawi yathu, makamaka kumapeto. Tinalephereka mpira ndi kumapeto kunali koyimba," anati. M'magemu ena, Mafco anawina pa Chitipa United 2-0, Clifford Msowoya anagola magoli awiri. Karonga United inawonawo 1-0 Civil Service United, ndi Godfrey Suwale akupela goli.

en.title: Mozambique invites Malawi businesses to use new port
en.body: The Government of Mozambique has asked Malawian business people to invest in Zambezia Province. On Saturday in Blantyre, Governor Pio Augusto Matos met with Malawi business leaders. He said Malawi can use Quelimane Port on the ocean. The port is 306 kilometres from Malawi. This will help Malawi trade more easily. Matos said Zambezia Province is a safe place to do business.
en.body_more:
Lucky Mfungwe is the business director of the Malawi Confederation of Chambers of Commerce and Industry. He said using Quelimane Port will help Malawi. It will cost less money to ship goods. He asked Malawi businesses to take this chance. The meeting was called "Zambezia open for business: Trade and investment opportunities for Malawi through the Port of Quelimane."

Rachel Mijiga works for HS Winehouse Beverages. Her company makes Capestars liquor with Ethanol Company Limited. She said her company is talking with Mozambique about starting work there. She said they want to begin in Mozambique by the end of this year. Her business is not the only one interested. Shoprite, a big shop from South Africa, is also watching what happens. A Shoprite person said their company can bring more jobs. They can help with storage and moving goods.

The port is also the capital of Zambezia Province. This means the rules are clear for new businesses. Malawi businesses will pay less to move their goods. They can sell to other countries more easily. This can help many Malawi companies—not just food and drink makers. Small businesses in packing and moving goods can also grow. Lower costs mean Malawi goods are cheaper for people in Mozambique and other countries. Malawi people will also buy cheaper goods from other places. Business people say this is a good time to start work in Mozambique.

ny.title: Mozambike imwalira Malaŵi kuti agule pa nsonga ina
ny.body: Boma la Mozambike walamula kuti aambuye a Malaŵi agule maphuphu m'Zambezia. Pa Sabata, Mfumu wa Zambezia jina lake Pio Augusto Matos anakumana ndi aambuye a Malaŵi m'Blantyre. Anawambirani kuti Malaŵi imatha kugwiritsa ntchito nsonga ya Quelimane m'nyanja. Nsonga ino ili 306 kilomita kuchoka ku Malaŵi. Izi zithandiza Malaŵi kugula ndi kugulitsa zinthu. Mfumu anasandila kuti Zambezia ndi malo abwino opangira bisinesi.
ny.body_more:
Lucky Mfungwe ndi wotsogolera bisinesi pa Malawi Confederation of Chambers of Commerce and Industry. Anati kugwiritsa ntchito nsonga ya Quelimane ndi zabwino. Kuwotcha maphuphu ndi zinthu zina sizakuwonjeza ngongole. Anayosera aambuye a Malaŵi kuti agwire mwai uno. Msonkhanano imeneyi idatchedwa "Zambezia open for business: Trade and investment opportunities for Malawi through the Port of Quelimane."

Rachel Mijiga amapangira Capestars, inuwo imapangwa ndi Ethanol Company Limited. Akunena kuti kapani lake akuyankhula ndi anthu a Mozambike. Akufuna kuposeza ntchito kumeneko m'kumapeto kwa chaka. Si yekha kapani ili. Shoprite, sitolo lalikulu lochokera ku South Africa, likuyang'anirangani. Wina pa Shoprite anati kapani lake atha kupanga maamalipiro angapo. Atha kuthandiza kusungira ndi kubolesera zinthu.

Nsongayo ndinso likulu la Zambezia. Ichi chimatanthauza kuti malangizo ali osangalatsa kwa aambuye atsopano. Aambuye a Malaŵi azabweretsera ngongole zochepa. Atha kugulitsa zinthu zawo ku mayiko ena mosavuta. Izi zithandiza kapani zambiri za Malaŵi—si ziamakuti zinthu zokumwa. Bisinesi yaying'ono ya nsisi ndi kubolesera zinthu ingathandizikenso. Zinthu zowonjeza ngongole zazing'i ndi zochepa. Aambuye ati nthawi yoposeza ntchito ku Mozambike ndi yabwino.

en.title: Football Legends Give Clothes to Elderly People
en.body: Football legends in Malawi gave clothes to old people on Saturday. The Football Legends Association brought sweaters and other clothes to Mai Mbambande Foundation in Dzama Village, Lilongwe District. More than 70 old people live at the centre. The clothes came from Finish Line Ministries. Leaders said this is important work for the community.
en.body_more:
The Football Legends Association leader, Gomezgani Solomon Mchawi, said that old people need help from everyone. He said: "These old people are like our parents. They raised us when we were young. So we must care for them now." Two other leaders, Rashid Ntelera and Shadreck Chikhosi, came with him to give the clothes.

The head of the foundation, Richard Masina, said thank you to the football legends. He said the old people need food, clothes and medicine every day. Winter is coming soon, so the sweaters are very good. Masina said the centre needs more help from people who want to give.

One old man, Kapiseni Ching'ombe, was very happy. He said: "May God bless the Football Legends Association. They show us that we are not alone." He said that his life got much better when he came to the centre. But the centre needs many things every day to help all the old people.

The football legends first got the clothes from Finish Line Ministries on Friday. They gave some to old football players at Bingu National Stadium on the same day. But they chose to give clothes to the old people at Mai Mbambande too. The leaders said this is what real football legends do—they help people off the ground as well as on it.

ny.title: Akatswiri a Ball Alowa Ngati Kunyumba Kwa Aakulu
ny.body: Akatswiri a mpira wa maginito, Football Legends Association, anapenda ngati kumpatula zovala ku Mai Mbambande Foundation m'mudzi wa Dzama, lilongwe. Pali akatswiri oposela 70 akulu amenewo akugona mchilankhulo. Zovala zinabwera kwa Finish Line Ministries. Atsogoleri a Football Legends anati kuti ntchitoyi ndi yofunika pa moyo wa anthu onse.
ny.body_more:
Mnanunkhulu wa Football Legends Association, Gomezgani Solomon Mchawi, anati kuti akulu akufuna thandizo lili lonse. Iye anati: "Aakulu awa ndi ngati atsikana athu. Aja anatiletsa tikhala atangata. Ndicho chifukwa tikudziwa kuti tiyenera kuwathandiza." Atsogoleri ena awiri, Rashid Ntelera ndi Shadreck Chikhosi, anabwera naye kuti apatulireko zovala.

Mnunkhulu wa Fundation, Richard Masina, anati zikomo kwa akatswiri a mpira. Iye anati kuti akulu akufunika chakudya, zovala ndi mankhwala Mthawi iliyonse. Nyengo yoozira ikubwera, ndipo masiweta anawophuta kwambiri. Masina anati kuti Fundation ikufuna thandizo lina kuchokera kwa anthu amene ali ndi mmitima ya kuthandiza.

Mmodzi mwa akulu, Kapiseni Ching'ombe, anali akondwela kwambiri. Iye anati: "Mulungu akatsime Football Legends Association. Iyawa akuonetsani kuti sitili okhaokha." Iye anati kuti moyo wake unabadwanso bwino pokalowa ku Fundation. Koma Fundation ikufuna matu ambiri Mthawi iliyonse kuti ithandizire akulu onse.

Akatswiri a mpira anamugula zovala ku Finish Line Ministries Lachisanu. Iwo anapela zina kwa katswiri akulu a mpira ku Bingu National Stadium lachi linalo. Koma iwo anasankha kuti apele zivalunga ndi akulu a Mai Mbambande. Atsogoleri anati kuti izi ndi zimene akatswiri enieni a mpira amachita—iwo anthandiza anthu panja pa ulalo ndi pa munda mwamipira.

en.title: Government calls back all diplomats, plans new team
en.body: The government has called back all diplomats who work at Malawi's embassies abroad. A new team will be sent to these jobs soon. The government says this is part of a planned change to match new priorities. Minister Shadric Namalomba said the government will tell people who the new diplomats will be at the right time. Some names have already appeared on social media.
en.body_more:
Three people have already said they have new diplomat jobs. Linda Kunje will work in New York as deputy high commissioner. Kondwani Munthali will work in the United Kingdom, also as deputy high commissioner. Leonard Chimbanga will work in South Africa as first secretary. They told people about their new jobs on Facebook.

The Foreign Affairs Ministry says it needs more time before it speaks about who will get which jobs. A list is moving around on social media, but the government has not yet said if it is correct. Parliament's Public Appointments Committee will check all the new diplomats when the government sends them the names. The committee must make sure all new diplomats are good enough for the work.

Benedicto Kondowe from the National Advocacy Platform says the government must choose people who are good at their work, not people who are political friends. He says if the government picks diplomats for political reasons, it may cost more money and hurt Malawi's work around the world. He says Malawi's embassies should help bring trade, jobs, and money to the country. They should also help Malawians who live in other countries and help local business people make links with other countries.

Kondowe says the government must choose diplomats who have the skills and knowledge to help Malawi grow. The new diplomatic team is one of the biggest changes in Malawi's foreign service in many years. It comes at a time when Malawi faces money problems and needs strong friends in other countries.

ny.title: Boma yibweretsa nkhondo zonse za ulendo, ndi team yatsopano
ny.body: Boma ibbweretsapo onse amene ankagwira ntchito m'mabungwe a Malawi m'mayiko ena. Gulu latsopano likubwerera kumenya ntchito zimenezo. Boma likuti ichi ndi chinthu cina chotsikapo kuti kugwirizane ndi zinthu zamakono. Minister Shadric Namalomba ankati boma likupereka uthenga wa anthu atsopano pa nthawi yabwino. Maina angapo atavimba mupaintano pa Facebook.
ny.body_more:
Anthu atatu atakale akati ali ndi ntchito zatsopano. Linda Kunje akubwera ku New York kumagwira ntchito ya deputy high commissioner. Kondwani Munthali akubwera ku United Kingdom, nawonso ngati deputy high commissioner. Leonard Chimbanga akubwera ku South Africa kumagwira ntchito ya first secretary. Onse atapeza anthu a maina awo pa Facebook.

Bungwe la Foreign Affairs likuti likufunika nthawi yambiri musanayambe kulankhula pa maina a onse. Maina angapo ayambika pampaintano, koma boma sichasankhule kuti ndi chowona kapena chachinyengo. Parliament's Public Appointments Committee idzayang'ana anthu onse atsopano pomwe boma likatumiza maina awo. Committee idzagwira mkati kuti anthu onse azikhala abwino pantchitozi.

Benedicto Kondowe akuwonetsa kuti boma liyenera kusankha anthu omwe ali oma na ntchito, osati anthu ambiri. Akuti ngati boma lasankha anthu chifukwa cha ubwampini, izi zingakutsa ndalama zambiri ndikuchotsa kampani ya Malawi. Akuti mabungwe a Malawi ayenera kuthandiza kuti mwabu, ogula, ndi ndalama zabwinike mu dziko. Ayenera kusankhana Amalawi omwe ali m'mayiko ena ndi kusankhana anthu akatswiri kumbuza anthu a mayiko ena.

Kondowe akuti boma liyenera kusankha anthu omwe ali ndi luso ndi nzeru zolinga kuthandiza Malawi kuti lipange. Gulu latsopano la anthu akatswiri ndi chinthu cakulu kwambiri m'mabungwe a Malawi. Ichi chimachitika pa nthawi yomwe Malawi ili ndi mavuto a ndalama ndipo likufunika anzache abwino m'mayiko ena.

en.title: Eagles go top after Bullets draw one-one
en.body: Blue Eagles moved to the top of the FDH Bank Premiership after a 1-1 draw with FCB Nyasa Bullets at Civo Stadium in Lilongwe. Bullets came from behind to get a point. Tonic Viyuyi scored first for Eagles in the 38th minute. Ephraim Kondowe levelled for Bullets five minutes into the second half. Eagles now have 10 points. Bullets stay on 9 points.
en.body_more:
Viyuyi's goal came from a header after Eagles started the match very well. In the second minute, a corner from Mecium Mhone almost gave Eagles the lead, but Innocent Nyangulu cleared the ball off the line to save Bullets. Eagles kept their lead at half-time, but Bullets came out strong after the break. Kondowe came on as a substitute and scored the equaliser when Eagles keeper Precious Masamba failed to hold the ball.

Bullets pushed hard for a winner after levelling. Hassan Kajoke played a fine pass to Madalitso Safuli in the 57th minute, but Safuli's shot went wide. One minute later, Andrew Jovinala's free-kick hit the Eagles wall. Wongani Lungu also came close but shot wide. Paul Master played for Bullets and was named player of the match. Eagles coach Deklerk Msakakuona said after the game that he wanted to win. Bullets coach Gilbert Chirwa said the draw was fair.

Eagles have now moved to the top with 10 points from their matches. Bullets are on 9 points, level with Silver and Masters FC, who play today. Eagles won back-to-back games before this draw, beating Silver Strikers 1-0 and Goshen City Dedza Dynamos 2-0. Bullets had drawn with Red Lions the week before without scoring.

ny.title: Eagles akupeza pamwamba pambuyo pa msonkhano ndi Bullets
ny.body: Blue Eagles anapita pamwamba pa FDH Bank Premiership pomwe FCB Nyasa Bullets anadala 1-1 pa Civo Stadium mu Lilongwe. Bullets anabwera kumbuyo kuti apeze mfundo imodzi. Tonic Viyuyi adascore koyamba kwa Eagles miniti ya 38. Ephraim Kondowe adascore kuti kufikile pa miniti ya 5 ya gawo lachiwiri. Eagles ali ndi mfundo 10. Bullets ali ndi 9.
ny.body_more:
Goli la Viyuyi linachoka ku header pambuyo poti Eagles adasamba bwino poyambira. M'miniti ya 2, kona ya Mecium Mhone inali ya kuloza, koma Innocent Nyangulu anachotsa bola palimu kuti asunge Bullets. Eagles anathandiza mfundo yawo pakati pa gawo. Koma Bullets anabwera ndi mphamvu mu gawo lachiwiri. Kondowe analowa ngati m'malo mwa wina ndi kuscore pomwe Eagles goalkeeper Precious Masamba sanathandize kunyamula bola.

Bullets anatsimikizira kuti apeze goli lina pambuyo pa kulinganiza. Hassan Kajoke anapitsa bola bwino kwa Madalitso Safuli m'miniti ya 57, koma Safuli ataponyanya. Miniti imodzi m'pambuyo, Andrew Jovinala atalodzaza free-kick kwa Eagles. Wongani Lungu anasonkhezanso koma atapanyanya. Paul Master amasewera kwa Bullets ndipo adasankhidwa ngati mseweyi wakuti.

en.title: MEC evicted from Lilongwe office over unpaid lease
en.body: The Malawi Electoral Commission (MEC) has been evicted from its office in Lilongwe. The property owner, Capital Developments Limited, sent a letter on May 21 saying MEC must leave right away. The Ministry of Lands did not renew the lease. MEC was given 14 days to respond to a new offer, but did not reply. The deadline was May 10, 2026. The government wants MEC to move to Blantyre.
en.body_more:
The property owner, through its manager Mpico Plc, sent the eviction letter to MEC Chief Elections Officer Andrew Mpesi. Stella Sokosa, the General Manager, wrote that MEC must leave by May 21 and give back the empty office. She said MEC did not answer two letters from the company. A new tenant is ready to move in on June 1.

The eviction follows an order by President Peter Mutharika from October 2025. The President said MEC, along with three other government bodies, must move from Lilongwe to Blantyre. Two other groups and one have already moved. MEC has stayed in Lilongwe because of court cases. All the court cases have been decided against MEC. A High Court judge threw out three people who asked the court to stop the move. Another judge said MEC was too late to ask for a review.

The Attorney General said MEC must move right now. The Office of President and Cabinet said the same thing. They said the government gave MEC three months, which has now passed. A person from MEC said the group has not yet decided what to do. An expert said the long wait creates worry and may take attention away from MEC's main job of running fair elections. The government said it will pay rent for a new tenant at the office in Lilongwe.

ny.title: MEC itulutsa m'nyumba yake ku Lilongwe
ny.body: Malawi Electoral Commission (MEC) itulutsa m'nyumba yake ku Lilongwe. Wene wa nyumba, Capital Developments Limited, anatumiza barasi pa May 21 kuti MEC iyende msanga. Ministry ya Lands sanarenyu kusamba nyumba. MEC inalipiridwa kuti ayankhe mkati mwa masiku 14, koma sanayankhire. Nthawi yopitira inali May 10, 2026. Boma likufuna kuti MEC isundukunze ku Blantyre.
ny.body_more:
Wene wa nyumba, mwa ntchito ya Mpico Plc, anatumiza barasi la kutulutsa ku MEC Chief Elections Officer Andrew Mpesi. Stella Sokosa, General Manager, analemba kuti MEC iyende pa May 21 ndipo ipereke nyumba yopanda anthu. Anati MEC sanayankhire barasi ziwiri za kampani. Munthu wina akusubirira kuyamba pa June 1.

Kutulutsa kumatsatira lamulo la President Peter Mutharika kuchokera October 2025. President anati MEC, limodzi ndi magulu ena atatu a boma, ayende ku Lilongwe ku Blantyre. Magulu ena awiri anasundukunze. MEC nayichira ku Lilongwe chifukwa cha milanzi ya khothi. Milanzi yonse idametsedwa. Mfumu ya High Court ituwala anthu atatu amene anafunira kuti khothi lisitse kusundukunze. Mfumu ina iti kuti MEC inali late kufunsira kuwerengetsedwa.

Attorney General anati MEC iyende msanga. Office of President and Cabinet anati chinchito chimodzi. Anati boma linapatsa MEC miyezi itatu, ndipo yapita. Wina wa MEC anati gulu lonjeza kuti salikhani zindikilo zathu. Wodziwa zinthu anati kusubirirako kwa nthawi kumabzukula nkhawa ndi kumapangitsa kuti anthu asaganizire ntchito yeniyeni ya MEC. Boma lidati lidzalipira ndalama za kusalira ku nyumba ina ya boma ku Lilongwe.

en.title: Malawi tobacco faces big problems with buyers and markets
en.body: Malawi's tobacco sales are in trouble. About ninety percent of tobacco was rejected at the auction floors this year. Tobacco is very important to Malawi. It brings money from other countries. Farmers need this money to buy food, fuel, and medicine. But now many buyers say no to Malawi's tobacco. The main problem is quality. Some farmers add sand and stones to make the tobacco heavier. Buyers want good tobacco, not bad tobacco.
en.body_more:
The Tobacco Commission works to help farmers grow better tobacco. But maybe it is not doing enough work. One idea is to sell tobacco to China instead of only to the West. China bought two hundred thirty-six million dollars of tobacco from Zimbabwe this year. Malawi sold only two point one four million dollars to China. This shows China may be a new market for Malawi's tobacco if the quality is good.

Other countries are also moving away from tobacco. People say smoking is bad for health. Many countries now have laws to stop smoking. This means fewer people want to buy tobacco in the future. Malawi's farmers will have big problems if fewer people want tobacco.

Malawi needs to grow other crops too. Soybeans, groundnuts, and nuts can make money for farmers. These crops do not have the same problems as tobacco. But farmers need help to grow new crops. They need to know the new crops will sell and make money. If the government and the markets support other crops, farmers may try them. This will help Malawi not depend only on tobacco.

Right now, farmers have no good choice. Tobacco is still the easiest crop to sell. Other crops do not have good markets or good prices. Farmers must feed their families. So they keep growing tobacco even if it is hard. Malawi must fix this problem fast.

ny.title: Tobacco ya Malawi ili ndi mavuto kwambiri pano
ny.body: Tobacco ya Malawi ili ndi zovuta. Zana ndi makumi asanu ndi masingano a tobacco zinakanidwa ndi oyendetsa pa malo a mtengo lino. Tobacco ndi chofunika kwambiri ku Malawi. Iyi ndi ndalama yeniyeni ya dziko. Malawi imagulitsa tobacco kuzipindizipindimu. Iyi ndalama imagula maungu, petrololi, ndi mankhwala. Koma lero ambiri oyendetsa akusowa. Vuto limodzi ndi kuti tobacco yini siyabwino. Amalimi ena amaika mchere ndi miyala patomu potaya kupsinjira kwa tobacco.
ny.body_more:
Tobacco Commission ndi ogwira ntchito yomvetsa malimi momanga tobacco yabwino. Koma minofu isiyakwanira. Lingaliro limodzi ndi kulengeza tobacco kwa China m'malo mwa kumvetsa zipindizipindimu. China inagula maliyoni 236 a dollars a tobacco kwa Zimbabwe lino. Malawi inagula maliyoni 2.14 okutsata. Ichi chikuwonetsa kuti China ingakhale malo atsopano a gunawa tobacco ngati tobacco ili bwino.

Mizimu ina siyakufuna tobacco. Anthu akumanena kuti mpungwe ndi zovuta. Mayiko ambiri ali ndi malamulo a kusiya mpungwe. Ichi kutanthauza kuti pang'ono pang'ono anthu angafune tobacco. Malawi ili ndi mavuto aakulu ngati anthu acepsa kupeza tobacco.

Malawi iyenera kumlima zina. Soya, nandolo, ndi nzama zingagulitse ndalama. Zolengeleza zinzayi sizili ndi zovuta zofanana ndi tobacco. Koma amalimi akufunika thandizo. Iyenera kudziwika kuti zolengeleza zina zidzagulitsa bwino. Ngati boma ndi malo ogulitsa azithandiza, amalimi atha kuyesera. Ichi chimvetsa Malawi kuti isadwele pa tobacco yokha.

Pano, amalimi alibe njira yabwino. Tobacco ndi zosavuta kugulitsa. Zolengeleza zina zilibe malo ogulitsa bwino kapena mitengo yabwino. Amalimi iyenera kuphira ana awo. Ndipo akupilingira tobacco ngakhale zili zovuta. Malawi iyenera kuthana ndi vuto lino msipakalikulira.

en.title: Pension Fund Report Shows Big Money Problems
en.body: A new report shows the Public Service Pension Trust Fund has serious money problems. The report says 1,144 workers are missing from the records. It also says K11.5 billion in workers' pension money was never added to their accounts. The fund bought a big hotel for K128.75 billion, and people say the price was too high. The report was made in July and August 2025.
en.body_more:
The fund looks after retirement money for about 139,000 civil servants. But the report found many problems. The fund did not have audited accounts since 2021. For nine months, the fund had no board to check what the leaders did. During that time, the leaders gave out K4.9 billion without board approval. This is against the rules.

The report also found six people listed twice in the records. It says K11.5 billion in worker money was not put in their accounts from 2023 to August 2025. The rules say the government must put money in within 14 days after each month ends. But this did not happen. Workers cannot see how much money they have saved for retirement.

The fund also lost money on other things. It paid K1.4 billion for land in Lilongwe. But the land was only worth K937 million. That is a loss of K463 million. The fund also lost K169 million on a computer system that did not work. One more problem: the government owes the fund K90.4 billion. The fund also put too much money in hotels. Hotels make up 37.2 percent of all the money the fund put in. Records were kept on old computer sheets that are not safe.

The Reserve Bank of Malawi now says the fund must clean up all its work. The bank said it will stop some payments and may fine the fund K100 million. A person from the fund said they are doing audits and will tell the public what they find.

ny.title: Ulimi wa Ndalama za Apolojeni Wotengeka
ny.body: Lipoti lokuti Public Service Pension Trust Fund likuwonetsa mavuto akulu a ndalama. Lipoti likuti anthu 1,144 akucikawa mu ndandanda. Kwazonse, K11.5 biliyoni ya ndalama za apolojeni sinashowe mu akawunti a anthu. Fundilo liligula hotelo ya Amaryllis ku K128.75 biliyoni, ndipo anthu akuti mtengo unakwera kuposa. Lipoti linapangidwa mu July ndi August 2025.
ny.body_more:
Fundilo likusunga ndalama za apolojeni kwa anthu okwanira 139,000 omwe agula nthawi mu boma. Koma lipoti likuwonetsa mavuto ambiri. Fundilo silizigwire msonkhano wa kuwerengera kuchokera 2021. Kwa miyezi isanu ndi inai, fundilo linalikhala popanda bodi iyo ingakuyang'anire zimene zechoncho. Munthawi imeneyo, atsogoleri adapereka K4.9 biliyoni popanda lamulo. Ichi si chimene chiyenera kuchitika.

Lipoti likuwonetsa kuti anthu asanu ndi mtundu anapangidwira monga awiri mu ndandanda. Zikuwonetsa kuti K11.5 biliyoni ya ndalama za apolojeni sizinawonjezedwe mu akawunti a anthu kuchokera 2023 mpaka August 2025. Lamulo likuti boma liyenera kukunja ndalama mu miyezi isanu ndi unawo lachisanu. Koma izi sizinachitike. Amalawi salaleza bwanji ndalama zawo zimene zili saladi.

Fundilo lidapenya ndalama pa zinthu zina. Lidagula malo ku Lilongwe ku K1.4 biliyoni. Koma malo amenowu unkalingilire kuti uli K937 miliyoni. Izo zimapezako kusakasa kwa K463 miliyoni. Fundilo lidapenya K169 miliyoni pa pulogalamu ya kompiyuta imene sinagwire. Ndipo boma likuliopa fundilo K90.4 biliyoni. Fundilo lidaika ndalama zambiri mu hotelo. Hotelo zimapanga 37.2 pezenti ya ndalama zonse zomwe fundilo lidaika. Nambala zinasungidwa pa mapepala achikale amene si amakondo.

Reserve Bank of Malawi yapeza kuti fundilo liyenera kusita ntchito yake. Banki ikutani kuti ingakwirira ndalama zina ndi ingakhoza kumulitsa K100 miliyoni. Munthu wake fundilo akuti iri kuwerengera ndalama ndi kuuzuza anthu zomwe zapeza.

en.title: Electoral Commission Ordered to Leave Lilongwe Offices
en.body: Malawi's Electoral Commission must leave its offices in Lilongwe. The landlord, a company called Capital Developments Limited, sent an eviction letter on May 20, 2026. The company said MEC did not reply to an offer sent on April 27. The offer was only good for 14 days and expired on May 10. MEC must leave by May 21.
en.body_more:
The eviction comes after the President ordered MEC to move to Blantyre last year. The government said it would not pay rent for MEC offices in Lilongwe any more. The government also said another institution will use the building instead. A high court ruled that the President's order was lawful and MEC must obey it.

MEC has resisted the move for months. The commission said the move would cause problems with its work. But other government bodies have already moved to Blantyre without complaint. MEC is now the only one still in Lilongwe. A government spokesperson, Focus Maganga, said the move does not harm MEC's freedom to work. He said moving offices to a new city does not change MEC's job or power.

MEC's spokesperson Sangwani Mwafulirwa said he did not even know about the eviction letter. This shows poor communication inside the commission. A political expert said MEC made a mistake by ignoring the landlord's letter and losing the court case. He said the commission should have responded faster and made better plans.

MEC now faces a hard choice. The commission must find new offices or move to Blantyre very soon. The timing is serious because MEC runs elections. Any delay or confusion could harm the country's democratic work. MEC has lost both its legal fight and now its physical space. The next few weeks will show how the commission moves forward.

ny.title: Bungwe la Malawi Electoral Commission Lipandzwa ku Lilongwe
ny.body: Bungwe la Malawi Electoral Commission (MEC) liyenera kusiya ofisi zake ku Lilongwe. Anthu a ku Capital Developments Limited anatumiza chidziwitso cha kusiya malo pa May 20, 2026. Anati MEC sunachoka ndi kakatanidwe ka ofisi kana komwe kanatchedwa April 27. Kakatanidwe kameneku kunali kokwanira masiku 14 okha ndipo kinathera pa May 10. MEC iyenera kusiya malo pa May 21.
ny.body_more:
Kuyenera kusiyacho kumabwera pambuyo pa President yemwe analayicha MEC kusiyira ku Blantyre chaka chatha. Boma linati sichasalira ndalama za malo a MEC mu Lilongwe. Bomo linati bungwe lina limatengenga nyumba imeneyi. Chuchimu cha High Court chinasonyeza kuti malayidwe a President ndi ololera komanso MEC iyenera kumvera.

MEC yakokilana ndi kusiyira kundege kwa miyezi ingapo. Bungwe linati kusiyira kudzakwetsa ntchito zake. Koma mibungwe ina ya bomo yosalira yatha kusiyira ku Blantyre popanda vuto. MEC ndiyo yokha imene imakhalakala mu Lilongwe. Yankho la bomo ndi Focus Maganga linasonyeza kuti kusiyira sikulangitsa ufulu wa MEC. Anati kusiyira ofisi kumudzi wina sikuzichita kuti MEC ilose ntchito zake.

Mlangizani wa MEC Sangwani Mwafulirwa anati azidziwa nawonso za chidziwitso cha kusiyira. Izi zikuwonetsa kuti kuwalamunika mkati mwa bungwe sikuli bwino. Mphunzitsi wa mfundo za boma anasonyeza kuti MEC idachita zolakwa. Anati bungwe linayenera kuvomereza mwachangu ndipo kukonzekera bwino.

MEC zino ili ndi nzeru zovuta. Bungwe liyenera kupeza malo atsopano kapena kusiyira ku Blantyre mwachangu. Nthawi imeneyi ndiyovuta chifukwa MEC ipangitsa mapungwe. Sanjika kapena kupsinjika kumatha kukangana ndi ntchito ya boma. MEC yalonda mfundo yake ngakhale ndipo sizikhalakali malo ake. Masiku otsatira adzawonetsa momwe bungwe lifike mbiri ino.

en.title: Army arrests five Russians over illegal mining in Malawi
en.body: The Malawi Defence Force arrested five Russian nationals in Chikwawa on Wednesday, May 20, 2026. They are accused of entering the country without permission and searching for minerals illegally. The men are named Badalyan, Rushan Nersisyan, Andreir Sklyarchuk, Mark Sklyarchuk, and Aleksel Latcyk. They worked for two companies: Baze International Limited and Avro International Limited. A local group called CDEDI said Malawians must protect the country's mineral wealth.
en.body_more:
The arrests came after the Ministry of Mining and Energy met with artisanal miners. Mining Minister Jean Mathanga and artisanal miner leader James Kudazala agreed to work together. The miners promised to tell the government about illegal mining activities. One week after this meeting, CDEDI got a tip. Someone said six Russian men were staying at a lodge in Chikwawa. They had been there for three weeks. They did not let the lodge staff clean their rooms. This strange behavior worried people. CDEDI told the government. The army then arrested the five men.

CDEDI's leader Sylvester Namiwa said hotels and lodges must report suspicious guests. He said this is important for the country. He also said the government must make the Mining and Minerals Regulatory Authority stronger. This authority does not have enough workers. Many jobs are empty. Namiwa asked the government to stop giving mining licenses to foreign people. He said all licenses that are more than three years old must be cancelled.

"The minerals in our soil are our wealth," Namiwa said. "Let us protect them to benefit us and generations to come." The case shows how ordinary people and the government can work as a team. Citizens who report suspicious activity help catch criminals who steal Malawi's resources.

ny.title: Asilikali anabweretsa asilankera asanu a Chirusi chifukwa cha mining
ny.body: Asilikali a Malawi anabweretsa asilankera asanu a Chirusi pa Chikwawa pa Wanesiti, 20 Meyi 2026. Iwo amakusulidwa kuti analowa m'dziko popanda chilolezo ndipo akufufuza miyala kuti iyambe kutengekeka. Anthu onsewa ndi Badalyan, Rushan Nersisyan, Andreir Sklyarchuk, Mark Sklyarchuk, ndi Aleksel Latcyk. Anagwira ntchito pa makhomipani awiri: Baze International Limited ndi Avro International Limited. Bungwe lina la CDEDI linanena kuti anthu a Malawi ayenera kukonza kayezi ka dziko.
ny.body_more:
Kubwerera kwa anthuwo kunabwera pambuyo pa msonkhano wa Ministry of Mining and Energy ndi anthu omera miyala. Minister Jean Mathanga ndi mfumu ya anthu omera miyala James Kudazala ankagwirizana. Anthu omera miyala analonjeza kutulutsa uthenga ndi boma kumatsimikiza za anthu amene akumera miyala popanda chilolezo. Masiku asanu ndisanu atapita pambuyo pa msonkhano uno, CDEDI inalandira uthenga. Kunali anthu asanu ndi mmodzi a Chirusi akugona pakhosi m'Chikwawa. Iwo anakala kula masiku makumi atatu. Sanaloze kwa anthu a khosi kuti apange ntchito ya safini yao. Zamenezo zinathi zosocheza. CDEDI inauza boma. Asilikali inawo inawabweretsa.

Mfumu ya CDEDI Sylvester Namiwa anati malodgi ndi pakhosi ziyenera kutulutsa uthenga za amalinyata omwe anachoka kwalungu. Anati izi ndi zofunika bwino pa dziko. Anafunsanso kuti boma litembenuze Mining and Minerals Regulatory Authority. Bungweli lilibe anthu ozungulira bwino. Malo ambiri asili opanda anthu. Namiwa anafuna kuti boma liletse kupereka silense ya mining kwa asilankera. Anati silense zonse zomwe zanakhalako zaka zitatu komanso kupitilira zikathenso kuchotsedwa.

"Miyala yomwe ili m'nthaka ndi chuma chathu," Namiwa anati. "Tiyenera kukonza kuti titipindule ndi ana athu mawa." Nkhaniyi ikuwonetsa momwe anthu ndi boma angagwire ntchito palimodzi. Anthu omene amatulutsa uthenga amatsimikiza kuti achotse olephera kumera miyala popanda chilolezo.

en.title: Malawi Trade Fair Opens with Focus on Making and Selling
en.body: The 36th Malawi International Trade Fair opened on Thursday in Blantyre. Trade Minister Simon Itaye said Malawi must make more goods and sell them abroad. He said the country buys too many things from other countries. Instead, Malawi should use its own goods to make products for export. This will bring money into the country and create jobs. The fair runs from 21 to 29 May at Chichiri Trade Fair Grounds. About 200 companies are taking part from Malawi and five other countries.
en.body_more:
Minister Itaye told businesses to add value to Malawi's natural goods. He said this will help grow the economy and make the country stronger. The trade fair is run by the Malawi Confederation of Chambers of Commerce and Industry with help from the Trade Ministry. This year's theme is "Promoting Economic Stability through Manufacturing and Industrialization."

The head of the chambers, Ronald Ngwira, said fewer foreign companies are coming to the fair now because of money problems in Malawi. But he said local businesses still gain from meeting other traders and finding new markets. Companies from Mozambique, India, South Africa, Zimbabwe, and Tanzania are showing their goods at the fair.

The Ministry of Transport and Public Works is also at the fair. They show roads, trains, airports, and water transport projects. They want private businesses to work with government on these big projects. They also show new technology for paying road fees and keeping drivers safe. The Ministry says this fair helps people see where money is going and where private companies can help.

The fair gives local businesses a chance to learn new ways of doing business and meet people who give money for projects. Business owners can talk to government workers and find new work. The fair helps bring buyers and sellers together in one place.

ny.title: Mwika wa Malawi utsala Kupanga ndi Kugulitsa Zinthu
ny.body: Mwika wa Malawi wa 36 unatsalira locheza pa Lachiwechi mu Blantyre. Mnzake wa Malonda Simon Itaye anati Malawi iyenera kupanga zinthu zambiri ndi kuzigulitsa kunja kwa dziko. Anati Malawi imalanula zinthu zambiri kuchokera kudziko lina. Komanso iyenera kugwiritsa ntchito zinthu zake ndi kupanga zogulitsira kunja. Izi zibweretsa ndalama kwa dziko ndi kupanga ntchito. Mwikawa wachita kuchokera 21 mpaka 29 May pa Chichiri Trade Fair Grounds. Makampani 200 kuchokera Malawi ndi mayiko 5 ena ali mwikamo.
ny.body_more:
Mnzake Itaye anawuza abizinesi kuti achite zinthu zokhala ndi mtengo wokwera kuchokera ku Malawi. Anati izi zidzachititse kuti mapazi a Malawi akule ndi ziwiri zake. Mwika wamenewo ndi wachitidwa ndi Malawi Confederation of Chambers of Commerce and Industry pamodzi ndi Ministry ya Malonda. Mumutu wa mwikawa ndi "Promoting Economic Stability through Manufacturing and Industrialization."

Mvunguti wa makampani, Ronald Ngwira, anati makampani a kunja akwela pochepa pa mwikawa chifukwa chakusowa kwa ndalama m'dziko. Koma anati abizinesi a anu amadziwa kuti mwika uwu amathandiza pochezeana ndi anthu ena komanso kupeza msika watsopano. Makampani kuchokera Mozambique, India, South Africa, Zimbabwe, ndi Tanzania amasonyeza zinthu zawo pa mwika.

Ministry ya Transport and Public Works ikonso ili pa mwika. Amasonyeza zinthu zofuna kukhala: magalimoto a mpanda, sitima, ndege, ndi zinthu za njira yam'madzi. Akufuna kuti abizinesi a anu agwire ntchito pamodzi ndi guvenementi pa ntchitozo. Akusonyezanso njira zatsopano zomwe zimachititsila kugulitsa nazo zimakhalidwe zamapanda ndi kupereka chigamulo chakuposera. Ministry inati mwika wukuthandiza anthu kuti aone komwe ndalama ikuchokera komanso komwe abizinesi amatha kuthandiza.

Mwika umapatsa abizinesi a anu mwayi wophunzira njira zatsopano za kugwira ntchito ndi kuchezeana ndi abungwe amene amapatsa ndalama. Abizinesi amatha kulankhula ndi anthu a guvenementi ndi kupeza zinthu zatsopano zamene azagwira ntchito. Mwika wamenewo umapangitsa kuti ogula ndi ogulitsa azizane pamodzi m'malo limodzi.

en.title: Court lets election case against Gangata go forward
en.body: The High Court of Malawi said no to Alfred Gangata on Wednesday. Gangata is a member of parliament for Lilongwe. He wanted the court to throw out a case against him. But Justice Yakuwawa Msiska said no. Now George Zulu can move forward with his case. Zulu says the election result was wrong. He wants a new election in that area.
en.body_more:
Gangata is from the Democratic Progressive Party. He is also a minister for youth, sports and culture. In September 2025, there was a general election in Lilongwe Mtandire-Mtsiriza. Gangata won. But George Zulu, from the Malawi Congress Party, said the result was not right. Zulu asked the court to say Gangata did not win.

Gangata tried to stop Zulu's case. He said there was an earlier court case about the same thing. The judge would not agree. He said the earlier case was different. The earlier case was about the Malawi Electoral Commission's decision to cancel the first election. The new case is about whether Gangata really won after new votes were counted.

Zulu's lawyer said the problem was about null and void votes. He said the results sheet showed 445 votes that could not be used. But the commission said it got only 345 such votes. No one said why the numbers were different. This is what Zulu wants the court to look at.

After the judge said no to Gangata, Gangata's lawyer said his team will read the full court order. Then they will decide what to do next. The court has not set a date yet for the full hearing of Zulu's case. If Zulu wins, Gangata will lose his seat and there will be a new election.

ny.title: Khothi ikavomereza kuti msonkhano wa Gangata ukapitilire
ny.body: Khothi Kachikulu ya Malawi sikunavomereza chida cha Alfred Gangata pa Lachisanu. Gangata ndi mamembala pa Lilongwe. Anafuna kuti khothi lisisunge msonkhano ya George Zulu. Koma Judge Yakuwawa Msiska sanafune. Zulu akha ndi ufulu kupitilapo ndi msonkhano wake. Zulu akuti zotsatira za zisankho zinali zosayenera. Anafuna kuti pakhale zisankho zatsopano.
ny.body_more:
Gangata ali mu Democratic Progressive Party. Anso ndi minista wachitukuko cha ana, masewera ndi unizimu. Mu Seputembala 2025, panali zisankho zazikuluzikulu mu Lilongwe Mtandire-Mtsiriza. Gangata anagwira. Koma George Zulu, wochokera ku Malawi Congress Party, akuti zotsatira sizinachitika bwino. Zulu anabera khothi kuti Gangata sanagwire.

Gangata anatengera kumaliza msonkhano wa Zulu. Anati kunali msonkhano wina wa khothi zomwe zinali zofanana. Koma judge sanalankhule. Iye anati msonkhano wina unali wosiyana. Msonkhano woyamba unali wokhudza zisankho zimene Malawi Electoral Commission idachotsa. Msonkhano watsopano ndi wokhudza kuti Gangata anagwire ndithu podigitsidwa mavoti atsopano.

Mwini wa Zulu anati mufeso unali ndi mafunso a mavoti opanda phindu. Anati shiti la zotsatira lidawonetsesa 445 mavoti omwe sangathe kugwiritsidwa ntchito. Koma komishini inati inalandira 345 mavoti ngati mwano. Palibe yemwe anasankha chifukwa chake chiŵerengero chinali chosiyana. Ichi ndiye Zulu akufuna kuti khothi liyang'anire.

Makamaka judge anasankha kana Gangata, mwini wa Gangata anati eni ake adzawerengetsa lamulo lonse lena. Pambuyo pake adzasankha zomwe adzachita. Khothi sinaloweza tsiku lolankhulira msonkhano wathunthu. Ngati Zulu agwire, Gangata azisokonekeza ndi panizisankho zatsopano.

en.title: 45 students go to hospital after vaccine at school
en.body: On Monday, 45 students from Dowa Secondary School felt sick after they took a vaccine against bilharzia. They had stomach pain, headache, and were sick. The school sent them to Mponela Rural Hospital. The hospital checked them and found they had mild side effects from the vaccine. All the students went home after one day.
en.body_more:
The students took the vaccine around 3pm on Monday afternoon. By 6pm the same day, some started to feel ill. The head teacher, Saulos Mdalamba, said the students were rushed to hospital for care. The hospital's in-charge, Owen Chipewa, said the side effects were not serious. He said doctors watched the students at the hospital and all of them left in good health.

One report says the students may have taken the medicine without eating food first. The District Commissioner for Dowa, James Kanyangalazi, said this can cause problems. He said the medicine, called Praziquantel, is safe and works well. But when people take it with empty stomachs, they may feel pain, feel sick, or be dizzy. He said only this one school had this problem. Other schools in the area had no big issues.

Health workers say this vaccine stops a sickness called bilharzia. The government is giving it to all children in Dowa district now. Kanyangalazi told parents to make sure their children eat before they take the medicine. He said health workers will watch more carefully when they give the vaccine. He said thank you to the doctors, nurses, and teachers who helped the students fast.

ny.title: Ophungu 45 ku sekondale anapita ku chipatala
ny.body: Lachisanu, ophungu 45 a ku Dowa Secondary School adawala pokapeza vaccine ya bilharzia. Anali ndi maumimu, mutu, ndi kufa. Sekondale inawalimbikitsira ku Mponela Rural Hospital. Mankhwala anawaona kuti vaccine inawapatsa vuto laling'ono. Ophungu onse anapita kwa inu tsiku lililonse.
ny.body_more:
Ophungu anatengapo vaccine pang'ono pa capila ca 3pm pa Lachisanu. Pang'ono pa 6pm tsiku lililonse, ena anayamba kuwala. Mphunzitsi wakulu, Saulos Mdalamba, anati ana anadweremereka ku chipatala mwachangu. Wogwira nacho chipatala, Owen Chipewa, anati vuto lali laling'ono. Mankhwala anawalankhula ansomba ndipo ophungu onse anapita kwa inu.

Wina anati ophungu sanatenga chakudya koyamba kutenga mankhwala. Kachimwene ka Dowa, James Kanyangalazi, anati izi zimapangitsa mavuto. Anati mankhwala a Praziquantel ndi abwino ndipo amagwira kanthu. Koma akadya ndi mphuno yopanda chakudya, anthu atha kuwala, kufa, kapena kudziyeyesa. Anati sekondale imodzi yokha yavute. Masekondale ena a m'deralo sapangisa mavuto akulu.

Mankhwala anati vaccine iyi imayimitsira matenda a bilharzia. Boma likugawa mankhwala awa kwa ana onse a Dowa. Kachimwene anena kwa ana akuti asanamtenga vaccine akutenga chakudya koyamba. Anati mankhwala ayendera ndikuyang'anira moyenera posambira vaccine. Anasiya mwachangu mamankhwala, aphunzitsi, ndi odzigwira nacho chipatala chifukwa chamathandiza ana.

en.title: MDF soldiers learn smart money skills in new workshops
en.body: Old Mutual Malawi has run money classes for soldiers and other workers across the country. The classes teach people how to save, budget, and plan for the future. Soldiers from the Navy, Cobbe Barracks, and other bases joined the workshops. Old Mutual says people need good money skills to stay safe when money is hard.
en.body_more:
Benard Chiluzi works as a Financial Education Manager for Old Mutual. He said the workshops cover many topics. People learn about changing bad money habits, how to save, how to invest, and how to plan for when they stop work. They also learn about emergency money and how to handle debt. Chiluzi said these things are very important right now because money is hard to find and prices are high.

The workshops reached soldiers in seven places. These were the Navy Battalion in Mangochi, Cobbe Barracks and Changalume Barracks in Zomba, MAFCO in Salima, Mvera Battalion in Dowa, Moyale Barracks in Mzuzu, and Chilumba Barracks in Karonga. Old Mutual also taught classes for other groups. These groups included Africa Global Logistics, Beit Cure, Malawi Parliament, and St Andrews Primary School.

Catherine Makokola works for Africa Global Logistics. She said the workshops are very good and very much needed. Many people have money problems, she said. People do not know how to manage money well, how to invest, or how to get ready for when they are old. Without help, people will have big money stress and worry. Makokola said Old Mutual is doing good work by giving these classes for free.

ny.title: MDF asankhidwa kuwelengeza ndalama zabwino
ny.body: Old Mutual Malawi yachita maphunziro akupempherera anthu pansi pa MDF ndi mabungwe ena. Maphunzirowa anawawa anthu kuti akonde ndalama, kuwerengeza, ndi kupanga njira zabwino. Asankhi a MDF ochokera ku Navy ndi malo ena ena anakumana. Old Mutual kunena kuti anthu akufunika luso lokhothalira ndalama.
ny.body_more:
Benard Chiluzi ndi woyang'anira maphunziro a ndalama pa Old Mutual. Anena kuti maphunzirowa aphunzitsa zinthu zambiri. Anthu amaphunzira kusintha nzeru zazibi pa ndalama, kuchokola, kupita pamapikisi, ndi kupanga njira pa chitukuko. Amaphunziransa nthenda ya ndalama yokhala ndi nkhawa ndi momwe mungayang'anire ngongole. Chiluzi anena kuti zinthu zino ndi zofunika kwambiri pa nthawi ino chifukwa ndalama zinali zosowa komanso mitengo yokula.

Maphunzirowa anachita masero awiri ndi khumi. Malo amenewa anali Navy Battalion ku Mangochi, Cobbe Barracks ndi Changalume Barracks ku Zomba, MAFCO ku Salima, Mvera Battalion ku Dowa, Moyale Barracks ku Mzuzu, ndi Chilumba Barracks ku Karonga. Old Mutual inawonetsa amalimbikitsira omwe ena. Mabungwewa anali Africa Global Logistics, Beit Cure, Parliament ya Malawi, ndi St Andrews Primary School.

Catherine Makokola akuthandiza mu Africa Global Logistics. Anena kuti maphunzirowa ndi abwino kwambiri ndipo akufunika. Anthu ambiri ali ndi vuto la ndalama, anene iye. Anthu sachimwa kwa bwino momwe angayang'anire ndalama, momwe angapite mu mapikisi, kapena momwe angapangire tsogolo lake. Ngati anthu sasankhidwa, adzasungidwa ndi nkhawa ya ndalama. Makokola anene kuti Old Mutual ikuchita ntchito yabwino popatsa maphunziro onsewa pa umoza.

en.title: Court stops Blantyre Water Board chief's suspension
en.body: A High Court judge in Malawi has stopped the suspension of Yeremiah Chihana. Chihana is the chief of the Blantyre Water Board. The court gave him leave to appeal. This means Chihana can stay in his job until a higher court makes a final choice. The board had suspended him for making decisions without asking them first.
en.body_more:
Chihana went to court and asked for help. He said the board should not suspend him. The board had said he made choices on his own, without talking to the board. Chihana then got an injunction, a court order that stopped the board from suspending him. But the board said the court was wrong. A court ruled against Chihana. This would have made his suspension real.

Now the High Court has stopped that ruling from going forward. The judge said the case has hard legal questions. The judge said it is fair to wait and let Chihana stay in his job while the case goes to a higher court. The board cannot suspend him or fire him while the appeal is happening. Chihana stays in his office.

The court also said the board and all its members must not do anything to make the suspension real. They cannot put it on paper. They cannot tell anyone he is fired. They must wait. The case will now go to the Supreme Court of Appeal. This is the highest court in Malawi. The Supreme Court will make the final choice about who is right.

ny.title: Khothi lapakana susumeseni wa Blantyre Water Board
ny.body: Khothi la High Court ya Malawi yasima kusambesa Yeremiah Chihana. Chihana ndi Chief Executive Officer wa Blantyre Water Board. Khothi linamupatsa chilungamo chokambirana pamlungu wolamulira kwambiri. Izi zitanthauza kuti Chihana angapitilize ntchito yake mpaka mlungu wolamulira wolamulira apeze nkhani iyi. Bodi ya Blantyre Water Board inasambesa Chihana chifukwa anagwira zinthu zosachita ndi mwaliko wa bodi.
ny.body_more:
Chihana anapita ku khothi ndipo akafunsani thandizo. Anati bodi iyi isamusambese. Koma bodi inati Chihana anagwira zinthu zake yekha, osachita ndi anzawo. Chihana analandira lamulezi la khothi lomwe linasiya bodi kumsambesa. Koma bodi inati khothi linali lombolombo. Khothi lina lacheka kwa Chihana. Izi zikutanthauza kuti kusambesa kwake kunawona mulingo.

Koma lero, High Court yasima lamulo limenelo kuti lisagwire mtengo. Jaji anati nkhani iyi ili ndi mafunso akulu amandala. Jaji anati kuli bwino kusunga Chihana mu ntchito yake pomwe nkhani ikupitiliza pa mlungu wolamulira. Bodi siyingathe kusambesa Chihana kapena kumuponya pamene kambirana kumapitiliza. Chihana amakhalabe mu ntchito yake.

Khothi linalitanunthula bodi ndi mamembala ake onse kuti asagwire ntchito yosambesa. Siyingathe kulemba pa pepala kuti wapukutsidwa. Siyingathe kuuza anthu kuti wapukutsidwa. Ayenera kuyembekezera. Nkhani iyi iyenera kupita ku Supreme Court of Appeal. Uku ndi khothi lolamulira kwambiri pa Malawi. Supreme Court iyenera kupeza nkhani iyi ndipo idzadziwa amene ali ndi anzake.

en.title: Tobacco Buyers Reject Crops from Independent Farmers at High Rate
en.body: Tobacco buyers in Malawi are rejecting tobacco from independent farmers at a rate of 93 percent. Members of Parliament visited auction floors on Wednesday and heard this from Alison Kambiri, the Tobacco Commission manager in Lilongwe. Independent farmers grow tobacco without contracts. Many are facing big losses after months of work and spending money on seeds and tools.
en.body_more:
The Tobacco Commission board was shocked by what they found. Daniel Gunya, the board chairperson, said the high rejection rates surprised them. He said the board will meet with buyers and farmers to find a way forward. But members of Parliament who saw the auction floors were angry and disappointed. They said the situation is unfair.

Anthony Kamoto, a lawmaker, said it is wrong that tobacco is bought at low prices and independent farmers cannot sell their crops. He said Malawi does not produce too much tobacco. He said blaming low prices on too much supply is not fair. Abel Kalima Banda, a farmer leader, agreed. He said farmers need to sell their tobacco because farming is how they live. He called for talks to fix the rejection problem.

Since the market opened, about 22 million kilograms of tobacco have been sold. But only 8,000 kilograms came from independent farmers. This shows how little room there is for farmers who do not have contracts. Kambiri said many things cause the rejections, but the commission will help solve this. If this trend continues, independent farmers may be pushed out of tobacco farming completely. This is bad for rural families who depend on tobacco for food, school fees, and medical help.

ny.title: Ogula Tobacco Asamatchala Akana Kuwala Madongosolo Opanda Pangano
ny.body: Anthu amene agula tobacco mu Malawi akana kuwala madongosolo a tobacco a anthu osasamatche. Peresenti 93 ya tobacco yomwe anthu osasamatche akabisala ndi anthu amene agula akuwala. Abwanapakhombo a parliament anakachera pa malo amene tobacco imaguliwila pa Mwazeqneza. Alison Kambiri, mnanso wa Tobacco Commission, akunena za vuto ili. Anthu ambiri akugona kugwa mu philimbi pamutu pamutu gawa chuma chochuluka.
ny.body_more:
Gulu lanzeru la Tobacco Commission litsala ndi zomwe zidazindikira. Daniel Gunya, ndiye nzeru ya gulu, anena kuti kuwala kopambana sikunachitika mwamoyo wawo. Anena kuti gulu la board lizakumana ndi anthu agula ndi mafarama kuti zithetse vutolo. Koma abwanapakhombo amene anakachera ma auction floors anali akudzaza ndi utali. Anena kuti sitili bwino.

Anthony Kamoto, mnzake wa parliament, anena kuti sikuti mwambiri pa mtengo wa tobacco ndipo anthu osasamatche sawala kugula tobacco yawo. Anena kuti Malawi sakagula tobacco kwambili. Anena kuti kusawadika mtengo ndi kuwula kwambili sikuti zinthu zili bwino. Abel Kalima Banda, mzake wa mafarama, wangozengeŵereza. Anena kuti mafarama akufuna kugula tobacco yawo popeza iyi ndi njira ya moyo wawo. Anakafunsitsa kuti azungulire za kuwala kopambana.

Kuyambira pamene ma market atambulidwa, kilogalaamu 22 miliyoni za tobacco zagululidwa. Koma kilogalaamu 8,000 yokhayo yanachoka kwa anthu osasamatche. Izi zikuwonetsa kuti paliyi malo ochepa kwambili pa anthu amene alibe pangano. Kambiri anena kuti pali zinthu zingapo zimene zimathandiza kuwala. Koma commission idzathandize kuthetsa vuto. Ngati zimenezo zikupitila, anthu osasamatche angawonekera kuti alibe malo m'mene mungakupanga tobacco. Izi ndi zovuta kwambili pawanthu amene alima popeza iyi ndiye njira yawo yopeza ndalama, zakudya, ndalama ya sekondale, ndi ziwirezi.

en.title: Malawi must cut prices to reach 15 percent inflation by 2027
en.body: Malawi's inflation is now 23.4 percent and must fall to 15 percent by March 2027, says the Economics Association of Malawi. Food prices are the biggest problem. They make up more than half of all prices in the country. Food also adds 13.5 points to the inflation number. The central bank keeps interest rates high at 24 percent to slow prices down.
en.body_more:
The group says food prices must fall by 1.5 points every three months to reach the goal. But many things make this hard. Fertiliser costs are high. Transport is costly. The kwacha is weak. These things all push prices up.

Housing, water and electricity now cost much more. In 2023, these made up 18 percent of inflation. Now they make up 23.7 percent. This is because of high power bills. Transport costs also went up. In one quarter, transport rose by two points.

Economists say the path forward is fragile. One expert says the cost of living may stay high even if the inflation number goes down. He says real help for poor people may not come. The central bank says it will keep using its money tools to fight high prices. But experts warn that tight money may make it hard for people to get loans and start work.

Fix the problem, the group says, will need many actions at once. Prices that the state controls must be managed carefully. Money costs in business must fall. The kwacha must get stronger. Food supply must get better. Money policy alone cannot do all this work.

ny.title: Malawi iyenera kuchotsa mitengo kuti ikhale 15 percent pa 2027
ny.body: Mitengo m'Malawi ikupunduka kwambiri. Kala 23.4 percent mu January-Marichi 2026. Iyenera kuika 15 percent pofika Marichi 2027, ndiye Gulu la Zachuma la Malawi. Mitengo ya chakudya ndi chipata chachikulu. Imapanga lingaliro lanyumba zabwino. Chakudya chimapangitsa kuwuka kwa mitengo ndi 13.5 point. Banki lazikulu likusunga kachikala ka chuma pa 24 percent pofuna kuchotsa kuwuka.
ny.body_more:
Gulu likunena kuti mitengo ya chakudya iyenera kufa 1.5 point kila miyezi itatu kuti ikhale 15 percent. Koma zinthu zambiri zimaweteza. Pezi la pulaneti ndi lotentha kwambiri. Zomwe zimayendetsedwa, zotsika mtengo. Kwacha ikugwedezeka. Zinthuzi zonse zimapukusa mitengo.

Nzeru za nyumba, madzi ndi mphafu linu yaloweka mtengo wambiri. M'2023, imapangidza 18 percent ya kuwuka. Tsopano imapangidza 23.7 percent. Izi zili chifukwa cha zoyesezerera kutentha. Mitengo ya momwe zipitirire izikwanso kuwunika. Mu mwezi itatu imodzimodzi, momwe zipitirire inapindula pa point ziwiri.

Akatswiri anena kuti njira imeneyi ndi yoopsa. Mzake mmodzi anena kuti pali mwayi kuti kufa kwa mitengo kungakhale kolinga ngakhale nambala ya kuwuka yichotsa. Anena kuti anthu ang'onoang'ono angakayambe pangani. Banki lazikulu likuti likonzekera kugwiritsa ntchito zake zonse kutsika mitengo. Koma akatswiri akunena kuti kugawira chulutsire mtengo wa magalimoto kuopa kutha kungazilakwitsa anthu kugula ndi kugwira ntchito.

Kuvuta izi, gulu likuti ayenera kugwira ntchito zambiri pamodzi. Mitengo yomwe boma likuyang'anira iyenera kugawidwa bwino. Mtengo wa kubunetsa iyenera kuchotsa. Kwacha iyenera kuwambitsa. Chakudya iyenera kuwonjezeka. Chulutsire chuma chokha sizingazithe ntchito yonse.

en.title: Old Mutual Fund Gives Members 94% Return in 2025
en.body: Old Mutual Life Assurance Company said its Guaranteed Fund gave members a 94% return in 2025. This is much higher than the 37% return in 2024. Mark Mikwamba is the Managing Director. He said the company made good choices with money and wants to help members save for old age.
en.body_more:
Mikwamba said the 94% return is not just a number. It shows that the company's plan works well. He said the return came from good choices, a strong fund, and focus on keeping members' money safe. This matters because it helps people who are saving for retirement.

The Old Mutual Guaranteed Fund is the biggest retirement investment fund in Malawi. It has three parts: Segregated Funds, the Unrestricted Fund, and Preservation Fund, which is also called Protektor. These give members different ways to get their money.

Mikwamba said many funds do well when markets are good. But the real test is what happens when markets go bad. The Old Mutual Guaranteed Fund has a protection system. This system keeps members safe from big changes in markets, both up and down. This means members get steady returns over many years. That is what makes Old Mutual different from other funds.

Mikwamba said the company will keep working to give returns that are higher than inflation. Inflation means prices go up. If returns beat inflation, members' money grows and stays strong. The company says it will keep protecting members' savings in the future.

ny.title: Old Mutual Imachitira 94% Return pa 2025
ny.body: Old Mutual Life Assurance Company inaneneza kuti Guaranteed Fund yayo idapereka 94% return kwa 2025. Izi ndi kwambiri kuposa 37% return mu 2024. Mark Mikwamba ndi Managing Director. Iye anathe kuti kampani idagwira bwino ndalama ndipo imafuna kuteteza ndalama za anthu akumakhala ndi zaka.
ny.body_more:
Mikwamba anathe kuti 94% return si chiwerengero chokha. Izo zikuwonetsa kuti njira ya kampani imagwira bwino. Iye anathe kuti return inayamba chifukwa cha zisankho zabwino, fund yolimba, ndi kuchita kuti ndalama za anthu zikhale salula. Izi ndi zofunika chifukwa zimachotsa anthu omwe akusunga ndalama pa zaka zaulemu.

Old Mutual Guaranteed Fund ndi fund yaikulu kwambiri pa ulemu ku Malawi. Ili ndi zigawo zitatu: Segregated Funds, Unrestricted Fund, ndi Preservation Fund, womwe amatchedwa Protektor. Izi zimapatsani anthu njira zosiyanasiyana za kupeza ndalama zawo.

Mikwamba anathe kuti ma fund ambiri amachita bwino ngati mamenyu ali abwino. Koma phunziro lalikulu ndi zimene zimachitika ngati mamenyu asakhala abwino. Old Mutual Guaranteed Fund ili ndi chikongofu. Chikongofu ichi chimagunda anthu kuti kugone kwa mamenyu, kungodzikeleza kapena kungokhazikika. Izi zikutanthauza kuti anthu amakoka ndalama ziwiri zimasintha pang'onopang'ono kwa zaka zambiri. Izi ndi zimene zimapanga Old Mutual yosiyana ndi ma fund ena.

Mikwamba anathe kuti kampani idzapitiliza kugwira ndalama zomwe zimakhala zazikulu kuposa chachiritsiro cha mtengo wa chimbudzi. Chachiritsiro cha mtengo wa chimbudzi chimatanthauza kuti mtengo wa zinthu umawonjera. Ngati returns izi zimakhala zazikulu kuposa chachiritsiro, ndalama za anthu zimawonjerera ndipo zimatsala ndi mphamvu. Kampani inathe kuti idzapitiliza kuteteza ndalama za anthu mbuyo.

en.title: MEPA lets Press Cane restart work after five months
en.body: The Malawi Environment Protection Authority (MEPA) has lifted a stop order on Press Cane Limited. The company makes ethanol and was shut down in January after waste water leaked into the land. MEPA checked the company's work in April and saw good progress. Press Cane paid 895 million kwacha to help the people who were hurt. The company can now start work again.
en.body_more:
MEPA gave the stop order on 26 January 2026 to stop more damage to the land. The company had to fix the waste water ponds, move dirt to make walls higher, and clean up the soil. The letter from MEPA boss Wilfred Kadewa on 18 May 2026 said Press Cane did good work. The company also built a plant to make the waste water clean and safe. No bad water will come out from making ethanol now.

Before Press Cane can work, MEPA says it must do more. The company must finish fixing all the waste water ponds. It must make the land good again so people can grow food there. The District Commissioner told MEPA which gardens need help. Press Cane must also pay for tests on the water in the area to show it is clean and safe.

Press Cane's boss, Bryson Mkhomaanthu, said the company is happy to start work again. He said they learned from what went wrong. "We will work hard to keep the land and water clean," he said. The company said it will follow all the rules and work with the people and the government to keep the area safe.

ny.title: MEPA aleza Press Cane kuti iyambe ntchito kachiwiri
ny.body: Malawi Environment Protection Authority (MEPA) yaleza Press Cane Limited kuti iyambe ntchito. Kampani imeneyi imapanga mkate wa alcohol ndipo idamala kwa miyezi isanu chifukwa madzi osakwanira anagwa mʼmunda. MEPA idi ganizirani ntchito ya kampani mʼAprilisi ndipo inawona kuti zimabwino. Press Cane idapereka ndalama 895 miliyoni kwacha kuti atsunde anthu omwe adavutika. Kampani iyenera kuti iyambe ntchito.
ny.body_more:
MEPA idaloza ntchito pa 26 January 2026 kuti isiye kusiya ndi kuzunzika kwa dziko. Kampani inapasidwa kuti ifike mitsinje ya madzi osakwanira, ichitisire mphika zobirira madzi, ndikukonzekera nthaka yomwe inanyamutsidwa. Kalata lotsatira kwa Wilfred Kadewa amene akhala ndale wa MEPA ndichifukwa cha 18 May 2026, linasema kuti Press Cane idagwira ntchito yabwino. Kampani iyensu inatengapo ndime imene ingakonstitutsere madzi onama ndikupanga kuti asapambe. Madzi osakwanira asaletse kuchokera pa ntchito ya kupanga mkate wa alcohol.

Kampani iyenera kuti ichite zinthu zina zisanayambe ntchito. Iyenera kuti ikhale ndi ntchito yofika mtsitsi ya madzi osakwanira. Iyenera kuti itsunde munda kuti anthu azitha kumdima pamenepo. Mfumu ya chigawo idanena kuti ali ndani amene akufunikiridwa. Press Cane iyenera kulipu kuti anthu amayang'ane madzi kuti asanu ndikuti alungile.

Mbuymu wa Press Cane, Bryson Mkhomaanthu, ananena kuti kampani ili ndi mwayi. Iye ananena kuti idaphunzira kuchokera pa zinthu zomwe zinachitike. "Tisagwira ntchito yomvetsa kuti nthaka ndi madzi akhalemo bwino," iye anati. Kampani inanena kuti iyenera kusuntha malamulo onse ndikugwirani anthu ndi boma kuti dera lidikale komanso dziko likhalemo bwino.

en.title: Court Pauses Chihana's Suspension in Water Board Fight
en.body: The Supreme Court has stopped a High Court order that removed Yeremiah Chihana from his job at the Blantyre Water Board. The politician was appointed Chief Executive Officer on April 8, 2026. The board suspended him on April 26 after he made decisions without asking them first. A judge said Chihana went to the wrong court. The Supreme Court is now allowing him to stay in the job while the legal case continues.
en.body_more:
The High Court judge, Allan Muhome, ruled that Chihana's dispute is about his work contract. He said the Industrial Relations Court, not the High Court, should handle this case. Muhome wrote that the law made special courts for work problems. The High Court should not deal with these cases at first time, the judge said.

Chihana's lawyer, Michael Goba Chipeta, said they will take the case to the Supreme Court of Appeal. They want to change the High Court's decision. The board says Chihana did many things wrong. He told people the water board would give free water to sick people without asking the board first. He also said water kiosk operators did not need to pay old debts. The board says he had no right to say these things. Chihana also let someone who was not allowed to use his official car.

The case has caused big problems at the water board. The board is supposed to help many people in Malawi get clean water. But now the leaders are fighting in court instead of working. Chihana was told he could work until April 2029. The Supreme Court's choice means this fight will go on for more time.

ny.title: Mahkhamaka Yapamwamba Njira ya Chihana ku Blantyre Water Board
ny.body: Mahkhamaka Yapamwamba ya Malawi inapitsa mlangu wa Yeremiah Chihana wopeza ntchito ya Chief Executive Officer ku Blantyre Water Board. Chihana anatenga ntchito yoyi pa April 8, 2026. Bwalo linamusiyitsa ntchito pa April 26 chifukwa anagwira ntchito popanda kulimbikira. Jaji anati Chihana anathawa mahkhamaka yolakwika. Mahkhamaka Yapamwamba itawewuza njirayo ndipo Chihana akadali mu ntchito yawo.
ny.body_more:
Jaji Allan Muhome anati nkhani iyi ndi yantchito, osati yanduna ya sekelita. Anafuna kuti Industrial Relations Court ikhale ndi nkhani. Muhome anati mayikidwe a Malawi akamapanga ma coute apadera a ntchito. High Court siyiyenera kugwira ntchito yoterayo, anati jaji.

Mlangizi wa Chihana, Michael Goba Chipeta, anati adzathawira ka Supreme Court of Appeal. Akufuna kuti jaji wayamba asintchule mlanduwu. Bwalo likuti Chihana anagwira ntchito zambiri zolakwika. Anakambira anthu kuti Blantyre Water Board ipereka madzi omasuka popanda kulimbikira bwalo lonselo. Anakambira opanga ma kiosk akuti asapeleke chuma chakale. Bwalo likuti Chihana sanali ndi ufulu wogwira zinthu zizofuna. Anapatsira munthu womwe sanali ndi chilolezo kugwira galimoto yake ya ofesi.

Nkhani iyi yayambitsa zovuta zowala pa Blantyre Water Board. Bwalo lili ndi ntchito yobisira anthu ambiri a Malawi madzi abwino. Koma panopa atsogoleri akufika mahkhamaka mopanda kugwira ntchito. Chihana anapatsidwa zonse mpaka April 2029. Mlanduwu umachitika kwa nthawi yochedwa.

en.title: New Malawi Firm Makes 50 Tonnes of Fertiliser Per Hour
en.body: A new company called Wait Holdings has started to make fertiliser in Blantyre. The plant makes 50 metric tonnes per hour. It is called MlimiFert. The company opened the plant in January 2026 at a place called Lunzu. The boss is Irene Mlundira. She said the plant can make 30,000 tonnes every month. The plant makes different types of fertiliser for different crops and soils.
en.body_more:
Mlundira said the company was made to stop Malawi from buying so much fertiliser from other countries.
