She said the plant has systems to blend the fertiliser, places to keep it, and ways to check if it is good. The plant also has a lab to test soil. This helps farmers know what fertiliser they need. It can lower waste and help soil stay good for many years.

The company will spend $5 million to make the plant bigger. They will also open new shops in Lilongwe and Mzuzu this year. Malawi needs between 450,000 and 475,000 tonnes of fertiliser every year. But the government has had trouble buying it from other lands because of money problems. The plant makes fertiliser for maize, tobacco, tea, beans, fruits and other crops.

One expert, Tamani Nkhono-Mvula, said this is good for Malawi. He said Malawi buys fertiliser from Russia and the Middle East, which is far away. This can cause big problems. Herbert Chagona, who leads a farm group, also said this is good. He said the plant shows that people want to put money into fertiliser work in Malawi.

The fertiliser made here fits the soil and crops of Malawi. This means farmers can use less and grow more. The plant will serve big farms, small farms, and groups that buy fertiliser. Wait Holdings is a Malawi-owned firm. It was made to help Malawi make its own fertiliser and add more value to farm work.

ny.title: Kampani Yatsopano Imapanga Manure 50 Tonnes Pa Ola
ny.body: Kampani yathu yatsopano yotchedwa Wait Holdings yayambira kupanga manure ku Blantyre. Ikupanga manure 50 metric tonnes pa ola. Manure amatchedwa MlimiFert. Kampaniyo iyambira ntchito mwa Januari 2026 ku Lunzu. Mnanso wake ndi Irene Mlundira. Akuti kampaniyo imapanga manure 30,000 tonnes pa mwezi. Imapanga mitundu yosiyyana ya manure kwa mbewu ndi nthaka zosiyyana.
ny.body_more:
Mlundira anati kampaniyo idapangidwa kuti Malawi isaphe manure ambiri a kunja. Akuti kampaniyo ili ndi zipangizo zopanga manure, malo opeza, ndi njira zomenyera ngati manure ndi yabwino. Kampaniyo imeneyo ili ndi labora loti imaonda nthaka. Izi zimachita kuti amalimi azindikire manure amene akuwafunika. Izi zimatha kuthetsa zida zache kupeza zowerengeka komanso kumteteza nthaka kuti igwire ntchito kwambiri.

Kampaniyo idzasungula ndalama za $5 million kuti ikulitse magalimoto a kupanga manure. Amatukula kubukabu kumalo enjanji mu Lilongwe ndi Mzuzu chaka chamano. Malawi akufuna manure pafupifupi 450,000 mpaka 475,000 tonnes pa chaka. Koma boma lili ndi vuto loti lisaphe manure a kunja chifukwa cha mavuto a ndalama. Kampaniyo ipangira manure a chimanga, tumbako, chai, nyemba, zipopompo ndi mbewu zina.

Mmodzi wa akatswiri, Tamani Nkhono-Mvula, anati zonsezi ndi mabwino kwa Malawi. Anati Malawi limaphe manure kwa Russia ndi Middle East, zomwe zili kutali kwambiri. Izi zimatha kubangira vuto lakubwino. Herbert Chagona, amene ankhala ndi bwino la gulu la amalimi, anena kuti uYesetsano uwu ndi wabwino. Anati kampaniyo yatsimikizira kuti anthu akufuna kumombo ndalama pa za manure mu Malawi.

Manure amene apangidwa pano amayendera nthaka ndi mbewu za Malawi. Izi zikutanthauza kuti amalimi atha kugwira ntchito manure ocheperako ndipo kumera kwambiri. Kampaniyo idzagwidwa ndi amalimi akulu, amalimi ansanga, ndi mapangidwe amene amaphe manure. Wait Holdings ndi kampani ya Malawi. Idapangidwa kuti Malawi ipange manure yake yeniyeni komanso ipongeze nthawi ya izi.

en.title: Police and army raid Msundwe trading centre, arrest 14
en.body: Police and army officers came to Msundwe trading centre early yesterday morning. They arrested 14 people. The officers were heavily armed. Some wore riot gear. They stopped cars at roadblocks. They searched homes and shops. They took ethanol, fake alcohol, gas bottles and other things. Msundwe is about 40 kilometres west of Lilongwe.
en.body_more:
The operation involved many police officers and soldiers. Residents woke up to see armed men in the streets. Some people said this was the biggest security action they have seen in many years. Two residents spoke about what they saw. Malita Kakudzala said she saw many police but did not know what was happening. Chikaiko Damson, a trader, said business went on but people were worried. He heard some people were arrested but was not sure what they did.

The 14 arrested people are 13 men and one woman. Police say they broke laws. They say the people obstructed police work. They had things that may have been stolen. They had gas bottles without permission. They made fake alcohol and fake trademark goods. Police took the goods but did not say how much they were worth. The arrested people are at Lilongwe Police Station now.

Police deputy spokesman Alfred Chimthere said the raid went as planned. He said there was no fighting. He said the area is calm and safe. He said police and the army are working together to make Msundwe safer. Some people on social media asked if this was about former president Lazarus Chakwera or if it was political trouble. Chimthere said he would not talk about this. He said the operation was about crime only.

Msundwe has a long history of crime and unrest. After the 2019 election, there was violence in the area. Jean Sendeza, a former member of parliament, said she saw more security in the area but was still getting details about what happened.

ny.title: Apolisi ndi asitaa anagwiritsa ntchito mphamvu ku Msundwe, anabweretsa 14
ny.body: Apolisi ndi asitaa amodza anabwera ku Msundwe trading centre mmakakamata. Anabweretsa anthu 14. Magwiritsi antchito anali ndi zingwe zambiri. Ena anali ndi chero ndondomeko yamasewera. Anayimila magalimoto panthawi. Anasaka m'ndalama ndi m'malo ogulitsira. Anatenga nthano, mowa wofosweka, botolo lamungu ndi zina. Msundwe ili thukuta 40 pambali kumadzulo kwa Lilongwe.
ny.body_more:
Ntchito imeneyi inakhala ndi apolisi angapo ndi asitaa ambiri. Anthu anakamata pamene magwiritsi antchito anali mu misewu. Ena anati ichi ndi kugwiritsa ntchito mphamvu kwakukulu kwambiri zomwe anamvere m'makaka ake. Anthu awiri anakhulupirira za zomwe anamva. Malita Kakudzala anati anawona apolisi ambiri koma sanamvetsetsa chit chimene chingachitike. Chikaiko Damson, wogulitsa, anati ntchito yigulitsiro idachitika koma anthu anali olimba mtima. Anawona kuti anthu ina anabweretsa koma sasadziwa zomwe anachita.

Anthu 14 omwe anabweretsa ndi amuna 13 ndi mkazi umodzi. Apolisi akunena kuti anakonda malamulo. Anati awa anasumikiza ntchito ya apolisi. Anali ndi zinthu zomwe zingakhale zotengeka molakwika. Anali ndi botolo lamungu popanda chilolezo. Anagwira nthano ndi mowa wofosweka. Apolisi anatenga zinthuzi koma sakunena kuti zili ndalama zingati. Anthu omwe anabweretsa ali ku Lilongwe Police Station pano.

Mdandanda wa apolisi Alfred Chimthere anati ntchito imeneyi inachita bwino. Anati kunali kugona. Anati malo ali pokhapokha. Anati apolisi ndi asitaa akugwiritsa ntchito pamodzi kuti Msundwe ikhale yoteteza. Anthu ena anafunsa kuti mwalinga ndi tembenuza Lazarus Chakwera kapena kukhutsa. Chimthere sanafune kukhulupirira izi. Anati ntchito imeneyi ndi chifukwa cha dolo yokha.

Msundwe ina chikulu chachifukwa cha dolo. Pambuyo pa zisankho za 2019, kunali dolo m'malo ino. Jean Sendeza, yemwe anali pa mpaliyamenti, anati aonero kuti apolisi ndi asitaa ali m'malo koma anali akusonkhana malaŵi okwanira.

en.title: PressCane pays K895m to farmers hit by chemical spill
en.body: PressCane Limited gave K895 million to Chikwawa District Council on Monday. The money will help farmers whose crops died in a chemical spill. The spill came from the company's waste ponds. It happened when crops were almost ready to pick. Many families lost a lot of money.
en.body_more:
The government told PressCane to pay this money. Two groups ordered the action: the Malawi Environmental Protection Authority and the National Water Resources Authority. They checked what went wrong for many months before this.

PressCane's leader, Bryson Mkhomaanthu, said the company is sorry. He said they obeyed the rules now. The company fixed the waste ponds and made them stronger. They added more space at the top of the ponds. They put in wells to check the water. They also closed some old ponds.

Chikwawa's government leader, Frank Mkandawire, got the money for the farmers. He said the wait is over. The spill came at the worst time—when the crops were about to be ready. It hurt many families very much. Now the families can get help and plant again next season.

The spill was one of the worst things that happened to Chikwawa farmers this year. It came when they needed their crops the most. The money will help them buy seeds and tools for next year.

ny.title: PressCane amapereka K895 miliyoni kwa ana a Chikwawa
ny.body: PressCane Limited inapereka K895 miliyoni ku Chikwawa District Council pa Monda. Ndalama iyi iyenera kugawa kwa alimi omwe mbewu yawo inafuna chifukwa cha masamba ang'ono. Masamba enewo anachoka mu nkondo za PressCane. Zinalowa pa nthawi yamene mbewu inali pafupifupi kuyimilika. Mimalire yambiri inakula.
ny.body_more:
Boma linayambitsa PressCane kupeka ndalamazo. Machinda awiri anayimbitsa: Malawi Environmental Protection Authority ndi National Water Resources Authority. Anavutikira kwambiri pomwe zimachitika zosiyanasiyana.

Mvunguti wa PressCane, Bryson Mkhomaanthu, anati adasukulira. Anati iye ndi kampuni yawo yazigwira ntchito moyendetsera malamulo. Ankangani ma nkondo amapopela ndi kuwonjeza mphamvu. Anawonjezeranso malo omwe kudzera kwa madzi. Anayikani ziphimpe zatsopano kuti akambe madzi. Ndi kopanda izo inkondo zachikale zina.

Mkuluakulu wa Chikwawa, Frank Mkandawire, anatengera ndalama za alimi. Anati kuyikira kwatha. Masamba enewo anachoka pa nthawiyi yoyipa—pomwe mbewu inali pafupifupi kuyimilika. Inaswa mimalire yambiri kwambiri. Tsopano alimi amatha kugula chi chingwe ndi zipangizo zing'ono za chaka chachakubwira.

Masamba enewo anali chimodzi mwa zinthu zoyipa kwambiri panyumba za alimi a Chikwawa mwaka uno. Anachoka nthawi yomwe alimi ankafuna ndalama. Ndalama iyi itabweza alimi kuti athe kumimina mbewu atsopano chaka chikubwira.

en.title: Parliament starts new probe into 2024 plane crash
en.body: A government committee has started a new inquiry into a plane crash that killed nine people in June 2024. The crash happened near Mzuzu in central Malawi. It killed former vice-president Saulos Chilima and eight others, including military workers and the former first lady Patricia Shanil Dzimbiri. The committee visited Kamuzu International Airport in Lilongwe on Monday to start its work.
en.body_more:
The committee is led by Walter Nyamilandu Manda. He said the team wants to find new information that earlier inquiries did not find. "There are certain areas that are gray," Nyamilandu said. "There are some inconsistencies and irregularities where things are not making any sense." He said the committee will dig deeper before asking witnesses to speak.

On Monday, committee members met with airport workers at Kamuzu International Airport. They visited the Air Traffic Control Tower where pilots file flight plans before they leave. They also met with people from the civil aviation office and airport companies. These visits help the committee understand how the airport works and what happened before the plane took off on June 10, 2024.

Yesterday, the committee visited the crash site in Nthungwa Forest in Nkhata Bay, where the Dornier aircraft went down. Many senior government workers went to the site, including the Inspector General of Police, the Secretary for Health, and military officials. The committee wants to see exactly where the plane crashed and understand the final moments of the flight.

Nyamilandu said the committee hopes to finish its work by August 2026. The team plans to visit Mzuzu Airport next and then go to Cobbe Barracks in Zomba, where parts of the aircraft are kept. An earlier inquiry by German experts said bad weather and low visibility caused the crash, but many people in Malawi want answers from a local investigation.

ny.title: Kamiti ya Paliyamenti yayamba kuwunika ngozi ya ndege
ny.body: Kamiti ya Paliyamenti yayamba kuwunika kumene ndege inakumana ndi ngozi mchaka cha June 2024. Kuwunika konse kumapeza kuti anthu 9 adamwalira, kuphatikiza ndi Vice President Saulos Chilima ndi Patricia Shanil Dzimbiri. Ndege yopirikira Mzuzu inakumana ndi mavuto m'mwamba wa Nthungwa Forest. Kamiti idayamba kugwira ntchito la kuwunika pa Lilongwe pa Monda.
ny.body_more:
Nyamilandu Walter Manda ndi mtsogoleri wa kamiti. Anati kamiti iyenera kuzindikira zinthu zatsopano zomwe kuwunika kale sikuzipeza. "Pali malo angapo omwe zimakumana ndi mavuto," anati iye. "Pali zinthu zomwe sizingofanane ndi zomwe zinalembedwa kale." Anati kamiti ingafunse mafunso a anthu yemwe akhoza kudziwa mwezi umenewo.

Pa Monda, anthu a kamiti anakumana ndi ogwira ntchito pa ndege ku Lilongwe. Anakamata malo ena pa aepo pankaka kuti azindikire bwino komwe ndege inayambira. Anambuza kwa anthu a bungwe la ndege ndi anthu a ndege a Malawi Defence Force. Zinthu izi zaphindiritsa kuti kamiti izindikire bwino komwe ndege inayambira pa June 10, 2024.

Msonda uno, kamiti inakamata malo enena komwe ndege inaswa m'Nthungwa Forest mu Nkhata Bay. Anthu ambiri a government anakumana ndi kamiti, kuphatikiza ndi Polisi Mfumu ndi anthu a Army. Kamiti akufuna kuona bwino komwe ndege inaswa ndi kuzindikira zomwe zichitika mwatha wapekolo.

Nyamilandu anati kamiti iyenera kummaliza ntchito ya kuwunika paka Epulo wa 2026. Kamiti ingakumana ndi Ndege Mzuzu airport komanso Cobbe Barracks mu Zomba pomwe mbeta za ndege zilimwe. Kuwunika kale kwa anthu a Germany kunatiika kuti nyengo yoipa ndi kwiya kosiyana kunasinya ndege, koma anthu ambiri a Malawi akufuna mauwu a kuwunika kwa Malawi.

en.title: Escom asks public to stop vandals stealing power equipment
en.body: The Electricity Supply Corporation of Malawi (Escom) has asked the public for help. Between October 2025 and March 2026, vandals damaged 40 transformers and cables. The damage costs Escom about K3 billion each year to fix. A single transformer costs K20 million. Escom says this is a big crisis. The parastatal has made 59 arrests and got 10 convictions so far.
en.body_more:
Escom's chief public relations officer Pilirani Phiri said the problem is very serious. He said that K3 billion is for the broken equipment only. It does not count the money lost when people have no power. Businesses close, machines stop, and hospitals cannot work well. He said this money could help poor areas get electricity instead. Phiri said Escom has started a 30-day plan to tell people about the problem and stop the thieves.

In April 2026 alone, five transformers were damaged. Three were in Lilongwe, one in Blantyre, and one in Chiradzulu. In May, one transformer was damaged in Zomba. Another was damaged on the Blantyre-Chikwawa Road. A security expert said the vandals must be people who work or worked at Escom. They know how the equipment works, he said.

Doctors say the broken transformers hurt hospitals. When there is no power, mothers cannot have babies safely. Babies are at risk. The cold boxes for vaccines break. Blood tests cannot happen. The new Electricity Act says vandals can go to jail for 30 years. There is no fine. Escom says it needs everyone to help—communities, leaders, and business people must protect the transformers. These are public things. They help all people.

ny.title: Escom imamva anthu adaseppa makina a power
ny.body: Escom, kampani yomwe imagwiritsa ntchito ufulu wamawotchi, yapempha anthu kuti amusankhe. Kuchokera pa October 2025 mpaka March 2026, anthu osasamala aseppa makina 40 ndi zina. Kuwopseza kuno kumawerengedwa ku K3 biliyoni pa chaka. Makina amodzi amakalidwa K20 miliyoni. Escom imati ichi ndi vuto lalikulu. Mpaka lero, anthu 59 wanagwidwa, ndi 10 amakonezedwa.
ny.body_more:
Mnumzane Pilirani Phiri akambitsa za Escom anati vuto ili ndi lobwira. Anati K3 biliyoni imakhala inkhani ya makina okhaokha. Sipali mowa yomwe amabadwa kusamala anthu asataonse ufulu. Misika imatseka, makina amasiya ntchito, ndi zibwalo za zaumoyo sizingagwire bwino. Mowa iyi ingagwire anthu osalamulira kukhala ndi ufulu m'malo mwa kuseppa. Phiri anati Escom idayambitsa pulogalamu ya masiku 30 ndi kumawuza anthu ndi kusiya kuseppa kwakwawo.

Mu April 2026, makina 5 anaseppedwa. Atatu anali mu Lilongwe, chimodzi mu Blantyre, ndi chimodzi mu Chiradzulu. Mu May, makina amodzi anaseppedwa mu Zomba. Chimodzi chinaseppedwa pa msewu wa Blantyre ndi Chikwawa. Mnumzane wakuzindikira za chitetezo anati omaseppa awa ayenera kukhala anthu omwe sangugwira ntchito kwa Escom kapena osagwira zaka zina. Iwo amadziwa momwe makina ayagwira ntchito.

Amagwiritsa ntchito zaumoyo anati makina okosedwa amapha anthu. Akabadwa samakuona ufulu, amayi atakubereka amatera vuto. Ana amalephera. Bokisi losunga vaksini siligwira ntchito. Kuzingwa kwa magazi sikubvera. Lamulo latsopano likuti anthu omaseppa angapitse m'jeilo zaka 30. Palibe mowa yina. Escom imapempha anthu onse—migwirizano ya anthu, asing'anga, ndi anthu ambiri—kuteteza makina. Awa ndi cha anthu onse. Amathandiza anthu onse.

en.title: Lake Malawi Water Rises. Homes and Resorts Are Flooded.
en.body: Lake Malawi is getting bigger. Water is now very high. It covers beaches, homes and tourist places. The water level is 476.41 metres above sea level. This is close to the highest ever level in 1980. Rain has fallen a lot for many seasons. Now people are worried. Families must leave their homes.
en.body_more:
In Mangochi, many resorts have water inside the rooms and buildings. The sandy beaches are gone. Some homes cannot be used. Two areas called Ngalamu and Mapila have lost many families. About 30 families live in one school now. They left their homes because of the water. In Karonga, homes, roads and schools near the lake are broken or will break soon. Fishing areas have no fish to catch. In Likoma Island, about 50 homes in three villages are gone under water.

Farmers are also hurt. Along the Shire River in Chikwawa, the water from the lake floods the fields. Crops are under water. Fishers cannot go to the lake. They cannot work. The National Water Resource Authority says this happens because rain came many times. The lake is very big, so water stays in it for a long time. When rain comes again the next year, the water does not go down.

The lake is now at a very high level. Even a small amount of rain will flood more areas. People try to move water out through the Kamuzu Barrage, but they cannot move too much. If they move too much water, it will flood other places downstream. Between 2012 and 2017, the water was much lower. But for five years now, it keeps going up. Visitors, farmers, fishers and families all wait and hope the water stops.

ny.title: Madzi a Nyasa Akuwonjezeka. Nyumba ndi Otansi Zadetso.
ny.body: Madzi a Lake Malawi akulimba. Madzi ali kumtali kwambiri. Nkhata za chibwanzi, nyumba, ndi malo opesa akha pansi pa madzi. Kuchuluka kwa madzi kuli 476.41 meetala. Iti ndi yakufika pafupi ndi kuchuluka kwakukulu kwakale munthawi ya 1980. Mvula yanyamba machaka angapo. Anthu ali ndi nkhawa. Mipango yanyumba iyamba kusunthika.
ny.body_more:
M'Mangochi, malo opesa a anthu okwera ali ndi madzi mkati. Nkhata za chibwanzi zadetso. Nyumba zina sizingagwire ntchito. Madera awiri aitanidwa Ngalamu ndi Mapila sana sadetso. Miyumba ya 30 kumakala ku sukulu yina. Anthu asunthike chifukwa cha madzi. M'Karonga, nyumba, misewu ndi sukulu pafupi ndi nyasa zanyimbidwa kapena zotsalira. Malo okatonka nsomba samagwire ntchito. M'Likoma Island, mizumba ya 50 mmatatu akatanidwa Chiwoko, Jalo ndi Mbamba yadetso.

Akulima akutesana. Kuwala kwa madzi kumatuluka kwa Shire River, kuntchito ya Chikwawa, madzi aitapisa kulima. Mimbale yadetso. Akatonka nsomba samagwira ntchito. Gulu la National Water Resource Authority limati izi zimachitika chifukwa mvula inanyamba machaka angapo. Nyasa ndi yaikulu, choncho madzi akamakala kwambiri. Mvula ikabwera chaka chinachinacho, madzi sakubwera pansi.

Nyasa ili pali kuchuluka kwambiri. Ngakhale mvula yambiri yochepa, madera ena atsadetso. Anthu akuyesesa kumasula madzi kudzera ku baraji, koma samagwera mambala ambiri. Kumasula kwambiri kumadetsa madera ena kumanda kwa Shire. Pakati pa 2012 ndi 2017, madzi anali ochepa. Koma machaka asanu oliya, akuwonjezeka. Opesa, akulima, akatonka ndi anthu onse akudikira kuti madzi akamire.

en.title: Sunbird wins beach soccer title with 4-1 win
en.body: Sunbird Nkopola from Mangochi has won the 2025-26 beach soccer championship. They beat Peninsula Stars of Karonga 4-1 in the final at Arnold Lakeshore Resort in Nkhotakota. The game took place on Saturday and Sunday. Sunbird got a trophy, gold medal, and K9 million in prize money. Isaac Jamu, Sandram Saddie, Martin Biliati and Achraf Saidi scored for Sunbird. Vincent Mwanyongo scored for Peninsula Stars.
en.body_more:
To reach the final, Sunbird beat hosts Vinthenga FC 8-4 in the semi-final. Peninsula Stars won their semi-final on penalties, beating Umodzi 4-3 after a 5-5 draw in normal time. Chintheche Beach Eagles, who were the old champions, lost in the group stage. They had won the title three times in a row before this year.

Prizes went to the top three teams. Peninsula Stars got a silver medal and K5 million. Umodzi of Salima came third and got K2.5 million. The best players also got money. Isaac Jamu of Sunbird was named Player of the Tournament. Mackson Mzembe of Peninsula Stars was Best Goalkeeper. Yusuf Issa of Umodzi and Isaac Jamu were joint top scorers with nine goals each. Each of these players got K50,000.

FAM Vice President Christopher Madalitso Kuyera said the tournament was very competitive and well run. He said he will ask FAM President Fleetwood Haiya for more money for the next tournament. Beach Soccer Chairperson False Ngwira said beach soccer is growing in Malawi. The group wants to bring the sport to more districts, like Likoma Island. Willy Kumilambe, coach of the national team, said the tournament helped him find new players for the Beach Soccer Africa Cup of Nations qualifiers in September.

ny.title: Sunbird yapambana mu mpira wa beach soccer
ny.body: Sunbird Nkopola kuchokera ku Mangochi yapambana gawo la 2025-26 Beach Soccer Championship. Iwo anawonela Peninsula Stars a Karonga 4-1 mu final pa Arnold Lakeshore Resort ku Nkhotakota. Gawo linapangidwa Lasabata ndi Latanzi. Sunbird anapokela kazinisi, golide medal, ndi K9 miliyoni. Isaac Jamu, Sandram Saddie, Martin Biliati ndi Achraf Saidi anagulitsa ma bolo a Sunbird. Vincent Mwanyongo anagulitsa bolo limodzi la Peninsula Stars.
ny.body_more:
Kuti Sunbird atenge khumi mu final, iwo anawonela amizimu ya Vinthenga FC 8-4 mu semifinal. Peninsula Stars anawonela Umodzi 4-3 mu penalties chapakati cha monda yopanda score, 5-5. Chintheche Beach Eagles, omwe anali akacho kale, anachoka mu gulu. Iwo anali akugwina ichi katatu mobwerezabwereza kale.

Mphungu inakala kwa magulu atatu abwino kwambiri. Peninsula Stars anapokela silver medal ndi K5 miliyoni. Umodzi a Salima anachoka lachitatu ndi K2.5 miliyoni. Anzakusewera abwino anapenanso ndalama. Isaac Jamu a Sunbird ananidwa kuti ndiye Wosewera Wabwino. Mackson Mzembe a Peninsula Stars anali Golekeeper Wabwino. Yusuf Issa a Umodzi ndi Isaac Jamu anacheza molimba mofanana, mongosewera magolo khumi ndi limodzi. Aliyense anapokela K50,000.

Christopher Madalitso Kuyera, Vice President wa FAM, anati sewereni sizinalungama kwambiri komanso zinalungamidwa bwino. Anati adzafunsa FAM President Fleetwood Haiya kuti azipe ndalama zambiri pa sewereni lotsatira. False Ngwira, Purezidenti wa Beach Soccer, anati mpira wa beach soccer ukuwonjezeka muMalawi. Iwo akufuna kulowa m'magulu ena, monga Likoma. Willy Kumilambe, wosunga gulu lachimaika, anati sewereni linathandiza kupeza osewera atsopano kwa Africa Cup of Nations qualifiers mu September.

en.title: Over 2,400 Malawians flee South Africa over attacks
en.body: More than 2,400 Malawians have come back home from South Africa in just a few weeks. They are leaving because people are attacking foreign workers there. At two border posts, officials counted 1,672 people at Dedza and 777 at Mwanza. This is much more than normal. The attacks are making people very afraid.
en.body_more:
William Kalanje, a person from the immigration office, said the numbers are not normal. "In December we see many people come home for the holidays. But now we see much more," he said. Sophie Chibenthu, another immigration worker, said Mwanza gets about 400 people each week in normal times. Now it gets more. Seven buses come each week with about 490 people sent back from South Africa.

Malawians went to South Africa to find work and make money. But now they are coming back because they are afraid. They face attacks and bad treatment there. One group working for peace in Malawi said the people who go home are doing the right thing. "Safety is more important than money," the group said. An expert on money and jobs said Malawi has big problems. "People have no money and no jobs here. The government must make new jobs if we want to stop people from leaving," he said.

Violence against foreign people in South Africa has been going on for many years. A group that watches these attacks said more than 660 people have died since 1994. More than 125,000 people have lost their homes. A movement called Operation Dudula says foreign workers take South African jobs. South Africa's leader, Cyril Ramaphosa, said he is against these attacks. But the violence keeps going. The Malawi government said it will help about 200 people who want to come back home from South Africa.

ny.title: Anthu a Malawi akubwera ku nyumba chifukwa cha njala ndi mantha
ny.body: Anthu a Malawi angapo, kuposa 2,400, akubwera ku nyumba kuchokera ku South Africa m'sabata zochepa. Akubwera chifukwa anthu akunyanganya amalawi amene akula kumenya ndi njala. Kumakhala kwa mipata mikubwa awiri, 1,672 anthu anabwera ku Dedza ndi 777 ku Mwanza. Izi ndi ziphinja kwambiri kuposa nthawi zonse. Anthu ali ndi mantha kwambiri.
ny.body_more:
William Kalanje, wamene akugwira ntchito ku immigration office, anati chizimenecho si chanthawi zonse. "Mu December anthu ambiri amabwera ku nyumba chifukwa ndi holiday. Koma tsopano anthu ambiri kwambiri," anati. Sophie Chibenthu, womwe akugwira ntchito naye, anati Mwanza amapeza anthu kuzungulira 400 nthawi zonse. Tsopano amapeza zambiri. Mabasi asanu ndi awiri amabwera sabata iliyonse ndi anthu okwana 490 omwe anatumizidwa kutumbuka kuchokera ku South Africa.

Anthu a Malawi anabwera ku South Africa chifukwa akufuna ntchito ndi ndalama. Koma tsopano akubwera ku nyumba chifukwa akamva mantha kwambiri. Akunyanganywa ndi kunyalanyalwa komwe. Bungwe limodzi lomwe lakuphunzitsira kapena kudzimatitsa njala anati anthu amene akubwera ku nyumba ndi anzawo aluso. "Kusungidwa kwa moyo ndi kofunikira kuposa ndalama," anati bungwe. Munthu wamene akudziwa za ndalama anati Malawi ali ndi vuto lakukulu. "Anthu alibe ndalama ndi palibe ntchito pano. Boma liyenera kupanga ntchito zatsopano ngati tikufuna kulepheretsa anthu kuti asabwere," anati.

Maintchito ovuta a anthu akunja mu South Africa akuchitika kwa zaka zambiri. Gulu limodzi lomwe likuyang'anira izi linanena kuti anthu kuposa 660 afa kuyambira 1994. Anthu angapo, kuposa 125,000, akusiya nyumba zawo. Gulu lotchedwa Operation Dudula likunena kuti anthu akunja akugula ntchito za anthu a South Africa. Wandimitimumuzi wa South Africa, Cyril Ramaphosa, anati akutsutsa izi. Koma njala ikulili. Boma la Malawi linanena kuti liyenera kuthandiza anthu angapo okwana 200 amene akufuna kubwera ku nyumba.

en.title: New team begins work on plane crash that killed vice-president
en.body: A group from Parliament started work today on a plane crash that killed former Vice-President Saulos Chilima and eight other people. The crash happened on June 10, 2024, near Nthungwa in Chikangawa Forest. A military plane left Kamuzu Airport in Lilongwe that morning. It was going to Mzuzu Airport but crashed. Everyone on the plane died. Now Parliament wants to learn what happened.
en.body_more:
The committee is led by Walter Nyamilandu Manda. Today the team visited Kamuzu Airport to look at records and ask questions. Over the next few days, they will go to the crash site, Mzuzu Airport, and Zomba, where the broken plane is kept. The committee wants to see everything with their own eyes and gather facts.

The nine people who died were Chilima, former first lady Patricia Shanil Dzimbiri, and seven others. Some were soldiers. They were flying to a funeral for a former minister. Two earlier groups already looked at this crash. They said bad weather and the plane's use caused the crash. But many people still had questions. They wanted to know more about what happened.

Parliament gave the new committee 90 days to finish its work. The group has 13 members from different political parties. They opened a hotline so people can send in new information. Experts say this inquiry must be done well and fairly. One person said it is a test of how honest and open the government can be. The committee will also look at how the plane was kept in good condition and how the flight was run.

ny.title: Gulu latsopano limayamba kugwiritsa ntchito pa ndegeu yomwalira
ny.body: Gulu la Paliyamenti liyambitsa ntchito lero paukadali wogalimula zomwe zidachitika pa ndegeu yomwalira. Ndegeu imeneyi inafwa ma muntu 9 pa Juni 10, 2024. Zomwe zinachitika zinali pafupi ndi Nthungwa mu Chikangawa. Ndegeu inatuluka ku Kamuzu Airport ku Lilongwe koma inagwa panjira. Anthu onse amene anali mu ndegeuyo anamwalira.
ny.body_more:
Mlembi wa gulu lino ndi Walter Nyamilandu Manda. Lero, gulu linakwera Kamuzu Airport kuti lidzione zinthu ndi kufusa mafunso. Mu masiku akenako, lidzakwera ku malo amene ndegeu inagwa, ku Mzuzu, ndi ku Zomba. Ku Zomba ndizomwe zala za ndegeu zikusungidwa. Gulu likufuna kumvetsa bwino bwino zomwe zinachitika.

Anthu 9 amene anamwalira anali Chilima, Patricia Shanil Dzimbiri yemwe anali mkazi wa mfumuso wakale, ndi ena 7. Ena mwa awa anali asoldeti. Akadali akupita ku funeral ya mninimu wakale. Pali magulu ena awiri amene amakumbukira zomwe zinachitika. Aja magulu anati nyengo imbi ndi njira yogwiritsa ndegeu zinachitika kutero. Koma anthu ambiri anafunsa mafunso ena. Akunena kuti akufuna kudziwa zambiri.

Paliyamenti inapatsa gululo masiku 90 kuti ligwire ntchito. Gulu lili ndi anthu 13 kuchokera m'mapotipoti osiyanasiyana. Alinenso nambala ya foni kuti anthu atha kutumiza zina zawo zomwe zingathandize. Anthu anena kuti kugwira ntchito kowona mtima ndi kusiyanasinza ndi zofunika. Gulu lidzakuwono bwino momwe ndegeu inakhalidwira ndi momwe ndegeu inayambitsa.

en.title: Malawi marks World Hypertension Day, urges blood pressure tests
en.body: Malawi joined the world on May 17 to mark World Hypertension Day. Health experts warned that many people in Malawi have high blood pressure but do not know it. The theme was "Controlling Hypertension Together". Events were held in villages and towns. Experts said about 32 percent of Malawian adults have high blood pressure. Nearly half of these people do not know they are sick.
en.body_more:
High blood pressure is called the silent killer because it can cause no signs. People can have it for years and feel well. But it can damage the heart, blood vessels and kidneys. It can lead to stroke, heart disease and kidney failure. Health workers said many people only go to the hospital when they feel very sick, but by then it is too late.

Health experts said most causes of high blood pressure can be stopped. They said people can change their habits. Tobacco smoking, alcohol abuse, no physical activity and too much salt in food are the main causes. One man, Siveli Aliyeri, was 53 years old and had very high blood pressure from drinking and smoking. He changed his life. He stopped drinking and smoking. He ate less oily and salty food. His blood pressure became normal again. He said he could not even ride a bicycle for a short way without falling off. Now he is much better.

Health groups are working in villages to find people with high blood pressure early. Community volunteers check blood pressure and teach people. The government's health workers also help. Leaders in the villages support this work. One chief in Lilongwe said high blood pressure is a big problem in his area. Some chiefs have become very ill and had to go to the hospital.

Experts asked all people to check their blood pressure often, even if they feel well. The Ministry of Health said people must go to the doctor early if they have high blood pressure. Groups like the Kidney Foundation and the Stroke Foundation are helping. They said this work must continue and get stronger.

ny.title: Malawi zindikiritsa World Hypertension Day, anthu akomedwa kuyeza mmtundu
ny.body: Malawi inachita chinthu chofanana ndi dziko lonse pa Mei 17 kuti zindikire World Hypertension Day. Akatswiri a zaumoyo adakamatula kuti anthu ambiri ku Malawi ali ndi mmtundu womapiri koma sadziwa. Mutu wa usitikali unali "Controlling Hypertension Together". Zisitikali zidachitika m'midzi ndi m'matauni. Akatswiri adati pafupifupi 32 peresenti ya anthu akulu ku Malawi ali ndi mmtundu womapiri. Kawirikawiri limodzi ndi theka la anthuwa sadziwa kuti ali ndi matenda aja.
ny.body_more:
Mmtundu womapiri amatchedwa "silent killer" chifukwa chilichonse chimakhalanso chopanda zizindikiro. Anthu angakhale ndi matenda aya miyezi yosiyanasiyana ndipo azichisowa kanthu. Koma matenda aja angachotse mtima, magazi ndi ubongo. Angapangitse stroke, heart disease ndi kidney failure. Akatswiri anati anthu ambiri amagenda ku chipatala akamva mavuto akulu, koma nthawi imeneyo ndi kumtangu.

Akatswiri adati zifukwa zochotsa mmtundu womapiri zimakhalabe zingapeweke. Adati anthu angasinthe chikhalidwe chawo. Kutokoza, kunywa mowa mobadwa, kusachita masewera ndi kuphika chakudya chotiza salt ndi ziphukusi zazikulu. Munthu wina, Siveli Aliyeri, anali ndi miaka 53 ndipo anali ndi mmtundu womapiri kwambiri chifukwa cha kunywa mowa ndi kutokoza. Anasinthasintha. Anayima kunywa mowa ndi kutokoza. Anayesa chakudya chopepuka ndi chosaliza salt. Mmtundu wake unabwerera ku malo abwino. Anati akunyamuka chowerengera ndi kutali pang'ono. Tsopano ali bwino.

Mbomba za caumoyo zikugwira ntchito m'midzi kuti zichitapo anthu ali ndi mmtundu womapiri. Anangonatanthauzira amayeza mmtundu ndi kuphunzitsa anthu. Boma ndi anthu ake a caumoyo amathandizanso. Oyendera m'midzi amathandiza. Mfumukazi wina ku Lilongwe anati mmtundu womapiri ndi vuto likulamba mu magawo ake. Akatswiri ena ali ndi matenda akulu ndipo apita ku chipatala.

Akatswiri adamanena kuti aliyense ayeze mmtundu wake nthawi ndi nthawi, ngakhale akumva bwino. Boma linena kuti anthu ayeni kwa dokotala mwachangu ngati ali ndi mmtundu womapiri. Mabomba monga Kidney Foundation ndi Stroke Foundation athandiza. Adati kugwira ntchito kundikinu kuyenera kutsitsimutsimuka ndi kukhala ndi mphamvu.

en.title: Parliament wants housing firm to build schools and houses
en.body: A group of lawmakers wants the government to give work to the Malawi Housing Corporation. The group is called the Public Accounts Committee. They met with the housing firm in Blantyre on Saturday. They want the firm to build schools and teachers' houses. The work will come from a new K5 billion building fund for each area. The committee says this will help the firm and give Malawi good buildings.
en.body_more:
The committee wants at least 10 percent of the K5 billion to go to the housing firm. Committee leader Steve Malondera asked why the firm does not get more work from the government. He said the firm builds cheap, strong, good buildings. "What are they failing to do?" he asked.

The housing firm has big money problems. It owes about K9 billion. It has only K6 billion in things it owns. The firm also has a problem. People who rent from it do not pay on time. Some renters are government offices. This has hurt the firm's money.

The housing firm's leader is Bob Chimkango. He said the firm is like a sleeping giant. He said it is being choked but can still grow. He said the firm does not want money from government. It only wants work. "Once we get the work, we will earn what we need," he said. In the last four years, the firm has lost about K8.1 billion.

The committee will now talk to two government offices. These are the Ministry of Lands, Housing and Urban Development and the Ministry of Local Government and Rural Development. They will talk about how to make this plan real. Parliament just made the building fund much bigger. It went up from K220 million to K5 billion for each area.

ny.title: Msonkhano wamkulu akufuna gulu lomangira nyumba lipeze ntchito
ny.body: Gulu limodzi la amalamulo limachita kuti boma lipereke ntchito kwa gulu lomangira nyumba, limatchedwa Malawi Housing Corporation. Izi zinachitika pokhapokha Blantyre sabata yapitali. Gulu limaopa kuti gulu lomangira nyumba lizigulire masekelelo a ana ndi nyumba za mphunzitsi. Ntchotoli idzatchoka mu ndalama zabilimu zokutanthauzira malo a K5 biliyoni. Gulu likuti izi zidzathandiza gulu ndi kupeza nyumba zabwino.
ny.body_more:
Amalamulo akuti peresenti 10 ya K5 biliyoni iyiyike kwa gulu lomangira nyumba. Mlengi wa gulu, Steve Malondera, adafunsa chifukwa chani gulu limbi ndi silipeza ntchito yogulira boma. Iye anati gulu limbi limamangira nyumba zochepa ndalama koma zomimba. "Ndi chani chimene alibe ichi?" adafunsa.

Gulu lomangira nyumba lili ndi mavuto akudana ndalama. Limalowereza pafupifupi K9 biliyoni. Lili ndi chintu chokhala ndi pafupifupi K6 biliyoni. Kundi lina lidapeza vuto limodzi. Anthu omwe angomala nyumba pati sakupereka ndalama pa nthawi. Ndi anthu a boma angomwe sakupereka. Izi zinathawa pano ku mwini wa pati.

Mlengi wa gulu lomangira nyumba ndi Bob Chimkango. Iye anati gulu ndi ngati chinthu chipulumutso. Anati chiliko chopondereza koma chili namakhala. Anati gulu silipempha ndalama kwa boma. Likufuna ntchito yokha. "Ngati tifuna ntchito, ija ndi ija tizapeza ndalama zomwe tifuna," anati. Za zaka zinayi zapitazi, gulu malipitilize kupeza ndalama palo pafupifupi K8.1 biliyoni.

Gulu la amalamulo lidzakamba ndi misitri iwiri. Imi ndi Ministry of Lands, Housing and Urban Development komanso Ministry of Local Government and Rural Development. Iwo azikulumikizana ndi momwe zonse izi zitha kugwirira ntchito. Boma lachita kuti ndalama zabilimu zikwere. Zatsika kuyambira K220 miliyoni kupita K5 biliyoni kwa chigawo chilichonse.

en.title: Government must show where austerity savings have gone
en.body: People in Malawi are asking the government to say how much money it has saved through austerity measures. The measures started in November 2025. The government says there were "huge savings" by March 31, 2026. But the government has not told the public the exact numbers. Many people are unhappy about the lack of information.
en.body_more:
The austerity measures cut government spending in many ways. The government stopped buying new cars. It stopped paying for travel abroad for workers. It stopped hiring new workers, except in key jobs. Senior workers got less fuel money. But people say they have seen no real help from these savings.

Willy Kambwandira works for the Centre for Social Transparency and Accountability. He said the government must show real numbers. He said people cannot see how the savings have made their lives better. John Kapito, from the Consumers Association of Malawi, sent a letter asking for details. He asked how much money was saved from travel, workshops, and vehicle purchases. He asked where the money has gone. He asked if the money went to pay debts, help poor people, or fix services.

George Chaima is a person who studies government. He said the government must be open. He said people will not trust the government if it does not show them the facts. Williams Banda works for the Treasury. He said the savings came from stopping big purchases, less travel, and fewer new workers. He said the money went to pay down debt, not for new things. He said the measures have helped bring down inflation and interest rates.

The Finance Minister, Joseph Mwanamvekha, said the government will show all the numbers in September. He said they will publish a report on the 2026/27 budget at mid-year. Many people say September is too long to wait. They say this makes them think the government is hiding something.

ny.title: Anthu akufuna kuti boma liwonetse pera loti linachoka
ny.body: Anthu a Malawi akufuna kuti boma liwonetse kuti ndalama zingati zidachoka chifukwa cha njira zodetsera ndalama. Njirazi zinayamba mu November 2025. Boma likuti panali ndalama zochuluka zomwe zinachoka pofika March 31, 2026. Koma boma silindi kuti anthu aone kuchuluka kweniwake. Anthu ambiri akusunguluka chifukwa cha chenicheni.
ny.body_more:
Njira zodetsera ndalama zinakhothatsa ndalama za boma m'njira zingapo. Boma lisiyidza kumani magalimoto. Lisiyidza kulipira anthu amabwino akuzipita ku dziko lina. Lisiyidza kufunafuna anthu atsopano, kupatula ntchito zofunika. Anthu abwino anapeza ndalama yochepa ya petulo. Koma anthu akuti sawona bulungu kuchokera mu ndalama zomwezi.

Willy Kambwandira amagwira ntchito ku Centre for Social Transparency and Accountability. Akuti boma liyenera kuwonetsa nambala zowona. Akuti anthu sawonapo kuti kuwonongeka kwa boma kunawathandiza mabwinomo. John Kapito amgwira ntchito ku Consumers Association of Malawi. Anatumiza kalata akufuna kuti boma linena zambiri. Akufuna kudziwa ndalama zongati zidachoka kuchokera kuzipita, semina, ndi magalimoto. Akufuna kudziwa kuti ndalama zonse zinagwiritsidwe ntchito ngati. Akufuna kudziwa ngati ndalama zinagwiritsidwe ntchito pomalipira ngongole, kuteteza anthu osauka, kapena kukonzekera ntchito.

George Chaima ndi munthu amene amaphunzira momwe boma likugwira ntchito. Akuti boma liyenera kuwonetsa chowona. Akuti anthu sadzikhulupira boma ngati silindi kuwonetsa mfundo zowona. Williams Banda amagwira ntchito ku Treasury. Akuti ndalama zomwezi zinachoka chifukwa cha kusiya kumani, zizipita kochepa, ndi kuchepa kwa anthu atsopano. Akuti ndalama zonse zinagwiritsidwe ntchito kumalipira ngongole, osati kugwiritsa ntchito ku ntchito itsopano. Akuti njirazi zinawondetsa kuti mtengo wa zinthu watsika komanso interest rates zitsika.

Minister wa Finance, Joseph Mwanamvekha, akuti boma liwonetsa nambala zonse mu September. Akuti adzafalitsa lipoti la 2026/27 budget pakati pa chaka. Anthu ambiri akuti September yaikidwe. Akuti izi zimawonetsa kuti boma likonda kucinja zambiri.

en.title: Wanderers face Mitundu in battle to return to winning ways
en.body: Mighty Wanderers play Luanar Mitundu FC today at Dedza Stadium. Wanderers lost 2-1 to Big Bullets last week. This is their first loss of the season. Coach Bob Mpinganjira said the team has prepared well. He said Mitundu is a tricky team and Wanderers must not underrate them. Wanderers are ninth on the table with three points.
en.body_more:
Mitundu are a new team in the top league. They finished second in the lower division last season. They lost 2-0 to Masters FC on Monday, so they also want to bounce back. Coach Oscar Kaunda said his players are not scared of Wanderers, even though Wanderers are the champions. Kaunda said his team will follow their game plan and not panic.

Wanderers have some problems before this match. Gaddie Chirwa cannot play because he got a red card in the last game. Alex Kambilinya is also hurt and may not play. Mitundu have two players who know Wanderers well. Oscar Kaunda coached Wanderers before. Vincent Nyangulu was a striker for Wanderers. But Wanderers have a much stronger team than Mitundu. Wanderers won the title last season with only one loss.

Today Wanderers are ninth with three points from two games. Mitundu are seventh with four points from three games. A loss or a draw for Wanderers is bad news. Masters FC are top of the table, six points ahead of Wanderers. Many other games also play today. Creck Sporting Club play Kamuzu Barracks. Chitipa United face Civil Service United. Ekhaya FC play Moyale Barracks and want their first win of the season.

ny.title: Wanderers akumbukira dziko lodziwira Big Bullets
ny.body: Mighty Wanderers azadza naLuanar Mitundu FC lero pa Dedza Stadium. Wanderers adawedwa 2-1 ndi Big Bullets sabata yapita. Ino ndi kawawa kwawo koyamba mu sezon. Mwini ntchito Bob Mpinganjira anati gulu lake likonzekerera bwino. Anati Mitundu ndi gulu lovuta ndipo alephera kuwapangira.
ny.body_more:
Mitundu ndi gulu atsopano mu ligidwe lapamwamba. Anamala malo achiwiri m'ligidwe laching'ono nyengo yapita. Adawedwa 2-0 ndi Masters FC Lwezonthu choncho nawoyo alowa kupita. Mwini ntchito wa Mitundu Oscar Kaunda anati amaonekawo osachoka ngakhale akukumana ndi ampinyi. Anati odzibalamula ndi kutsatira mapilo ake.

Wanderers ali ndi mavuto musanachoke. Gaddie Chirwa salekeza kugwira ntchito chifukwa anapatsidwa kadi yofiira. Alex Kambilinya sangathe kugwira chifukwa akumva kuwawa. Mitundu ali ndi anthu awiri omwe amadziwa Wanderers bwino. Oscar Kaunda adagwira ntchito ku Wanderers. Vincent Nyangulu ankagwira mpira pa Wanderers. Koma Wanderers ali ndi gulu lagazi kuposa Mitundu. Wanderers anapambana cholinga mu 2025 yokha ndi kuwedwa kumodzi.

Lero Wanderers ali pamilumulu isanu ndi chiwiri ndi mfundo zitatu kuchokera mu masewani awiri. Mitundu ali pamilumulu isanu ndi chisanu ndi mfundo zinayi kuchokera mu masewani atatu. Kuwedwa kapena kuwedza kwa Wanderers sikunagona. Masters FC ali pamwambataunda ndi mfundo zisanu ndi imodzi kupitilira Wanderers. Mamasewani ena ambiri azachita lero. Creck Sporting Club adzakumana ndi Kamuzu Barracks. Chitipa United adzakumana ndi Civil Service United. Ekhaya FC adzakumana ndi Moyale Barracks ndipo akufuna kuwina koyamba mu sezon.

en.title: Kwacha to stay strong, but problems hide below
en.body: The Malawi Kwacha will stay stable in the next few months. Tobacco farmers are selling crops and bringing in foreign money. But experts say this is not the full story. They warn that deep problems in the economy could make the currency weak very soon.
en.body_more:
The Reserve Bank of Malawi keeps the Kwacha steady by using money from tobacco sales. Tobacco is Malawi's biggest export and brings in most foreign cash. But this also means the Kwacha is not truly strong.

Experts say Malawi relies too much on one crop. The country does not make enough other things. There are not enough factories. Foreign money in the bank is low. Prices for things go up between 12% and 15% each year. This makes life hard for families and shops.

Business people need new ideas. They can make tobacco into finished products instead of selling raw crops. Other businesses like energy, food making, and digital work are growing fast. The government is helping small mines and farms with loans. Mobile money and digital banks are helping people in villages who cannot reach real banks.

Regional trade can also help. Malawi is between Zambia, Mozambique, and Tanzania. The African Continental Free Trade Area opens new markets. Business leaders should work with neighboring countries to sell more goods. The government must fix roads, fix power cuts, and make rules simpler. When all groups work together, Malawi can turn these problems into chances to grow.

ny.title: Kwacha ikhala yokhazikika, koma mavuto akuwona pansi
ny.body: Kwacha ya Malawi ikhala yokhazikika m'miyezi yotsatira. Alimi amtebvu akubweretsa ndalama zachigalimoto. Koma akatswiri akuti izi si nzeru yonse. Akugwiritsa ufulu kuti mavuto aakulu m'chilengedwe cha ndalama atha kuwerengeka mofulumira.
ny.body_more:
Bank ya Reserve Bank of Malawi imasunga Kwacha akhazikika pogwiritsa ndalama zomwe mtebvu wamasula. Mtebvu ndi chinthu chachikulu chomwe Malawi chikubweretsa. Koma izi zikutanthauza kuti Kwacha si yotsimikiza kwambiri.

Akatswiri akuti Malawi imakhudzidwa ndi chinthu chimodzi. Dziko sililipangira zinthu zina zambiri. Palibekela osatengira ndalama zakunja. Mitengo ya zinthu ikulowa pakati pa 12% ndi 15% pa chaka. Izi zimapangitsa kuti anthu ndi mashopu apite ndi kupomera.

Asomali azibizinesi akufuna zinthu zaposachedwa. Angapange mtebvu wa kumalizidwa njala mwapo kusela ndi kutsika zoyambira. Bizinesi ina monga mphamvu, kupanga chakudya, ndi kugwira ntchito yaying'ono kukula. Boma likuthandiza mashopu ang'onoang'ono ndi ampongwe ndi ndalama zolipira. Ndalama ya simu ndi mapanga a digitu akuthandiza anthu a m'mipiri yomwe samapeza bank yeniyeni.

Bizinesi yamdera ina ikanathandiza. Malawi ili pakati pa Zambia, Mozambique, ndi Tanzania. Malo achigalimoto a dziko lonse la Africa amalungamitsa masoko aposachedwa. Akuluakulu azibizinesi akufunika kugwira ntchito ndi dziko lina kuti atsenge zinthu zina. Bomo likufunika kuwongolera msewu, kuwongolera magetsi, ndi kuwongolera malamulo. Ngati anthu onse agwira ntchito molumikizana, Malawi ingasintsa mavuto akazi.

en.title: Democracy Group Asks Treasury Why Admarc Has No Maize Money
en.body: A group called Cdedi asked the Treasury to explain why it has not given money to Admarc for buying maize. Parliament passed the budget in April, but Admarc still has no funds. Cdedi said this breaks a promise made by the ruling party. The party said it would make Admarc strong so farmers get fair prices. Without money, farmers sell cheap to private traders instead.
en.body_more:
Cdedi wrote a letter to the Secretary to Treasury, Cliff Chiunda. The group said the delay is bad for farmers and for the country. When Treasury waits to give money, private traders buy maize cheaply and sell it high later. This means farmers lose money and poor people pay too much for food.

The ruling Democratic Progressive Party made promises in its manifesto. It said Admarc would be a strong business that helps farmers. It also said the country would keep 150,000 tonnes of maize in storage at all times. Cdedi said these promises have not happened yet.

Admarc's leader said the budget gave only 60 billion kwacha. But Admarc needs 144 billion kwacha to work well. This means Admarc must borrow 80 billion kwacha and pay 10 billion in interest. An expert called Hastings Sonjo agreed with Cdedi. He said quick funding is needed because slow action hurts food security. He also said importing maize is bad because it uses money the country needs for fuel, medicine and other goods. When farmers do not sell to Admarc, they sell to traders who take too much profit.

Cdedi said last year the country had to buy maize from other countries even though farmers grew it at home. The group called this embarrassing for a farm country. It asked Treasury to release the money now so Admarc can buy from farmers before private traders do.

ny.title: Cdedi Yofunsira Treasury Chifukwa Admarc Wopanda Ndalama
ny.body: Bungwe loyang'anira linthu litha, Cdedi, liyenera kwa Treasury kuti iufunse chifukwa chiyani sinapeze ndalama za kugulidwitsa chimang'ombo cha Admarc. Parliament inathetsedwa bajeti mu Epulo, koma Admarc ilibe ndalama. Cdedi inati iti sikugwirani pangano limene zipangitso zinalakwitsa. Zipangitso zinasangalatsitsa kuti Admarc idzakhala yamphamvu kuti agrikolomu apatsidwe ndalama zoyenera.
ny.body_more:
Cdedi italembeka kalata kwa Treasury Secretary Cliff Chiunda. Bungwelio linasangalatsitsa kuti kuvimbika kumaonongeka amagrikolomu ndi dziko lonse. Pomwe Treasury ikulikira kupatsa ndalama, ogulitsa amagulo chimang'ombo ndi mitengo yochepa ndipo amagugulitsa ndi mitengo yowala. Izi zimatanthauza kuti amagrikolomu amakonda ndalama ndipo anthu osakonda ndiponso amatenga mtengo wochuluka pa chakudya.

Zipangitso zomwe Democratic Progressive Party zinalakwitsa zaneneza zambiri. Zinalakwitsa kuti Admarc idzakala chipangizo chachikulu cholungira amagrikolomu. Zinalakwitsa kuti dziko lidzasungila nawo antalamula apakati pa 150,000 tonnes pa nthawi ina iliyonse. Cdedi inasangalatsitsa kuti zipangitozo sizikugwiridwe ntchito.

Mmuna wakutsogolola Admarc anasangalatsitsa kuti bajeti inathana 60 biliyoni kwacha. Koma Admarc imafunikira 144 biliyoni kwacha kuti igwire ntchito bwino. Izi zikutanthauza kuti Admarc iyenera kubolowa ndalama 80 biliyoni ndi kupereka zina 10 biliyoni pamlingo. Munthu amwake odziwa za ulimi, Hastings Sonjo, anagwirizana ndi Cdedi. Anasangalatsitsa kuti kumwa ndalama mwachangu kumafunika chifukwa kuvimbika kumaononga chakudya cha anthu. Anati ngulubuguluzing'a cha chimang'ombo kumabwera ndi zovuto chifukwa ndalama imafunika pa mafuma atambalale, mankhwala ndi zinthu zina.

Cdedi inasangalatsitsa kuti chaka chikadema dziko linatenga chimang'ombo ku dziko lina ngakhale amagrikolomu analimlimbitsa. Bungwelio linati iti lonowongorera chifukwa dziko litha kulima. Linaseka Treasury kuti ipatse ndalama mwachangu kuti Admarc izigula kwa amagrikolomu asanagulo alipo ogulitsa.

en.title: Dead bank asks for 1 trillion kwacha from Malawi
en.body: A bank that closed in 2005 is now asking Malawi for over 1 trillion kwacha. Finance Bank of Malawi stopped working after the central bank took away its licence. But a court said the central bank did this in the wrong way. Now the bank wants money for lost business and profits. A hearing will happen on May 25, 2026.
en.body_more:
The Finance Bank says it lost 551 million US dollars when the central bank closed it. It also wants 61.7 billion kwacha for other losses and interest. The bank says it could have been worth 352 million US dollars by now if it had stayed open.

The government says it will fight the claim. The Attorney General Frank Mbeta said his office will work with the central bank to say the claim is too big. A lawyer named Benedicto Kondowe said the court must think about what this costs ordinary people. He said the court must check if the bank's numbers are real and fair.

The case started in 2005 when the bank lost its licence. A court gave the bank money back in 2014, but a higher court just said the licence was taken away in the wrong way. The bank closed fully in January 2006. Money expert Mkuzo Kenneth Kuwani made the numbers. He said the bank made good money before it closed.

Many people are worried. A group called NACA said the money could hurt Malawi. People are already poor, food is expensive, and jobs are hard to find. If the court says the bank must get this money, it will make things harder for everyone.

ny.title: Benki yakale imafuna triliyoni ya kwacha kuchokera Malawi
ny.body: Benki yotchedwa Finance Bank of Malawi inafuna kwacha zokwana triliyoni imodzi kuchokera pa government. Benki iyi idamwalika m'2005 pambuyo kuti Reserve Bank idachotsa chilolezo chake. Koma cikokwe court chimenechi linati chochotezo chinachitika molakwika. Benki imafuna ndalama chifukwa chasambala lake. Mkutano wolankhula za ndalama udzachitike pa May 25, 2026.
ny.body_more:
Finance Bank imati inalakwitsa dola za America zokwana ma 551 miliyoni pambuyo kuti chigawo cha chitetezo cha ndalama chinaichotsa. Iwo akufunafunaso kwacha 61.7 biliyoni chifukwa chazinthu zina ndi ma interestu. Benki imaonera kuti iwokha ingakale ndi value ya dola 352 miliyoni ngati ingakhalako ngati bwino lero.

Government imati idzafika ndi mwambo womenyeretsa chichimu. Attorney General Frank Mbeta anati adzimagwira ntchito ndi Reserve Bank kuti alankhule kuti chichimu chachikulu kwambiri. Mlawu yemwe atchedwa Benedicto Kondowe anati cikokwe chinayenera kuganiza za anthu omwe sizili ndi ndalama zambiri. Anati cikokwe chiyenera kuyang'ana ngati ndalama za benki ndi zenizeni komanso pali chilungamo.

Chichimu chino chinayamba m'2005 pambuyo kuti benki ichotedwa licence. Cikokwe cinapatsidwa ndalama kwacha 13 miliyoni m'2014, koma cikokwe chachikulu lino liti licence yachotedwa molakwika. Benki idamwalika kwathunthu m'January 2006. Munthu yemwe amawerengera ndalama yemwe atchedwa Mkuzo Kenneth Kuwani anatentha manambala awa. Anati benki iyi idigwira ntchito bwino lero.

Anthu a Malawi amangoopa. Gulu lina loti NACA linati ndalama izi zitha kukhudzitsa malo ake. Anthu ndi mofooka, chakudya ndichakutali, ndi ntchito ndizosakanika. Ngati cikokwe citie kuti benki ipereke ndalama izi, anthu onse adzapangidwa ndi mavuto.

en.title: Police use tear gas at Kamuzu Day as Chakwera kept away
en.body: Police fired tear gas on Thursday in Lilongwe during Kamuzu Day. Former president Lazarus Chakwera and his supporters wanted to lay flowers at the mausoleum. Police stopped them at roadblocks near the city centre. The government said Chakwera could not come to the main ceremony. The Malawi Congress Party held a separate event instead.
en.body_more:
The government said Ken Kandodo and the Kamuzu family made the choice to keep Chakwera away. But Kandodo would not say anything more about it. A government spokesman, Shadric Namalomba, said the government had nothing to do with it. He said the day was for all people, not for one party. President Peter Mutharika sent his culture minister, Alfred Gangata, to the official event.

When Chakwera's supporters tried to walk to the mausoleum, police blocked them with roadblocks. Hundreds of people pushed forward from the MCP office. Police then shot tear gas into the crowd. People ran away in panic. Chakwera's party said they had asked the museum office for permission. They said they did nothing wrong.

Namalomba said the MCP march was not legal. He said they did not ask the police or the city council. "No clearance means no legality," he said. "The era of impunity is over." But the MCP secretary general, Richard Chimwendo Banda, said the police action was bad for democracy. He said it was wrong on a day to honour the founding president. A political analyst, Ben Chakhame, warned that these rival events show Malawi is becoming more divided. He said holding separate ceremonies on national days harms the country.

ny.title: Polisi agwedeza mpingu pa Kamuzu Day, Chakwera akupikisana
ny.body: Polisi idagwedeza mpingu mwadziulidwe pa Kamuzu Day pa Lilongwe Losonkhano lachiwiri. Chakwera ndi anthu ake amakonda kulowa ku Mausoleum kuti akutisani mwala. Koma polisi idasiya nawo, ndi wotsatira wa MCP anachoka. Boma linanena kuti Chakwera sakanene pa sipilisini yayikulu. Kunali sipilisini yachiwiri ya MCP.
ny.body_more:
Bomo linankati Ken Kandodo ndi banja la Kamuzu ndiamene anasankha kusiya Chakwera. Koma Kandodo sanalankhule zambiri. Mphunzitsi wa Sekreto, Shadric Namalomba, anati bomo lilibe ntchito. Anati dzuwa limeneli ndi la anthu onse, osati pa chipani chimodzi. Purezidenti Mutharika anatumiza Minista wa Chikhalidwe, Alfred Gangata, pa sipilisini yikulu.

Akamwini a Chakwera anafuna kuyenda ku Mausoleum, koma polisi idasiya nawo ndi masinthu anu. Anthu mamapiri anayesekezera kuchoka kwa sekreto ya MCP. Polisi idagwedeza mpingu. Anthu anadzoka. MCP inakuti ina onse mwasimu. Anati sipilisini yayikulu inasunthira.

Namalomba anati ndatulo ya MCP idalakwa. Anati sanalambe polisi kapena sekreto ya longa. "Mwina mulibe lamulo," anati. "Nthawi ya kusankha yanthu yatha." Koma sekreto wa MCP, Richard Chimwendo Banda, anati polisi idachita zabwino. Anati zikuipitsa ufulu wa anthu. Anati zichitike zabwino pa dzuwa la mfundisiro. Aliyense woganiza za boma, Ben Chakhame, ankazindikira kuti sizindisoni zizili. Anati sipilisini ziwiri kutikhala zikusezetsa ufulu wa anthu.

en.title: UK gives Malawi lab machines worth £1.2 million
en.body: The UK government gave the Malawi Bureau of Standards new lab machines. The machines cost £1.2 million. The UK sent them through the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office. TradeMark Africa helped. The machines test food and crops for bad things. Malawi can now test products at home.
en.body_more:
The new machines include ultra-high performance liquid chromatography, and two other machines that check for mycotoxins and pesticide residue. Mycotoxins are poisons found in food crops. Pesticide residue is poison left on plants. Before, Malawi had to send samples abroad for testing. This cost a lot of money and took a long time.

Now businesses in Malawi can test their products fast at home. This helps farmers and food makers sell more goods. They can sell to other countries in the region. Better testing helps companies get better prices in markets like COMESA. Small companies can now get fast tests for their crops and food. They can sell faster and sell more.

The new machines help many types of business. They can test food, medicine, and farm products. This helps keep bad products away from shops and people. Companies that use these new standards can sell to big buyers in other countries. The machines show that Malawi wants to make better products. This helps bring new business to the country.

The director of the Malawi Bureau of Standards said the machines help make services better. Companies can get tests fast and trust the answers. This is part of Malawi's plan to grow and make more things. Good quality control helps everyone in the country.

ny.title: UK imapatsa Malawi injini za zolinga za £1.2 miliyoni
ny.body: Boma la UK linapatsa Ofisi ya Malawi Bureau of Standards injini zatsopano za zolinga. Injini nziyo zimachokela £1.2 miliyoni. UK idikiti izo kudzera mu FCDO. TradeMark Africa inalikathandiza. Injini nziyo zimalinga mafuta ndi chimanga kuti aziwona ngati pali zinthu zoipa. Malawi sadzadikiti mafuta awo kunja kwa bansa.
ny.body_more:
Injini nziyo zonse zimapangitsa kufufuza bwino kuposa m'banje. Zimapeza tizilizali ndi zitsanzo zoyambitsa matenda m'chimanga ndi mafuta. Pamberi pa izi, Malawi inali kukamatsa zitsanzo kunja kwa bansa. Izi zinkachoka ndalama zambiri ndi kutenga nthawi yaifupi.

Chinakweninso anthu ndi makampani amu Malawi amatha kufufuza mafuta awo m'mabweni. Izi zikuthandiza alimi ndi anthu amene amagula mafuta. Amatha kuwetengeza kwambiri m'mayiko a pafupi. Kufufuza kwabwino kumapangitsa mtengo wamafuta kusiyika. Makampani ang'ono amatha kulandira mafufuzo mwachangu. Amatha kuwetengeza ndi kudzipangako bwino.

Injini nziyo zikuthandiza mabizinesi ambiri. Zimafufuzana ndi mafuta, mankhwala, ndi zinthu za ulimi. Zikupirira zinthu zoyipitsa kuzoloweza ndi anthu. Makampani amene amagwiritsa ntchito muyezo wabwino amatha kuwetengeza kwa alime akuluakulu mwanthu. Njeniyi ikuwonetsa kuti Malawi imafuna kubuula zinthu zabwino. Izi zikuthandiza bizinesi yatsopano kuja m'dziko.

Mukulu wa Malawi Bureau of Standards anati injeni nziyo zimapangitsa ntchito yabwino. Makampani amatha kulandira mafufuzo mwachangu ndi kudzikhulupilira. Izi ndikwingongole la Malawi kuzikula ndi kubuula zinthu zambiri. Kufufuza kwabwino kumapangitsa anthu onse atheke m'dziko.

en.title: Eighth person dies in mob violence over false rumours
en.body: An eighth person has died in attacks linked to false stories about disappearing private parts in Chikwawa and Nsanje. The man, a phone charger seller from Tomali, died early on Kamuzu Day after a mob beat him on Sunday. More than 90 people have been arrested. Police have sent 300 officers to stop the violence.
en.body_more:
Police say the rumours are not true. "No one has lost their private parts," said Frank Kumukumu, the police chief for the South East Region. "These claims are false and dangerous." He said police will punish anyone who spreads lies that harm people.

The police have set up a camp in Ngabu, a town in Chikwawa. Officers patrol villages and markets every day. They tell people not to hurt others and to report crimes to police instead. The government sent Ben Phiri and Noel Kayira to visit people and tell them the stories are not real. "We have agreed that this foolishness and thuggery ends today," Phiri said.

About 60 people are now in Blantyre Central Prison. Police say they will face murder charges. Traditional leaders have been told to stop the violence and not help criminals hide. Human rights groups say this is now a serious crisis for people's safety.

This echoes a similar panic in 2017, when false stories about "blood suckers" led to at least nine deaths. Kumukumu warned that more arrests will come as the operation continues to restore peace and order in the two districts.

ny.title: Munthu wachisanu ndi atatu amwalira pa mantha amalawi
ny.body: Munthu wachisanu ndi atatu amwalira m'njira za mantha okhudza mantha opeza male mu Chikwawa ndi Nsanje. Munthu yemwewu anagulitsa magetsi mwambiri komanso amwalira kumawa kwa Kamuzu Day atakumenyedwa ndi ogona. Anthu oposa 90 akonzedwa. Apolisi 300 amaletsa komwe kuti mimaera ikhale yabwino.
ny.body_more:
Apolisi akuti mantha amenewo sichoncho. "Palibe wamene wamwalira male," kunena Frank Kumukumu, mfumu ya apolisi mu South East Region. "Manthiwu ndi abodza ndi aopsa kwambiri." Akuti apolisi adzalanga anthu amene akugawa mantha amaopsa anthu.

Apolisi anapanga camp mu Ngabu, mumuji mu Chikwawa. Apolisi amapitiliza kumanda ndi misika lila lonse. Amauzani anthu kuti asaichitisenso ndi kuti agawire apolisi amene akuchita zoyaluka. Boma anayitana Ben Phiri ndi Noel Kayira kuti apite kuzauzani anthu kuti mantha onsewa sichoncho. "Tifikrika kuti umalawi ndi zosafunira uzabwino," kunena Phiri.

Anthu oposa 60 akukhala mu Blantyre Central Prison. Apolisi akuti azajambula za kupha akatansintha. Mafumu akazikidwa kuti asaichitisenso ndi kusiya anthu oyaluka. Anthu amene amachita kwa umoyo wa anthu akuti izi ndi zovuta zamphindu.

Nthintiyi inakhala mu 2017, ndipo kunena kwa anthu za "opeza magazi" kunasonyeza kupha kwa anthu oposa 9. Kumukumu akati anthu ena atha kokonzedwa pamene apolisi akupitiliza ntchito iliyonse kuti mulungu ubwire ndi mtendere mu midzi iwiri.

en.title: Eight people die in Shire Valley mob attacks over false claims
en.body: Eight people have died in two weeks in Chikwawa and Nsanje districts. Men were killed by groups of people who believed false stories. The stories say men can steal other men's private parts. Police say this is not true. The government sent 300 police officers to stop the killings. Over 90 people have been arrested. One man died on May 14, Kamuzu Day.
en.body_more:
Police Commissioner Frank Kumukumu said detectives are working hard in the communities. He said the police will catch and punish anyone who spreads the false stories. He asked people to stop sending fake news. The police said they will keep safe anyone who gives them information to help stop the mob attacks.

On Wednesday, the Minister of Local Government came to Chikwawa. He said the stories about disappearing private parts are lies and "thuggery." He said beating and killing people is not right. He warned that chiefs who help the violence or hide the people who do it will lose their power. About 60 people arrested are waiting to face murder charges in court.

Groups of people in the area are now helping. Police work with local leaders and faith leaders to teach the community the truth. A human rights group is also helping spread awareness. The same false stories happened in Zambia and came from Mozambique. In 2017, stories about "blood suckers" killed at least nine people in six districts of Malawi. Police say no new attacks have happened since they came to the area.

ny.title: Anthu 8 apha ku Shire Valley akufa chifukwa cha mfano yosowa
ny.body: Anthu 8 apha ku Chikwawa ndi Nsanje akunyamatika m'mphindi ziwiri. Anthu odzakula anagwa chifukwa cha mfano yosamveka. Mfano imeneyi imanena kuti azimayi amatha kupeza zinthu za pa mwindo pa abalume. Nankopolo iyi si yowona. Sekreiti ya Ulamuliro anatumiza apolisi 300 kuima nkhondo izi. Anthu osachoka 90 anapezedwa. Mwina umodzi unafa pa May 14, Kamuzu Day.
ny.body_more:
Mkulu wa apolisi Frank Kumukumu anati adetektive akugwira ntchito moyenera m'mipingo. Anati apolisi adzapeza ndi kutsutsa anthu amene akuwafufuza mfano yosamveka. Anayembekezera anthu kupanikiza kuwafufuzira mabweza amakasinthidwe. Apolisi ananena kuti adzachita chipilisi kuchipatala kwa anthu amene achita mabweza ake.

Lachiwiri, Minista wa Mlanda ndi Chigawo anabwera ku Chikwawa. Anati mfano yoti anthu amatha kupeza zinthu za pa mwindo ndi mabweza okambirana. Anena kuti kupha anthu si chilichonse. Anoti ma chief amene amathandiza nkhondo iyi kapena akuzuza anthu amene akumachita atha kutsitsimuka.

Anthu ochokera m'midzi ikupita pang'ono pang'ono. Apolisi akagwirizana ndi atsogoleri a migwirizano ndi anthu amene amakhulupirira Mulungu. Malo opanga mwambongo ndi awa akuthandiza kutiphunzitsa ife nkositekko. Mabweza awa anabwera m'Zambia komanso ku Mozambique. Mu 2017, mfano yoti anthu akubowa ndi kuphedwa inapha anthu ochokera 6. Apolisi anati palibe nkhondo imipso yosankhala patali panthawiyi.

en.title: ACB Staff Angry Over Unfair Pay Raises
en.body: Workers at the Anti-Corruption Bureau are upset. Senior staff got very big pay rises, but junior staff got small ones. The director got 18.5 percent more. But department heads got 45.9 percent more. Junior staff got only 16 percent. Workers say this is not fair. They feel the top people got too much.
en.body_more:
The ACB made new salary rules. The Director General got 18.5 percent more pay. The Deputy Director General got 33.8 percent more. But department heads in Grade 3 got the biggest raise: 45.9 percent. New deputy director jobs start at about K3.3 million each month.

One worker said the changes made problems for other grades too. "Grade 4 is new, so they made Grade 3 pay higher. This moved chiefs from Grade 4 to Grade 5. This also changed extra things they get," the worker said. Lower staff say their 16 percent raise does not match the cost of living. They work hard but do not get fair pay.

The ACB spokesperson, Jacqueline Ngongonda, did not answer questions about this. A lawyer, Benedicto Kondowe, said the bureau can change pay. But big gaps between workers can cause bad feelings. "Lower staff think the top people got too much. This makes them sad and angry. It makes them not want to work well," he said. Junior staff do important work too, he added.

At the national level, the government gave all civil servants 10 to 20 percent more pay in 2026/27. The ACB raises are much higher. A professor, Chrispine Mphande, said the ACB has not had enough money for a long time. The pay changes may help. But staff trust is now low inside the bureau.

ny.title: ACB Yogwira Nkhawa Chifukwa Cha Malipiro Osagwirizana
ny.body: Antchito a Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) akugwira nkhawa. Antchito a kumapeto anapatsidwa ndalama zambiri koma antchito a pansi anapatsidwa pang'onopang'ono. Mwini ntchito akupeza 18.5 pezenti. Koma antchito akunyumba akupeza 45.9 pezenti. Antchito a pansi anapeza 16 pezenti. Antchito akuti izi sizigwirizana. Iwo akugawana kuti azachikulira amapeza zambiri.
ny.body_more:
ACB inatsintha njira ya malipiro. Mwini ntchito wamkulu anapatsidwa 18.5 pezenti yowonjezera. Mwini ntchito wachiwiri anapatsidwa 33.8 pezenti. Koma antchito akunyumba a Grade 3 anapatsidwa 45.9 pezenti. Izi ndi kuwonjezera kwakukulu. Ntchito zatsopano za deputy director zimayamba ndi malipiro a K3.3 miliyoni pa mwezi.

Wantchito amodzi anati njira iyi inathandiza koma inathandiziranso mavuto. "Grade 4 ndi yatsopano, choncho anatsintha Grade 3 kukhala ndi malipiro okulu. Izi inathandiza chiefs kusuntha kuchoka Grade 4 kupita Grade 5. Izi zinasinthiranso zina zomwe akuwapatsira," anati. Antchito a pansi akuti 16 pezenti sizokwanira. Ndalama zambiri zimakula koma malipiro ake sakhala ochepa.

Mbale imodzi ya ACB, Jacqueline Ngongonda, anakana kuyankha mafunso a ine. Wolamulira, Benedicto Kondowe, anati kuti ACB iyeneya kusintha malipiro. Koma sizigwirizana zazikulu zimapangitsa antchito kukhala ndi chikondi chinari. "Antchito a pansi amakumbukira kuti azachikulira amapeza zambiri. Izi zimapangitsa iwo kukhala ndi chisoni ndi chikondi chinari. Iyi itachitsa iwo kuti sazigwire ntchito bwino," anati. Antchito a pansi ndiogwira ntchito yofunika komanso, iye anati.

M'boma lonse, boma lilipani 10 mpaka 20 pezenti kuantchito onse a serevisi ya boma m'chaka cha 2026/27. ACB yapatsidwa ndalama zambiri kuposa izi. Wodziwa za boma, Chrispine Mphande, anati kuti ACB sinalipani bwino kwa madzi angapo. Izi zingatheke kuthandizira. Koma antchito sazikonda ACB mpakano.

en.title: MCP Plans Separate Prayer Service for Kamuzu Day
en.body: The Malawi Congress Party will hold its own prayers for Kamuzu Day after the government did not invite former president Lazarus Chakwera to the official ceremony on May 14. The MCP said the decision was unfair because Kamuzu Banda founded both the nation and the party. President Peter Mutharika will not attend the state event; instead, a minister will represent him. The MCP asked the government to change the decision but was told no.
en.body_more:
MCP Secretary General Richard Chimwendo Banda told the press that the government said Chakwera could not come because the President had sent a minister instead. He said this created a "protocol problem" that made it hard for the party leader to attend. Chimwendo Banda said Kamuzu Day has deep meaning for both the nation and the MCP. The party will hold prayers at its headquarters in Lilongwe after the official service ends. Chakwera will lay a wreath at Kamuzu's tomb to show respect.

The ceremony will take place at Kamuzu Mausoleum next to Parliament Building in Lilongwe at 10am. Organisers asked people to wear neutral clothes and not wear party colours. Ken Kandodo, who represents the Kamuzu family, said Kamuzu was "a man for all people" and believed in unity. Officials said the day should unite all Malawians, not divide them by politics.

Kamuzu Banda died on November 25, 1997, at age 99. He led Malawi for three decades after independence in 1964 and lost the first free elections in 1994. Activists said the day should help Malawians learn about both the good and bad parts of his rule. Some people feel the day brings back painful memories of arrests and hard times during his time as leader.

ny.title: MCP Adzapitiliza Pemphano Yake Yomwera Kamuzu Day
ny.body: Chipani cha MCP chidzachita pemphano lake chokha pa Kamuzu Day m'mawa wa May 14, chifukwa kuti mfundo zina za government sizinawalira mtsogoleri wamkati Lazarus Chakwera pa chipani. MCP inati sichinthu chabwino chonchi chifukwa Kamuzu Banda anakhazikitsa chipani ndi dziko lonse. President Peter Mutharika sawona malinga ndi chimene zinachitika. Chipani chidayalira government kuti ataganizire, koma government inayankha kuti sithing.
ny.body_more:
Mwonya wa Secretary General wa MCP, Richard Chimwendo Banda, anawuzira phukusi loti government inawuza kuti Chakwera sasakike chifukwa President ana chiwise minister m'malo mwake. Iye anati izi zidapangitsa kuti zikhale zovuta ndi lamulo la mpikisano. Chimwendo Banda anati Kamuzu Day ili ndi ntchito yayikulu kwa chipani ndi kwa dziko lonse. Chipani cha MCP adzachita pemphano pa malo ake a pa Lilongwe pambuyo pa ntchito ya government. Chakwera adzamangu mbale pa khubusi la Kamuzu kuwonetsa lingaliro labwino.

Ntchito iyonse idzachitike pa Kamuzu Mausoleum pafupi ndi Parliament ndi Lilongwe nthawi ya 10am. Anthu amene akusankhani ntchito iyi anafuna kuti anthu athe zovala zakuneutsa, osatha zovala za chipani. Ken Kandodo, yemwe akhala nkhani ya banja la Kamuzu, anati Kamuzu anali "munthu wa anthu onse" ndipo ankafuna kugwirizana. Anthu omwe akusankhani ntchito anati lero lidzachita kuti Malawi atichitire chimodzi, osalumikizana ndi chipani.

Kamuzu Banda adamwalira pa November 25, 1997, ali ndi zaka 99. Iye anagwiritsa ntchito dziko kwa zaka 30 kuyambira nthawi ya ufulu m'1964 ndipo anagonja pazosankha zaulere m'1994. Anthu ena anati lero likhoza kutisundiritsa za mabadwo abwino ndi awo asowothekapo m'nthawi ya kutsogolera kwake. Anthu ena anati lero ili ndi lingaliro la mabala ndi nkhawa zomwe zinachitika panthawiyo.

en.title: Prophet Bushiri gives K300 million to clean Malawi plan
en.body: Prophet Shepherd Bushiri and his wife Mary gave K300 million to Beam Trust this week. Beam Trust works to clean and beautify Malawi. First Lady Gertrude Mutharika received the money at Kamuzu Palace in Lilongwe. Bushiri made a promise to give this money in February. The First Lady said thank you and said the money will help keep public places clean.
en.body_more:
The Beam Trust is working on a plan for 2026 to 2030. The plan focuses on health, sanitation, and schools. Bushiri said he and his wife want to help because they saw the good work Beam does in towns and villages. He said true development needs people and groups to put money into things that help people every day.

Mutharika called on all people in Malawi to help. She said everyone must keep their homes, schools, hospitals, and markets clean. She said this is not only the job of the government. Every person must help to make the country beautiful and clean.

Beam Trust has already adopted many hospitals across Malawi. These include Bwaila District Hospital in Lilongwe, Mzuzu Urban Community Hospital, and hospitals in Balaka, Thyolo, and Rumphi. The trust works to fix broken buildings in hospitals and give them clean water and toilets. Bushiri said his gift will help Beam make towns and villages cleaner and better for all people.

Many people say this is one of the biggest gifts to help Malawi become clean in recent months. Beam Trust works with schools, villages, and companies to plant trees and clean up towns.

ny.title: Msiliza Bushiri amapereka K300 miliyoni kwa Beam
ny.body: Msiliza Shepherd Bushiri ndi mkazi wake Mary amapereka K300 miliyoni kwa Beam Trust lino. Beam Trust ndi bungwe lomwe limapangitsa kuti dziko lonselo likhale lotsimikizika ndi lokhazikika. Madam First Lady Gertrude Mutharika anamwalira ndalama ndi kuufunika ku Kamuzu Palace ku Lilongwe. Bushiri analonjeza kupereka ndalamazi mu February. Madam First Lady anasiyani kuti ndalamazi zitutsutsa ntchito zawo.
ny.body_more:
Beam Trust ili ndi mapulanyo awa kuyambira 2026 mpaka 2030. Mapulanyo amenewa akufuna kusangalatsa nthenda, kusambira, ndi mashule. Bushiri anati iye ndi mkazi wake akufuna kuthandiza chifukwa adaona zimene Beam imachita m'misonkhano ndi m'matauni. Anati kuti kupita pamberi kwa dziko likukhala ndi anthu ndi mabungwe amene amalowa ndi ndowe m'zinthu zomwe zimathanthauza kwa anthu nthawi iliyonse.

Madam First Lady analonjeza kuti anthu onse adzathandize. Anati aliyense ayenera kusambira nyumba yake, sekulayo, chipitala, ndi msika. Anati izi sizinthu za boma okha. Aliyense ayenera kudzasekedwa kusambira dziko lapansi lonse kuti likhalire lokwera ndi lokhazikika.

Beam Trust yaphunzitsa masipitala ambiri kwambiri mu Malawi. Izi zikuphatikiza Bwaila District Hospital ku Lilongwe, Mzuzu Urban Community Hospital, ndi masipitala a Balaka, Thyolo, ndi Rumphi. Bungwe limapangitsa kuti masipitalawi azitsitsidwa ndi kuonongekera, ndi kupatsa maji okwera ndi zindumba zabwino. Bushiri anati mphatso yake idzathandiza Beam kuti m'misonkhano ndi m'matauni mzake likhalire lokhazikika ndi lotsimikizika kwa anthu onse.

Anthu ambiri akuti idi ndi mphatso imodzi yaikulu kwambiri kwa Malawi mwachaka wano kupezeka kuti lisambire. Beam Trust imagwira ntchito ndi masule, m'misonkhano, ndi mabungwe m'zimu zomwe zimapanda mitengo ndi kusambira m'matauni.

en.title: ACB officer interdicted over failed arrest of ex-minister
en.body: The Anti-Corruption Bureau has interdicted senior officer Robert Mkandawire over the failed arrest of former finance minister Simplex Chithyola Banda on May 8. Acting ACB director Gabriel Chembezi wrote that Mkandawire found Chithyola at his home but did not take him. The letter says Mkandawire allowed the suspect to leave. A court later set aside the arrest warrant and Chithyola now faces summons instead of jail.
en.body_more:
Mkandawire said the situation was dangerous. He told people the team found many people at Chithyola's house. They threatened the officers. Some people said the arrest was political. Police only came at 9 PM, he said. Mkandawire said his team's safety came first.
